longExplanationEn: The ～にもかかわらず grammar point is used to express that something occurs, or a situation exists, despite or in spite of a contrasting or unfavorable condition. It can be translated as 'despite', 'in spite of', or 'even though' in English.

examples.ja: 忙しいにもかかわらず、彼は毎日運動しています。
examples.romaji: Isogashii ni mo kakawarazu, kare wa mainichi undou shiteimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Despite being busy, he exercises every day.

examples.ja: 雨が降っているにもかかわらず、彼女は散歩に行きました。
examples.romaji: Ame ga futteiru ni mo kakawarazu, kanojo wa sanpo ni ikimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Even though it was raining, she went for a walk.

examples.ja: 車が古いにもかかわらず、まだよく走ります。
examples.romaji: Kuruma ga furui ni mo kakawarazu, mada yoku hashirimasu.
examples.meaningEn: In spite of the car being old, it still runs well.

examples.ja: 結果が良くないにもかかわらず、彼は頑張り続けました。
examples.romaji: Kekka ga yoku nai ni mo kakawarazu, kare wa ganbari tsuzukemashita.
examples.meaningEn: Despite the results not being good, he continued to do his best.

title: ～により (〜ni yori)
formation: Noun + により, Verb + により
shortExplanationEn: Indicates the reason, method, or means by which something happens; 'by', 'through', 'due to'.
longExplanationEn: The ～により grammar point is used to indicate the reason, method, or means by which something happens or is done. It can be often translated as 'by', 'through', or 'due to' in English. It can be attached directly to nouns or verbs in plain form.

examples.ja: この事故は運転手の不注意により起こりました。
examples.romaji: Kono jiko wa untenshu no fuchuui ni yori okorimashita.
examples.meaningEn: This accident occurred due to the driver's carelessness.

examples.ja: その映画は小説により制作されました。
examples.romaji: Sono eiga wa shousetsu ni yori seisaku saremashita.
examples.meaningEn: The movie was produced based on the novel.

examples.ja: 犯人は警察により逮捕されました。
examples.romaji: Hannin wa keisatsu ni yori taiho saremashita.
examples.meaningEn: The criminal was arrested by the police.

examples.ja: 会社の成長は従業員の努力により実現しました。
examples.romaji: Kaisha no seichou wa juugyouin no doryoku ni yori jitsugen shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: The company's growth was achieved through the efforts of its employees.

title: ～にわたって (〜ni watatte)
formation: Noun + にわたって
shortExplanationEn: Indicates a range or extent; 'over', 'throughout', 'across'.
longExplanationEn: The ～にわたって grammar point is used to express a range or extent of a particular action, event, or situation. It can be translated as 'over', 'throughout', or 'across' in English. This structure can be used with various types of words such as time, distance, and quantity.

examples.ja: 彼女は一年にわたって日本語を勉強しました。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa ichinen ni watatte nihongo wo benkyou shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: She studied Japanese for over a year.

examples.ja: このプロジェクトは50キロにわたって道路を建設します。
examples.romaji: Kono purojekuto wa gojukiro ni watatte douro wo kensetsu shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: This project will construct roads across 50 kilometers.

examples.ja: 彼の著作は数多くの国にわたって読まれている。
examples.romaji: Kare no chosaku wa kazu ooku no kuni ni watatte yomarete iru.
examples.meaningEn: His writings are read throughout numerous countries.

examples.ja: その秘密は世代にわたって保たれ続けた。
examples.romaji: Sono himitsu wa sedai ni watatte tamotsu sareta.
examples.meaningEn: The secret was kept over generations.

title: ～に先立ち (〜ni sakidachi)
formation: Noun + に先立ち / Verb-dictionary form + に先立って
shortExplanationEn: Express an action or event that occurs before another; 'before', 'prior to', 'preceding'.
longExplanationEn: The ～に先立ち grammar point is used to indicate that a certain action or event occurs before another action or event. It can be translated as 'before', 'prior to', or 'preceding' in English. It is often used with nouns (Noun + に先立ち) or with dictionary-form verbs (Verb-dictionary form + に先立って).

examples.ja: 会議に先立ち、資料を確認しましょう。
examples.romaji: Kaigi ni sakidachi, shiryou wo kakunin shimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Before the meeting, let's check the materials.

examples.ja: 出発に先立ち、荷物を整理しました。
examples.romaji: Shuppatsu ni sakidachi, nimotsu wo seiri shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Before departure, I organized my luggage.

examples.ja: 就寝に先立ち、歯を磨きます。
examples.romaji: Shuushin ni sakidachi, ha wo migakimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Before going to bed, I brush my teeth.

examples.ja: 映画の上映に先立ち、予告編が放映されます。
examples.romaji: Eiga no jouei ni sakidachi, yokokuhen ga houei saremasu.
examples.meaningEn: Before the movie screening, the trailers are shown.

title: ～に反して (〜ni hanshite)
formation: Noun + に反して
shortExplanationEn: Contrary to, against, in spite of
longExplanationEn: The ～に反して grammar point is used to express that something is contrary to or against an expectation or belief. It can be translated as 'contrary to', 'against', or 'in spite of' in English. It is generally used with nouns.

examples.ja: 天気予報に反して、今日は雨が降っている。
examples.romaji: Tenki yohou ni hanshite, kyou wa ame ga futte iru.
examples.meaningEn: Contrary to the weather forecast, it is raining today.

examples.ja: 彼の予想に反して、試合に勝った。
examples.romaji: Kare no yosou ni hanshite, shiai ni katta.
examples.meaningEn: We won the match against his prediction.

examples.ja: 両親の意見に反して、彼と結婚した。
examples.romaji: Ryoushin no iken ni hanshite, kare to kekkon shita.
examples.meaningEn: I married him against my parents' opinion.

examples.ja: この映画は批評家の評価に反して大ヒットした。
examples.romaji: Kono eiga wa hihyouka no hyouka ni hanshite daihitto shita.
examples.meaningEn: This movie became a big hit contrary to the critics' ratings.

title: ～に基づいて (〜ni motozuite)
formation: Noun + に基づいて
shortExplanationEn: Based on, according to, depending on
longExplanationEn: The ～に基づいて grammar point is used to express that something is based on, according to, or depending on a certain source or foundation. It can be used with nouns that represent a source, rule, principle, or standard.

examples.ja: 研究結果に基づいて、新しい治療法が開発されました。
examples.romaji: Kenkyuu kekka ni motozuite, atarashii chiryouhou ga kaihatsu saremashita.
examples.meaningEn: A new treatment method was developed based on the research results.

examples.ja: 法律に基づいて、犯罪者は逮捕されました。
examples.romaji: Houritsu ni motozuite, hanzaisha wa taiho saremashita.
examples.meaningEn: The criminal was arrested according to the law.

examples.ja: そのデータに基づいて、結論を出すことができます。
examples.romaji: Sono deeta ni motozuite, ketsuron o dasu koto ga dekimasu.
examples.meaningEn: We can draw conclusions based on that data.

examples.ja: お客様の要望に基づいて、商品の改善が行われます。
examples.romaji: Okyakusama no youbou ni motozuite, shouhin no kaizen ga okonawaremasu.
examples.meaningEn: Product improvements will be made based on customer requests.

title: ～に対して (〜ni taishite)
formation: Noun + に対して
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'toward', 'against', 'in contrast to', or 'in response to'.
longExplanationEn: The ～に対して grammar point is used to indicate a direction or target, showing an action or attitude towards something or someone. It can also be used to show a contrast between two situations or to express 'in response to' an action or situation.

examples.ja: 日本の文化に対して尊敬の念を持っています。
examples.romaji: Nihon no bunka ni taishite sonkei no nen wo motte imasu.
examples.meaningEn: I have a sense of respect towards Japanese culture.

examples.ja: 彼に対して無礼な態度を取らないでください。
examples.romaji: Kare ni taishite burei na taido wo toranaide kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Please don't have a rude attitude towards him.

examples.ja: 先生の質問に対して、彼女はすぐに答えました。
examples.romaji: Sensei no shitsumon ni taishite, kanojo wa sugu ni kotaemashita.
examples.meaningEn: In response to the teacher's question, she answered immediately.

examples.ja: 男性に対して、女性は同じ仕事でも低い給料をもらっている。
examples.romaji: Dansei ni taishite, josei wa onaji shigoto demo hikui kyuuryou wo moratte iru.
examples.meaningEn: In contrast to men, women receive lower salaries for the same job.

title: ～に応えて (〜ni kotaete)
formation: Noun + に応えて
shortExplanationEn: Responding to; living up to; meeting.
longExplanationEn: The ～に応えて grammar point is used to express an action in response to something (an expectation, request, or situation). It can be translated as 'responding to', 'living up to', or 'meeting' in English. This grammar point is generally used with nouns or noun phrases.

examples.ja: お客様の要望に応えて、新しいメニューを作りました。
examples.romaji: Okyaku-sama no youbou ni kotaete, atarashii menyuu o tsukurimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Responding to the customers' requests, we created a new menu.

examples.ja: 彼はチームの期待に応えて素晴らしいプレーを見せた。
examples.romaji: Kare wa chiimu no kitai ni kotaete, subarashii puree o miseta.
examples.meaningEn: He lived up to the team's expectations and showed a great performance.

examples.ja: 政府は国民の声に応えて、新しい法律を制定した。
examples.romaji: Seifu wa kokumin no koe ni kotaete, atarashii houritsu o seitei shita.
examples.meaningEn: The government enacted a new law in response to the voice of the people.

examples.ja: 企業は環境問題に応えて、環境に優しい製品を開発している。
examples.romaji: Kigyou wa kankyou mondai ni kotaete, kankyou ni yasashii seihin o kaihatsu shite iru.
examples.meaningEn: Companies are developing environmentally friendly products in response to environmental issues.

title: ～に応じて (〜ni oujite)
formation: Noun + に応じて
shortExplanationEn: In accordance with, depending on, in response to.
longExplanationEn: The ～に応じて grammar point is used to express something that varies according to a certain condition or responds to a change. It can be translated as 'in accordance with', 'depending on', or 'in response to' in English. The formation is used with nouns.

examples.ja: 仕事の量に応じて給料が変わります。
examples.romaji: Shigoto no ryou ni oujite kyuuryou ga kawarimasu.
examples.meaningEn: The salary changes depending on the amount of work.

examples.ja: 季節に応じて服装を選びましょう。
examples.romaji: Kisetsu ni oujite fukusou wo erabimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Choose your clothes according to the season.

examples.ja: お客様の希望に応じてプランを提案します。
examples.romaji: Okyakusama no kibou ni oujite puran wo teian shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: We propose plans according to the customers' wishes.

examples.ja: 年齢に応じて健康診断項目が異なります。
examples.romaji: Nenrei ni oujite kenkou shindan koumoku ga kotonarimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Health check items vary depending on age.

title: ～に決まっている (〜ni kimatte iru)
formation: Verb-casual + に決まっている, い-Adjective + に決まっている, な-Adjective + だに決まっている, Noun + だに決まっている
shortExplanationEn: Express certainty; 'it is obvious', 'it must be', 'no doubt'.
longExplanationEn: The ～に決まっている grammar point is used to express certainty or a strong conviction about a situation or event. It can be translated in English as 'it is obvious', 'it must be', or 'no doubt'. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

examples.ja: こんなに暑い日に運動したら、汗をかくに決まっている。
examples.romaji: Konna ni atsui hi ni undou shitara, ase wo kaku ni kimatte iru.
examples.meaningEn: It's obvious that you'll sweat if you exercise on such a hot day.

examples.ja: 彼が試験に落ちたら、がっかりするに決まっている。
examples.romaji: Kare ga shiken ni ochitara, gakkari suru ni kimatte iru.
examples.meaningEn: If he fails the exam, it's obvious he will be disappointed.

examples.ja: 彼女はいつも遅れるから、今日も遅れるに決まっている。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa itsumo okureru kara, kyou mo okureru ni kimatte iru.
examples.meaningEn: She's always late, so it's obvious she'll be late today too.

examples.ja: このゲームは楽しいに決まっている。
examples.romaji: Kono geemu wa tanoshii ni kimatte iru.
examples.meaningEn: It's obvious that this game is fun.

title: ～に沿って (〜ni sotte)
formation: Noun + に沿って
shortExplanationEn: Along with; in accordance with; following
longExplanationEn: The ～に沿って grammar point is used to express that something happens or is done along with, in accordance with, or following a particular line, path, or guideline. It is often used in instructions or explanations. The に沿って is attached to the noun representing the line, path or guideline.

examples.ja: 川に沿って歩いていくと、橋が見えてきます。
examples.romaji: Kawa ni sotte aruite iku to, hashi ga miete kimasu.
examples.meaningEn: If you walk along the river, you will see a bridge.

examples.ja: このレシピに沿って料理を作れば、間違いありません。
examples.romaji: Kono reshipi ni sotte ryouri wo tsukureba, machigai arimasen.
examples.meaningEn: If you cook according to this recipe, there will be no mistakes.

examples.ja: 規則に沿って行動しなければなりません。
examples.romaji: Kisoku ni sotte koudou shinakereba narimasen.
examples.meaningEn: We must act in accordance with the rules.

examples.ja: 山道に沿って進むと、小さな神社があります。
examples.romaji: Yamamichi ni sotte susumu to, chiisana jinja ga arimasu.
examples.meaningEn: If you follow the mountain path, there is a small shrine.

title: ～に過ぎない (〜ni suginai)
formation: Verb-casual + に過ぎない, い-Adjective + に過ぎない, な-Adjective + に過ぎない, Noun + に過ぎない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses that something is 'just' or 'nothing more than'; 'merely', 'only', 'simply'.
longExplanationEn: The ～に過ぎない grammar point is used to indicate that something is 'just' or 'nothing more than' the stated quality or condition. It implies that the thing is not as significant or important as it might seem at first glance. This grammar point can be translated as 'merely', 'only', or 'simply' in English. It can be used with verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives, and nouns.

examples.ja: 彼の失敗はミスに過ぎない。
examples.romaji: Kare no shippai wa misu ni suginai.
examples.meaningEn: His failure is nothing more than a mistake.

examples.ja: この試験は簡単な問題に過ぎない。
examples.romaji: Kono shiken wa kantan na mondai ni suginai.
examples.meaningEn: This test is nothing more than a simple question.

examples.ja: 彼女は友達に過ぎない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa tomodachi ni suginai.
examples.meaningEn: She is nothing more than a friend.

examples.ja: その発言は冗談に過ぎない。
examples.romaji: Sono hatsugen wa joudan ni suginai.
examples.meaningEn: That remark is nothing more than a joke.

title: ～に関わって (〜ni kakawatte)
formation: Verb-casual + に関わって, い-Adjective + に関わって, な-Adjective + に関わって, Noun + に関わって
shortExplanationEn: Indicates that something is related to or influenced by a certain matter or factor; 'concerning', 'in connection with', 'regarding'.
longExplanationEn: The ～に関わって grammar point is used to express that something is related to, influenced by, or connected with a certain matter or factor. It can be translated as 'concerning', 'in connection with', or 'regarding' in English. The formation depends on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

examples.ja: 試験の内容に関わって、先生に質問があります。
examples.romaji: Shiken no naiyou ni kakawatte, sensei ni shitsumon ga arimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I have a question for the teacher regarding the content of the exam.

examples.ja: このプロジェクトに関わって、意見があれば、遠慮なく言ってください。
examples.romaji: Kono purojekuto ni kakawatte, iken ga areba, enryo naku itte kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: If you have any opinions regarding this project, please feel free to express them.

examples.ja: 健康に関わって、食生活はとても重要です。
examples.romaji: Kenkou ni kakawatte, shokuseikatsu wa totemo juuyou desu.
examples.meaningEn: Regarding health, dietary habits are very important.

examples.ja: その事件に関わって、彼も少し疑われています。
examples.romaji: Sono jiken ni kakawatte, kare mo sukoshi utagawarete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: He is also somewhat suspected in connection with the incident.

title: ～に限り (〜ni kagiri)
formation: Noun + に限り
shortExplanationEn: Expresses limitation; 'only', 'limited to', 'exclusive to'.
longExplanationEn: The ～に限り grammar point is used to indicate that something is limited or exclusive to a certain condition or situation. It can be translated as 'only', 'limited to', or 'exclusive to' in English. The formation is simple and can be used with nouns.

examples.ja: 今日に限り、この商品が半額です。
examples.romaji: Kyou ni kagiri, kono shouhin ga hangaku desu.
examples.meaningEn: Today only, this product is half price.

examples.ja: このクーポンはこの店に限り有効です。
examples.romaji: Kono kuupon wa kono mise ni kagiri yuukou desu.
examples.meaningEn: This coupon is valid only at this store.

examples.ja: 会員に限り、無料でこのサービスを利用できます。
examples.romaji: Kaiin ni kagiri, muryou de kono saabisu wo riyou dekimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Only members can use this service for free.

examples.ja: この特典は先着10名に限ります。
examples.romaji: Kono tokuten wa senchaku 10-mei ni kagirimasu.
examples.meaningEn: This benefit is limited to the first 10 people.

title: ～に際して (〜ni saishite)
formation: Noun + に際して
shortExplanationEn: Used to express the moment or the occasion of an action; 'at the time of', 'upon', 'when'.
longExplanationEn: The ～に際して grammar point describes an action that occurs during a specific event, occasion, or when something takes place. It can be translated into English as 'at the time of', 'upon', 'when', or 'in the event of'. This grammar is used with nouns to describe the context or situation during which an action takes place.

examples.ja: 卒業式に際して、校長先生がスピーチをします。
examples.romaji: Sotsugyoushiki ni saishite, kouchou sensei ga supiichi wo shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: At the graduation ceremony, the principal gives a speech.

examples.ja: この仕事に際して、責任感が大切です。
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto ni saishite, sekininkan ga taisetsu desu.
examples.meaningEn: Responsibility is important when doing this job.

examples.ja: 留学に際して、ビザの手続きが必要です。
examples.romaji: Ryuugaku ni saishite, biza no tetsuzuki ga hitsuyou desu.
examples.meaningEn: Visa procedures are necessary when studying abroad.

examples.ja: 結婚式に際して、両親に感謝の気持ちを伝えました。
examples.romaji: Kekkonshiki ni saishite, ryoushin ni kansha no kimochi wo tsutaemashita.
examples.meaningEn: At the time of the wedding ceremony, I conveyed my gratitude to my parents.

title: ～ねばならない (〜neba naranai)
formation: Verb-ない form → (remove い) → ね + ばならない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses obligation or necessity; 'must', 'have to', 'need to'.
longExplanationEn: The ～ねばならない grammar point is used to express obligation or necessity. It can be translated as 'must', 'have to', or 'need to' in English. This grammar point is mainly used with verbs, indicating that the action must be done.

examples.ja: 試験に合格するために、もっと勉強せねばならない。
examples.romaji: Shiken ni goukaku suru tame ni, motto benkyou se neba naranai.
examples.meaningEn: To pass the exam, I must study more.

examples.ja: 飛行機に間に合うためには、急がねばならない。
examples.romaji: Hikouki ni maniau tame ni wa, isoga neba naranai.
examples.meaningEn: To catch the flight, I must hurry.

examples.ja: ６時までに帰らねばならない。
examples.romaji: Roku ji made ni kaera neba naranai.
examples.meaningEn: I have to go home by 6 o'clock.

examples.ja: この仕事を今日中に終わらせねばならない。
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto wo kyoujuu ni owarase neba naranai.
examples.meaningEn: I need to finish this work by the end of the day.

title: ～のみならず～も (〜nomi narazu 〜mo)
formation: Verb-casual + のみならず + (も), い-Adjective + のみならず + (も), な-Adjective + のみならず + (も), Noun + のみならず + (も)
shortExplanationEn: Not only... but also; as well as.
longExplanationEn: The ～のみならず～も grammar point is used to show that something applies not only to one thing, but also to another thing. This can be translated as 'not only... but also', or 'as well as' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

examples.ja: 彼は英語のみならず、フランス語も話せます。
examples.romaji: Kare wa eigo nomi narazu, furansugo mo hanasemasu.
examples.meaningEn: He can speak not only English but also French.

examples.ja: このレストランは美味しいのみならず、雰囲気も素晴らしいです。
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa oishii nomi narazu, fun'iki mo subarashii desu.
examples.meaningEn: Not only is this restaurant delicious, but the atmosphere is also great.

examples.ja: このアプリは便利のみならず、安全性も高いです。
examples.romaji: Kono apuri wa benri nomi narazu, anzen-sei mo takai desu.
examples.meaningEn: This app is not only convenient but also highly secure.

examples.ja: 彼女は頭が良いのみならず、スポーツも得意です。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa atama ga ii nomi narazu, supootsu mo tokui desu.
examples.meaningEn: She is not only smart but also good at sports.

title: ～のももっともだ (〜no mo mottomo da)
formation: Verb-casual + のももっともだ, い-Adjective + のももっともだ, な-Adjective + なのももっともだ, Noun + なのももっともだ
shortExplanationEn: Express that something is understandable or reasonable.
longExplanationEn: The ～のももっともだ grammar point is used to express that a situation, action, or feeling is understandable or reasonable, considering the circumstances. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

examples.ja: 彼が緊張するのももっともだ。
examples.romaji: Kare ga kinchou suru no mo mottomo da.
examples.meaningEn: It's understandable that he is nervous.

examples.ja: この問題が難しいのももっともだ。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai ga muzukashii no mo mottomo da.
examples.meaningEn: It's reasonable that this problem is difficult.

examples.ja: 彼女が幸せなのももっともだ。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga shiawase na no mo mottomo da.
examples.meaningEn: It's understandable that she is happy.

examples.ja: 彼がリーダーなのももっともだ。
examples.romaji: Kare ga riidaa na no mo mottomo da.
examples.meaningEn: It's reasonable that he is the leader.

title: 〜の上では (〜no ue de wa)
formation: Noun or Phrase + の上では
shortExplanationEn: Indicates 'on the basis of', 'in terms of', or 'from the standpoint of'.
longExplanationEn: The 〜の上では grammar point is used to show that you are expressing a view or making a judgment based on a specific standpoint or perspective. It can be translated as 'on the basis of', 'in terms of', or 'from the standpoint of'. The formation is straightforward, which involves attaching 〜の上では to a noun or a phrase.

examples.ja: 効率の上では、この方法が最適です。
examples.romaji: Kouritsu no ue de wa, kono houhou ga saiteki desu.
examples.meaningEn: In terms of efficiency, this method is the best.

examples.ja: 法律の上では、彼の行為は違法ではありません。
examples.romaji: Houritsu no ue de wa, kare no koui wa ihan dewa arimasen.
examples.meaningEn: From a legal standpoint, his actions are not illegal.

examples.ja: 味の上では、このレストランは評価が高いです。
examples.romaji: Aji no ue de wa, kono resutoran wa hyouka ga takai desu.
examples.meaningEn: In terms of taste, this restaurant has a high rating.

examples.ja: 成績の上では、彼はクラスで一番優秀です。
examples.romaji: Seiseki no ue de wa, kare wa kurasu de ichiban yuushuu desu.
examples.meaningEn: On the basis of grades, he is the most outstanding student in the class.

title: ～の下で (〜no shita de)
formation: Verb-casual + の下で, い-Adjective + の下で, な-Adjective + の下で, Noun + の下で
shortExplanationEn: Under the context or condition of; under.
longExplanationEn: The ～の下で grammar point is used to express that something occurs under a certain context, condition, or circumstance. It can be translated as 'under' or 'in' in English. The formation is simple, as it can be used with verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives, and nouns.

examples.ja: 先生の下で勉強するのはとても楽しいです。
examples.romaji: Sensei no shita de benkyou suru no wa totemo tanoshii desu.
examples.meaningEn: Studying under the teacher is very enjoyable.

examples.ja: 晴天の下でピクニックをしましょう。
examples.romaji: Seiten no shita de pikunikku wo shimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Let's have a picnic under the clear sky.

examples.ja: 緊張感の下で、彼女はスピーチに成功しました。
examples.romaji: Kinchoukan no shita de, kanojo wa supiichi ni seikou shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Under pressure, she succeeded in her speech.

examples.ja: 親の愛情の下で、子供たちは幸せに育ちます。
examples.romaji: Oya no aijou no shita de, kodomo-tachi wa shiawase ni sodachimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Under the love of their parents, children grow up happily.

title: ～ばかりか〜も (〜bakari ka 〜 mo)
formation: Noun/Verb/Adjective + ばかりか + Noun/Verb/Adjective + も
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'not only... but also'; emphasizing the extent of something.
longExplanationEn: The ～ばかりか〜も grammar point is used to express the idea of 'not only... but also' in a sentence. It emphasizes the extent to which something applies, showing a certain component or factor is not the only relevant one.

examples.ja: 彼はかわいいばかりか、賢くもある。
examples.romaji: Kare wa kawaii bakari ka, kashikoku mo aru.
examples.meaningEn: He is not only cute but also smart.

examples.ja: このレストランは味が良いばかりかサービスも素晴らしいです。
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa aji ga yoi bakari ka saabisu mo subarashii desu.
examples.meaningEn: This restaurant not only has great taste but also wonderful service.

examples.ja: 彼女は日本語が上手ばかりか英語も話せる。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa nihongo ga jouzu bakari ka eigo mo hanaseru.
examples.meaningEn: She can speak not only Japanese but also English.

examples.ja: 彼は歌が上手いばかりかダンスもうまい。
examples.romaji: Kare wa uta ga umai bakari ka dansu mo umai.
examples.meaningEn: He is not only good at singing but also dancing.

title: ～ばかりだ (〜bakari da)
formation: Verb-てform + ばかりだ, い-Adjective + ばかりだ, な-Adjective + ばかりだ, Noun + ばかりだ
shortExplanationEn: Express a continuing state or negative tendency; 'just', 'only', 'always'.
longExplanationEn: The ～ばかりだ grammar point is used to express a continuing or unchanging state, often emphasizing a negative or undesired tendency. It can be translated as 'just', 'only', or 'always' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

examples.ja: 彼は文句を言ってばかりだ。
examples.romaji: Kare wa monku wo itte bakari da.
examples.meaningEn: He is always complaining.

examples.ja: この映画は面白くなくて、退屈してばかりだ。
examples.romaji: Kono eiga wa omoshirokunakute, taikutsu shite bakari da.
examples.meaningEn: This movie isn't interesting, and I'm just bored.

examples.ja: 彼女は暇でいるばかりだ。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa hima de iru bakari da.
examples.meaningEn: She is always just idling around with nothing to do.

examples.ja: 最近、仕事の失敗ばかりだ。
examples.romaji: Saikin, shigoto no shippai bakari da.
examples.meaningEn: Lately, it’s just been one failure at work after another.

title: ～ばかりに (〜bakari ni)
formation: Verb-casual + ばかりに, い-Adjective + ばかりに, な-Adjective + なばかりに, Noun + なばかりに
shortExplanationEn: Express an undesired situation or result caused by something; 'simply because', 'due to', 'on account of'.
longExplanationEn: The ～ばかりに grammar point is used to express an undesired situation or result that arises simply because of some factor. It can be translated as 'simply because', 'due to', or 'on account of' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

examples.ja: 雨が降ったばかりに、試合が中止になりました。
examples.romaji: Ame ga futta bakari ni, shiai ga chuushi ni narimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Simply because it rained, the match was cancelled.

examples.ja: 彼が遅れたばかりに、私たちも遅刻しました。
examples.romaji: Kare ga okureta bakari ni, watashitachi mo chikoku shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Because he was late, we ended up being late too.

examples.ja: 忘れ物をしたばかりに、買い物に戻らなければなりません。
examples.romaji: Wasuremono wo shita bakari ni, kaimono ni modoranakereba narimasen.
examples.meaningEn: Simply because I forgot something, I have to go back to the store.

examples.ja: 彼女が美人なばかりに、みんなが彼女を羨んでいます。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga bijin na bakari ni, minna ga kanojo wo urayande imasu.
examples.meaningEn: Just because she is beautiful, everyone is envious of her.

title: ～ば～というものでもない (〜ba 〜to iu mono demo nai)
formation: Verb-ば form + というものでもない
shortExplanationEn: Not necessarily true that if A, then B.
longExplanationEn: The ～ば～というものでもない grammar point is used to express that something is not necessarily true even if a certain condition is met. In other words, if A happens, it doesn't always mean B will happen.

examples.ja: 高いものがいいものばかりではない。
examples.romaji: Takai mono ga ii mono bakari de wa nai.
examples.meaningEn: Expensive things are not always good.

examples.ja: 英語ができれば、成功するというものでもない。
examples.romaji: Eigo ga dekireba, seikou suru to iu mono demo nai.
examples.meaningEn: Just because you can speak English doesn’t mean you’ll succeed.

examples.ja: 見た目が美しいからといって、心も美しいというものでもない。
examples.romaji: Mitame ga utsukushii kara to itte, kokoro mo utsukushii to iu mono demo nai.
examples.meaningEn: Just because someone is beautiful on the outside, it doesn’t mean they’re beautiful on the inside.

examples.ja: 有名大学に入れば、将来が安泰だというものでもない。
examples.romaji: Yuumei daigaku ni haireba, shourai ga antai da to iu mono demo nai.
examples.meaningEn: Getting into a famous university doesn’t guarantee a secure future.

title: ～はともかく～は (〜wa tomokaku 〜wa)
formation: Noun1 はともかく Noun2 は
shortExplanationEn: Used to compare or contrast two elements; 'as for...'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point ～はともかく～は is used to compare or contrast two elements in a sentence. It implies that the first element can be set aside or is less important compared to the second element. It often translates to 'putting A aside, B...'.

examples.ja: デザインはともかく、機能はこの携帯電話が一番です。
examples.romaji: Dezain wa tomokaku, kinou wa kono keitai denwa ga ichiban desu.
examples.meaningEn: As for design, the functionality of this phone is the best.

examples.ja: 彼の容姿はともかく、性格は素晴らしいです。
examples.romaji: Kare no youshi wa tomokaku, seikaku wa subarashii desu.
examples.meaningEn: Putting aside his looks, his personality is wonderful.

examples.ja: 料理の味はともかく、盛り付けは綺麗です。
examples.romaji: Ryouri no aji wa tomokaku, moritsuke wa kirei desu.
examples.meaningEn: Putting aside the taste of the food, the presentation is beautiful.

examples.ja: 値段はともかく、このバッグの質は良いです。
examples.romaji: Nedan wa tomokaku, kono baggu no shitsu wa yoi desu.
examples.meaningEn: Ignoring the price, this bag’s quality is good.

title: ～はまだしも (〜wa mada shimo)
formation: Noun1 + はまだしも + Noun2
shortExplanationEn: Expresses that while the first item is somewhat acceptable, the second item is not.
longExplanationEn: The ～はまだしも grammar point is used to show a contrast where the first item or situation might still be understandable or tolerable, whereas the second one is harder to accept. It’s often translated as 'X is one thing, but Y...'.

examples.ja: 失敗するのはまだしも、やらないで後悔する方がつらい。
examples.romaji: Shippai suru no wa mada shimo, yaranai de koukai suru hou ga tsurai.
examples.meaningEn: Failing is one thing, but regretting not even trying is worse.

examples.ja: このピザは野菜が少ないのはまだしも、味も悪い。
examples.romaji: Kono piza wa yasai ga sukunai no wa mada shimo, aji mo warui.
examples.meaningEn: It’s not just that this pizza has few vegetables, it also tastes bad.

examples.ja: たまに遅刻するのはまだしも、連絡もせずに遅刻するのは許せない。
examples.romaji: Tama ni chikoku suru no wa mada shimo, renraku mo sezu ni chikoku suru no wa yurusenai.
examples.meaningEn: Being late occasionally might be forgivable, but being late without any contact is unacceptable.

examples.ja: 難しい質問に答えられないのはまだしも、簡単な質問にも答えられないのは問題だ。
examples.romaji: Muzukashii shitsumon ni kotaerarenai no wa mada shimo, kantan na shitsumon ni mo kotaerarenai no wa mondai da.
examples.meaningEn: It’s one thing not to be able to answer difficult questions, but it’s a real problem if you can’t even answer simple ones.

title: ～はもとより (〜wa moto yori)
formation: Noun1 + はもとより, Noun2 + も
shortExplanationEn: Expressing that something is obvious, and there is also something else.
longExplanationEn: The ～はもとより grammar point is used to emphasize that something is obvious or a given, and on top of that, there’s another aspect to consider. It translates to 'not only…but also…' or 'of course…and also…'.

examples.ja: 英語はもとより、スペイン語も勉強しています。
examples.romaji: Eigo wa moto yori, supeingo mo benkyou shite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: I'm studying not only English, but also Spanish.

examples.ja: 彼女は料理はもとより、お菓子作りも得意です。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa ryouri wa moto yori, okashi tsukuri mo tokui desu.
examples.meaningEn: She’s not only good at cooking, but also at making sweets.

examples.ja: このホテルは部屋の清潔さはもとより、サービスも素晴らしいです。
examples.romaji: Kono hoteru wa heya no seiketsusa wa moto yori, saabisu mo subarashii desu.
examples.meaningEn: This hotel not only has clean rooms but also great service.

examples.ja: 彼は音楽はもとより、絵も上手です。
examples.romaji: Kare wa ongaku wa moto yori, e mo jouzu desu.
examples.meaningEn: He’s not only good at music, but also skilled at drawing.

title: ～は抜きにして (〜wa nuki ni shite)
formation: Noun + は抜きにして
shortExplanationEn: Leaving aside, excluding.
longExplanationEn: The ～は抜きにして grammar point is used to express the idea of 'leaving aside' or 'excluding' a certain aspect. It is often used to focus the discussion or judgment on something else by disregarding the omitted point.

examples.ja: 値段は抜きにして、味は美味しい。
examples.romaji: Nedan wa nuki ni shite, aji wa oishii.
examples.meaningEn: Leaving aside the price, the taste is delicious.

examples.ja: 彼の性格は抜きにして、彼の実力は認めざるを得ない。
examples.romaji: Kare no seikaku wa nuki ni shite, kare no jitsuryoku wa mitomezaru o enai.
examples.meaningEn: Putting aside his personality, I have to acknowledge his ability.

examples.ja: デザインは抜きにして、この携帯電話の機能は優れています。
examples.romaji: Dezain wa nuki ni shite, kono keitai denwa no kinou wa sugurete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: Leaving aside the design, this phone’s features are excellent.

examples.ja: 運転の速さは抜きにして、彼女は親切で信頼できる運転手だ。
examples.romaji: Unten no hayasa wa nuki ni shite, kanojo wa shinsetsu de shinrai dekiru untenshu da.
examples.meaningEn: Leaving aside her driving speed, she's a kind and trustworthy driver.

title: ～べきではない (〜beki dewa nai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + べき + ではない
shortExplanationEn: Express that an action should not be done; 'should not', 'ought not to'.
longExplanationEn: The ～べきではない grammar point is used to indicate that an action is not recommended or should not be done. It can be translated as 'should not' or 'ought not to' in English. The typical formation is “Verb-dictionary form + べきではない.”

examples.ja: 遅れる理由がないなら、遅れるべきではありません。
examples.romaji: Okureru riyuu ga nai nara, okureru beki dewa arimasen.
examples.meaningEn: If there's no reason to be late, you shouldn’t be late.

examples.ja: 子供に悪口を言うべきではありません。
examples.romaji: Kodomo ni waruguchi o iu beki dewa arimasen.
examples.meaningEn: You shouldn’t say hurtful things to children.

examples.ja: この薬は一度に飲みすぎるべきではない。
examples.romaji: Kono kusuri wa ichido ni nomisugiru beki dewa nai.
examples.meaningEn: You should not take too much of this medicine at once.

examples.ja: 知らない人と個人情報を共有するべきではない。
examples.romaji: Shiranai hito to kojin jouhou o kyouyuu suru beki dewa nai.
examples.meaningEn: You shouldn't share personal information with strangers.

title: ～まい (〜mai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + まい
shortExplanationEn: Express a negative conjecture or determination; 'probably not' or 'I will not'.
longExplanationEn: The ～まい grammar point is used to express a negative conjecture or a strong determination not to do something. In modern usage, it is generally used with the dictionary form of verbs (来る→来るまい, する→するまい, etc.). It can be translated as 'probably not' or 'I will not' in English.

examples.ja: 彼は今晩来るまい。
examples.romaji: Kare wa konban kuru mai.
examples.meaningEn: He probably won't come tonight.

examples.ja: こんなに遅れては、電車に間に合うまい。
examples.romaji: Konnani okurete wa, densha ni maniau mai.
examples.meaningEn: Being this late, I probably won't make it for the train.

examples.ja: あの人は絶対に忘れるまい。
examples.romaji: Ano hito wa zettai ni wasureru mai.
examples.meaningEn: That person will definitely not forget.

examples.ja: 私は二度とその失敗を繰り返すまい。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa nidoto sono shippai o kurikaesu mai.
examples.meaningEn: I will not repeat that mistake again.

title: ～まで～て (〜made 〜te)
formation: Time/Noun + まで + Verb(て-form)
shortExplanationEn: Express doing something until a certain time or condition; 'until', 'to'.
longExplanationEn: The ～まで～て grammar point is used to show that an action continues until a certain time or condition. In your examples, a time expression (such as 9時) is followed by まで, then the action in the て-form. It can be translated as 'until' or 'up to the point when/where'.

examples.ja: 9時まで働いて、その後休憩しました。
examples.romaji: Ku-ji made hataraite, sono ato kyuukei shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I worked until 9 o'clock, and then took a break.

examples.ja: 夜まで勉強して、試験に合格しました。
examples.romaji: Yoru made benkyou shite, shiken ni goukaku shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I studied until night, and passed the exam.

examples.ja: 最後のページまで読んで、本を閉じました。
examples.romaji: Saigo no peeji made yonde, hon o tojimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I read until the last page, then closed the book.

examples.ja: 電車が着くまで待って、友達と会いました。
examples.romaji: Densha ga tsuku made matte, tomodachi to aimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I waited until the train arrived, and then met my friend.

title: ～ままに (〜mama ni)
formation: Verb-て + まま, い-Adjective + のまま, な-Adjective + なまま, Noun + のまま
shortExplanationEn: Act according to; leave as is; unchanged.
longExplanationEn: The ～ままに grammar point is used to express leaving something in its current state or doing something exactly as is. It can also imply doing something naturally or without altering the circumstances. Depending on the part of speech, different particles are used: Verb-て + まま, い-Adjective + のまま, な-Adjective + なまま, Noun + のまま.

examples.ja: 子供が寝ているままに、静かに部屋を出ました。
examples.romaji: Kodomo ga nete iru mama ni, shizuka ni heya o demashita.
examples.meaningEn: I quietly left the room while the child was still sleeping.

examples.ja: 窓が開いたままで、寒くなりました。
examples.romaji: Mado ga aita mama de, samuku narimashita.
examples.meaningEn: It got cold with the window left open.

examples.ja: 彼は元気なままで老人ホームに入りました。
examples.romaji: Kare wa genki na mama de roujin hoomu ni hairimashita.
examples.meaningEn: He entered the nursing home while still in good health.

examples.ja: 昔の思い出のままに、この町に戻りました。
examples.romaji: Mukashi no omoide no mama ni, kono machi ni modorimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I returned to this town with my old memories intact.

title: ～もかまわず (〜mo kamawazu)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + もかまわず, Noun + もかまわず
shortExplanationEn: Express indifference or disregard; 'without hesitating', 'without worrying about'.
longExplanationEn: The ～もかまわず grammar point is used to express indifference or disregard toward possible consequences or external factors. It can be translated as 'without hesitating', 'without worrying about', or 'regardless of'. It attaches to verbs in their dictionary form or nouns.

examples.ja: 彼は周りの人もかまわず、大声で話しました。
examples.romaji: Kare wa mawari no hito mo kamawazu, oogoe de hanashimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Without worrying about the people around him, he spoke loudly.

examples.ja: 彼女は時間もかまわず、ゆっくりと本を読み続けました。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa jikan mo kamawazu, yukkuri to hon o yomitsuzukemashita.
examples.meaningEn: She kept reading the book leisurely, without worrying about the time.

examples.ja: 彼は寒さもかまわず、外で運動しました。
examples.romaji: Kare wa samusa mo kamawazu, soto de undou shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: He exercised outside without worrying about the cold.

examples.ja: 彼女は危険もかまわず、火事の中に飛び込んで助けに行きました。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa kiken mo kamawazu, kaji no naka ni tobikonde tasuke ni ikimashita.
examples.meaningEn: She jumped into the fire without fear of danger to help.

title: ～ものか (〜mono ka)
formation: Verb-casual + ものか, い-Adjective + ものか, な-Adjective + (な)ものか, Noun + (な)ものか
shortExplanationEn: Definitely not; no way; no chance.
longExplanationEn: The ～ものか grammar point is used to express a strong denial, refusal, or astonishment. It often appears with a rhetorical question tone in casual forms, such as Verb (dictionary/volitional/～たい) + ものか, Adjective + ものか, or Noun + ものか, meaning 'No way...', 'As if I'd ever...', etc.

examples.ja: そんなこと、僕がやりたいものか。
examples.romaji: Sonna koto, boku ga yaritai mono ka.
examples.meaningEn: No way do I want to do such a thing.

examples.ja: こんな仕事、誰が引き受けようものか。
examples.romaji: Konna shigoto, dare ga hikiukeyou mono ka.
examples.meaningEn: Who on earth would take on a job like this? (No one would!)

examples.ja: 誰が彼に助けを求めようものか。
examples.romaji: Dare ga kare ni tasuke o motomeyou mono ka.
examples.meaningEn: Who would ever ask him for help? (Certainly no one!)

examples.ja: この寒い日に外で遊ぶなんて、無理なものか。
examples.romaji: Kono samui hi ni soto de asobu nante, muri na mono ka.
examples.meaningEn: There's no way we can play outside on a day this cold.

title: ～ものがある (〜mono ga aru)
formation: Verb-casual + ものがある, い-Adjective + ものがある, な-Adjective + なものがある
shortExplanationEn: Express a strong impression or feeling; 'there's something about it that...'
longExplanationEn: 「～ものがある」 is used to convey that there is a certain intangible quality or strong feeling about something that the speaker cannot ignore. It is often used with verbs (plain form), い-adjectives, or な-adjectives + な, to show that one ‘feels strongly that...’ or ‘there’s something about it that…’.

examples.ja: 彼のスピーチには心を打たれるものがある。
examples.romaji: Kare no supiichi ni wa kokoro o utareru mono ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: There's something moving about his speech.

examples.ja: この映画には考えさせられるものがある。
examples.romaji: Kono eiga ni wa kangaesaserareru mono ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: There's something about this movie that really makes you think.

examples.ja: 彼の言うことにも一理あるものがある。
examples.romaji: Kare no iu koto ni mo ichiri aru mono ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: There's a certain point in what he says.

examples.ja: 毎日忙しすぎる生活には少し息苦しいものがある。
examples.romaji: Mainichi isogashisugiru seikatsu ni wa sukoshi ikigurushii mono ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: There's something a bit suffocating about being so busy every day.

title: ～ものだ (〜mono da)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + ものだ, な-Adjective + なものだ, (Also: Verb-た form + ものだ for recollections)
shortExplanationEn: Expresses something natural or expected; 'it is normal that...', 'should', 'ought to'.
longExplanationEn: 「～ものだ」 can indicate that something is regarded as a general truth, common sense, or natural behavior. It can also express strong sentiment or emotion. In the past tense (Verb-た form + ものだ), it often shows nostalgia or recollection ('I used to...').

examples.ja: 若い時はたくさん遊ぶものだ。
examples.romaji: Wakai toki wa takusan asobu mono da.
examples.meaningEn: It is natural to have a lot of fun when you are young.

examples.ja: 約束をしたら、守るものだ。
examples.romaji: Yakusoku o shitara, mamoru mono da.
examples.meaningEn: If you make a promise, you should keep it.

examples.ja: お客さんが来ると、お茶を出すものだ。
examples.romaji: Okyakusan ga kuru to, ocha o dasu mono da.
examples.meaningEn: When guests arrive, it's customary to serve tea.

examples.ja: 人生は困難に立ち向かうものだ。
examples.romaji: Jinsei wa konnan ni tachimukau mono da.
examples.meaningEn: In life, one ought to face hardships.

title: ～ものだから (〜mono dakara)
formation: Verb-plain + ものだから, い-Adjective + ものだから, な-Adjective + なものだから, Noun + なものだから
shortExplanationEn: Express the reason or cause for something; 'because', 'since', 'due to'.
longExplanationEn: 「～ものだから」 is used to explain a reason or cause from the speaker’s point of view. It can be translated as 'because', 'since', or 'due to'. It often carries the nuance of 'you see, that's why ...'.

examples.ja: 試験が近いものだから、毎日勉強しています。
examples.romaji: Shiken ga chikai mono dakara, mainichi benkyou shiteimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Because the exam is near, I'm studying every day.

examples.ja: このレストランはとても人気があるものだから、予約しないと席がないかもしれません。
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa totemo ninki ga aru mono dakara, yoyaku shinai to seki ga nai kamoshiremasen.
examples.meaningEn: Because this restaurant is very popular, there might not be any seats if we don't reserve.

examples.ja: 彼は若いものだから、まだたくさんの経験を積んでいない。
examples.romaji: Kare wa wakai mono dakara, mada takusan no keiken wo tsunde inai.
examples.meaningEn: Because he is young, he hasn't accumulated much experience yet.

examples.ja: 今日は寒いものだから、手袋を持って行きましょう。
examples.romaji: Kyou wa samui mono dakara, tebukuro wo motte ikimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Since it's cold today, let's bring gloves.

title: ～ものではない (〜mono dewa nai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + ものではない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses a strong negative suggestion or prohibition: 'should not', 'must not'.
longExplanationEn: 「～ものではない」 is used to indicate that something should not or must not be done. It often appears in written or formal contexts, meaning 'it is not proper to...' or 'you shouldn’t...'.

examples.ja: そんなことを言うものではありません。
examples.romaji: Sonna koto o iu mono dewa arimasen.
examples.meaningEn: You should not say such a thing.

examples.ja: 公共の場で大声を出すものではない。
examples.romaji: Koukyou no ba de oogoe o dasu mono dewa nai.
examples.meaningEn: You should not raise your voice in public places.

examples.ja: 彼の秘密を他の人に教えるものではありません。
examples.romaji: Kare no himitsu o hoka no hito ni oshieru mono dewa arimasen.
examples.meaningEn: You must not tell his secret to other people.

examples.ja: 安全な場所では遊んでいいが、危険なところで遊ぶものではない。
examples.romaji: Anzen na basho de wa asonde ii ga, kiken na tokoro de asobu mono dewa nai.
examples.meaningEn: It's okay to play in safe places, but you should not play somewhere dangerous.

title: ～ものなら (〜mono nara)
formation: Verb-(potential/plain) form + ものなら, etc.
shortExplanationEn: Expresses a conditional wish or unlikely possibility; 'if only', 'if it were possible'.
longExplanationEn: 「～ものなら」 is used when expressing a strong wish under a hypothetical or difficult condition. It often implies that fulfilling this condition is unlikely or very hard, but 'if only it could happen, then...'.

examples.ja: 帰れるものなら、今すぐ国へ帰りたい。
examples.romaji: Kaereru mono nara, ima sugu kuni e kaeritai.
examples.meaningEn: If I could possibly go back, I'd return to my country right now.

examples.ja: できるものなら、世界を一周してみたい。
examples.romaji: Dekiru mono nara, sekai o isshuu shite mitai.
examples.meaningEn: If it were possible, I'd love to travel around the world.

examples.ja: やり直せるものなら、子供の頃に戻りたい。
examples.romaji: Yarinaoseru mono nara, kodomo no koro ni modoritai.
examples.meaningEn: If I could redo my life, I'd go back to my childhood.

examples.ja: 元気になれるものなら、どんな薬でも試したい。
examples.romaji: Genki ni nareru mono nara, donna kusuri demo tameshitai.
examples.meaningEn: If it could make me healthy, I'd try any medicine.

title: ～ものの、～ (〜mono no、～)
formation: Verb-casual + ものの, い-Adjective + ものの, な-Adjective + なものの, Noun + であるものの
shortExplanationEn: Indicates contrast or contradiction between two clauses; 'although', 'but'.
longExplanationEn: 「～ものの」 is used to connect two parts of a sentence where the first part is true, yet the second part shows a contrasting or unexpected outcome. It can be translated as 'although', 'even though', or 'but still'.

examples.ja: 早起きしたものの、バスに間に合わなかった。
examples.romaji: Hayaoki shita mono no, basu ni maniawanakatta.
examples.meaningEn: Even though I got up early, I didn't catch the bus in time.

examples.ja: この部屋は広いものの、家具が少ない。
examples.romaji: Kono heya wa hiroi mono no, kagu ga sukunai.
examples.meaningEn: Although this room is spacious, there isn't much furniture.

examples.ja: 彼女は美しいものの、性格が悪い。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa utsukushii mono no, seikaku ga warui.
examples.meaningEn: She is beautiful, but her personality is bad.

examples.ja: 彼は有名な歌手であるものの、コンサートにあまり人が来なかった。
examples.romaji: Kare wa yuumei na kashu de aru mono no, konsaato ni amari hito ga konakatta.
examples.meaningEn: Although he is a famous singer, not many people came to his concert.

title: ～も～ば～も～ (〜mo〜ba〜mo〜)
formation: Noun/Verb/Adjective + も + (Verb/Adjective in ば-form) + も + (Verb/Adjective)
shortExplanationEn: Lists multiple attributes or actions applying to the same subject: 'both…and…', 'not only…but also…'.
longExplanationEn: 「～も～ば～も～」 is used when describing multiple characteristics or actions. It often appears with the conditional form (ば) plus も, indicating that 'A is true, and also B is true.' For example, 'He can do X, and he can also do Y.' It's different from '～ば～ほど', which means 'the more… the more…'.

examples.ja: 彼は勉強もできればスポーツもできる。
examples.romaji: Kare wa benkyou mo dekireba supootsu mo dekiru.
examples.meaningEn: He is good at studying and also at sports.

examples.ja: このレストランは値段も安ければ味もいい。
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa nedan mo yasukereba aji mo ii.
examples.meaningEn: This restaurant is cheap, and the food is also good.

examples.ja: 彼女はピアノも弾けばギターも弾く。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa piano mo hikeba gitaa mo hiku.
examples.meaningEn: She plays both the piano and the guitar.

examples.ja: 人生には楽しい時もあれば辛い時もある。
examples.romaji: Jinsei ni wa tanoshii toki mo areba tsurai toki mo aru.
examples.meaningEn: In life, there are both fun times and tough times.

title: ～も同然だ (〜mo douzen da)
formation: Noun + (も)同然だ, Verb-casual + (も)同然だ, etc.
shortExplanationEn: Indicates 'as good as', 'virtually', or 'practically' the same as something.
longExplanationEn: 「～も同然だ」 means something is practically the same as (X). It emphasizes that although it may not be literally identical, there is hardly any difference in reality.

examples.ja: 彼とは長い付き合いだから、家族も同然だ。
examples.romaji: Kare to wa nagai tsukiai dakara, kazoku mo douzen da.
examples.meaningEn: I've known him for so long that he's practically family.

examples.ja: 毎日遅刻するなんて、来ていないも同然だ。
examples.romaji: Mainichi chikoku suru nante, kite inai mo douzen da.
examples.meaningEn: Being late every single day is as good as not coming at all.

examples.ja: 彼女と私は幼なじみで、姉妹も同然の仲です。
examples.romaji: Kanojo to watashi wa osananajimi de, shimai mo douzen no naka desu.
examples.meaningEn: She and I are childhood friends, practically like sisters.

examples.ja: こんなに少ない給料じゃ、ただ働きも同然だ。
examples.romaji: Konna ni sukunai kyuuryou ja, tadabataraki mo douzen da.
examples.meaningEn: With such a small salary, it's practically like working for free.

title: ～やら～やら (〜yara〜yara)
formation: Verb-casual + やら, い-Adjective + やら, な-Adjective + やら, Noun + やら
shortExplanationEn: Expresses various items or actions, 'things like..., and so on'.
longExplanationEn: 「～やら～やら」 is used to list multiple things or actions in a somewhat non-exhaustive way. It can be translated as 'things like A and B' or 'A, B, and so on.' The grammar is often Verb-casual + やら, Noun + やら, い-Adjective + やら, etc.

examples.ja: 宿題やら掃除やら、やることがたくさんある。
examples.romaji: Shukudai yara souji yara, yaru koto ga takusan aru.
examples.meaningEn: I have lots of things to do, like homework and cleaning.

examples.ja: 野菜やら果物やら、色々な食べ物を買いました。
examples.romaji: Yasai yara kudamono yara, iroiro na tabemono wo kaimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I bought all sorts of foods, such as vegetables and fruits.

examples.ja: 彼女は歌がうまいやら踊れるやら、本当に多才だ。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa uta ga umai yara odoreru yara, hontou ni tasai da.
examples.meaningEn: She’s really talented—she can sing well, dance, and so on.

examples.ja: お店にはバッグやら靴やらアクセサリーやらが置いてある。
examples.romaji: Omise ni wa baggu yara kutsu yara akusesarii yara ga oite aru.
examples.meaningEn: The store has all kinds of items like bags, shoes, accessories, etc.

title: ～ようがない (〜you ga nai)
formation: Verb-ます stem + ようがない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses that there is no way (method) to do something; it's impossible to do.
longExplanationEn: 「～ようがない」 is used with the ます-stem of a verb to indicate that there is no means or method to carry out that action. It conveys the sense that it's impossible to do something or that there's absolutely no way to achieve it.

examples.ja: 彼の連絡先がわからないので、助けようがない。
examples.romaji: Kare no renrakusaki ga wakaranai node, tasukeyou ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: I don't know his contact information, so there's no way I can help him.

examples.ja: ここまで壊れてしまったら、もう直しようがない。
examples.romaji: Koko made kowarete shimattara, mou naoshiyou ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: Now that it's broken this badly, there's no way to fix it.

examples.ja: 証拠が全くないから、彼が犯人だと証明しようがない。
examples.romaji: Shouko ga mattaku nai kara, kare ga hannin da to shoumeishiyou ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: There’s no evidence at all, so there’s no way to prove he’s the culprit.

examples.ja: 突然英語で話しかけられて、何と答えればいいか分からず答えようがなかった。
examples.romaji: Totsuzen eigo de hanashikakerarete, nan to kotaereba ii ka wakarazu kotaeyou ga nakatta.
examples.meaningEn: I was suddenly spoken to in English and didn’t know how to respond, so there was no way for me to reply.

title: ～よりほかない (〜yori hoka nai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + よりほかない
shortExplanationEn: Express that there is no other choice; 'have no choice but', 'cannot do anything but'.
longExplanationEn: The ～よりほかない grammar point is used to express that there is no other choice or alternative in a given situation. It can be translated as 'have no choice but to...', 'nothing but...', or 'cannot do anything but...'.

examples.ja: 試験が近いので、勉強するよりほかない。
examples.romaji: Shiken ga chikai node, benkyou suru yori hoka nai.
examples.meaningEn: Since the exam is near, there is no choice but to study.

examples.ja: 風邪がひどいので、早く寝るよりほかない。
examples.romaji: Kaze ga hidoi node, hayaku neru yori hoka nai.
examples.meaningEn: My cold is severe, so I have no choice but to go to bed early.

examples.ja: バスが来ないので、歩くよりほかない。
examples.romaji: Basu ga konai node, aruku yori hoka nai.
examples.meaningEn: Since the bus isn't coming, I have no choice but to walk.

examples.ja: お金がないので、安いものを買うよりほかない。
examples.romaji: Okane ga nai node, yasui mono o kau yori hoka nai.
examples.meaningEn: Since I have no money, I have no choice but to buy cheap things.

title: ～わけがない (〜wake ga nai)
formation: Verb-casual + わけがない, い-Adjective + わけがない, な-Adjective + なわけがない, Noun + のわけがない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'there’s no way' or 'it’s impossible', indicating a high degree of certainty.
longExplanationEn: The ～わけがない grammar point is used to express that something is highly unlikely or impossible. It can be translated as 'there's no way' or 'it's impossible'.

examples.ja: 彼が怪我をしたから、試合に勝てるわけがない。
examples.romaji: Kare ga kega wo shita kara, shiai ni kateru wake ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: He got injured, so there's no way he can win the match.

examples.ja: 嘘ばかり言っている人を、信じるわけがない。
examples.romaji: Uso bakari itte iru hito wo, shinjiru wake ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: There's no way I can believe someone who constantly lies.

examples.ja: 彼女は忙しいから、このパーティーに来るわけがない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa isogashii kara, kono paatii ni kuru wake ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: She's busy, so there's no way she can come to this party.

examples.ja: 昨日はずっと寝ていたから、その事件を知るわけがない。
examples.romaji: Kinou wa zutto nete ita kara, sono jiken wo shiru wake ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: I was asleep all day yesterday, so there's no way I could know about that incident.

title: ～わけだ (〜wake da)
formation: Verb-casual + わけだ, い-Adjective + わけだ, な-Adjective + なわけだ, Noun + のわけだ
shortExplanationEn: Express a logical conclusion or reason; 'it means', 'no wonder', 'the reason is'.
longExplanationEn: The ～わけだ grammar point is used to express a logical conclusion or reason. It can be translated as 'it means...', 'no wonder...', or 'that explains why...'.

examples.ja: 彼女が遅れたのは、電車が遅れたわけだ。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga okureta no wa, densha ga okureta wake da.
examples.meaningEn: The reason she was late is that the train was delayed.

examples.ja: 授業が面白かったから、みんな熱心に聞いていたわけだ。
examples.romaji: Jugyou ga omoshirokatta kara, minna nesshin ni kiiteita wake da.
examples.meaningEn: The class was interesting, so that's why everyone was listening intently.

examples.ja: 彼はとても忙しいですから、休みがほとんどないわけだ。
examples.romaji: Kare wa totemo isogashii desu kara, yasumi ga hotondo nai wake da.
examples.meaningEn: He's really busy, which is why he hardly has any time off.

examples.ja: この問題が難しいから、全然わからないわけだ。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai ga muzukashii kara, zenzen wakaranai wake da.
examples.meaningEn: This problem is tough, so no wonder I don't understand it at all.

title: ～わけではない (〜wake dewa nai)
formation: Verb-plain + わけではない, い-Adjective + わけではない, な-Adjective + だわけではない, Noun + だわけではない
shortExplanationEn: Not necessarily; it doesn't mean that ~.
longExplanationEn: The ～わけではない grammar point is used to express that something is not necessarily the case or 'it doesn’t mean that...' It negates a broad assumption or generalization.

examples.ja: 彼が勉強しないわけではないが、成績があまり良くない。
examples.romaji: Kare ga benkyou shinai wake dewa nai ga, seiseki ga amari yoku nai.
examples.meaningEn: It's not that he doesn't study, but his grades aren't very good.

examples.ja: このレストランが高いわけではないけど、毎日通うには少し高いね。
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran ga takai wake dewa nai kedo, mainichi kayou ni wa sukoshi takai ne.
examples.meaningEn: It's not that this restaurant is expensive, but it's a bit too pricey to go there every day.

examples.ja: 彼女が優しくないわけではないが、時々話し方がきついことがある。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga yasashikunai wake dewa nai ga, tokidoki hanashikata ga kitsui koto ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: It's not that she's unkind, but sometimes her manner of speaking can be harsh.

examples.ja: 彼が忙しいわけではないが、時間を上手に使えていないようだ。
examples.romaji: Kare ga isogashii wake dewa nai ga, jikan o jouzu ni tsukaete inai you da.
examples.meaningEn: It's not that he's busy; he just doesn't seem to use his time effectively.

title: ～わけにはいかない (〜wake ni wa ikanai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + わけにはいかない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses that one cannot (or must not) do something due to responsibility, social pressure, etc.
longExplanationEn: The ～わけにはいかない grammar point is used to express that you cannot afford to do something or are not allowed to do it. It often implies a sense of responsibility, moral/social obligation, or external pressure that prevents you from doing it.

examples.ja: 試験が近いから、遊ぶわけにはいかない。
examples.romaji: Shiken ga chikai kara, asobu wake ni wa ikanai.
examples.meaningEn: Since the exam is close, I can't afford to just play around.

examples.ja: 田中さんが待っているので、遅れるわけにはいかない。
examples.romaji: Tanaka-san ga matteiru node, okureru wake ni wa ikanai.
examples.meaningEn: Mr. Tanaka is waiting, so I absolutely must not be late.

examples.ja: 子供たちのために、この仕事を辞めるわけにはいかない。
examples.romaji: Kodomo-tachi no tame ni, kono shigoto o yameru wake ni wa ikanai.
examples.meaningEn: For the sake of my kids, I can't afford to quit this job.

examples.ja: この問題は重要だから、無視するわけにはいかない。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wa juuyou dakara, mushi suru wake ni wa ikanai.
examples.meaningEn: This issue is important, so I can't simply ignore it.

title: ～をきっかけに (〜wo kikkake ni)
formation: Noun + をきっかけに
shortExplanationEn: Indicates that something serves as a trigger or starting point for another action/event.
longExplanationEn: 「～をきっかけに」 is used with a noun to show that an event, thing, or situation became a trigger or motive that led to a new development or action. It translates as 'taking ... as an opportunity,' 'due to ...,' or 'inspired by ...'.

examples.ja: 結婚をきっかけに、彼は真剣に仕事を頑張り始めた。
examples.romaji: Kekkon o kikkake ni, kare wa shinken ni shigoto o ganbarihajimeta.
examples.meaningEn: Getting married was the catalyst for him to start working seriously.

examples.ja: あの出会いをきっかけに、私たちは友達になりました。
examples.romaji: Ano deai o kikkake ni, watashitachi wa tomodachi ni narimashita.
examples.meaningEn: That encounter led us to become friends.

examples.ja: コンサートをきっかけに、彼女は音楽の道に進むことを決めました。
examples.romaji: Konsaato o kikkake ni, kanojo wa ongaku no michi ni susumu koto o kimemashita.
examples.meaningEn: Inspired by the concert, she decided to pursue music.

examples.ja: 留学をきっかけに、私の外国語スキルが向上しました。
examples.romaji: Ryuugaku o kikkake ni, watashi no gaikokugo sukiru ga koujou shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Studying abroad gave me the chance to improve my foreign language skills.

title: ～を～として (〜wo〜toshite)
formation: Noun1 + を + Noun2 + として
shortExplanationEn: Express 'as', 'in the capacity of', or 'taking something as...'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar pattern 「～を～として」 is used with nouns to indicate taking one thing 'as' or 'in the role of' another. It shows how a certain noun is being viewed or utilized.

examples.ja: 彼は教師を職業として働いています。
examples.romaji: Kare wa kyoushi o shokugyou toshite hataraite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: He works as a teacher.

examples.ja: 彼女はリーダーを役割として受け入れました。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa riidaa o yakuwari toshite ukeiremashita.
examples.meaningEn: She accepted the role of leader.

examples.ja: 私たちは彼を仲間として信頼しています。
examples.romaji: Watashitachi wa kare o nakama toshite shinrai shite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: We trust him as one of our own (a close companion).

examples.ja: このアーティストは伝統を基盤として作品を作ります。
examples.romaji: Kono aatisuto wa dentou o kiban toshite sakuhin o tsukurimasu.
examples.meaningEn: This artist creates works with tradition as the foundation.

title: ～を中心に (〜wo chuushin ni)
formation: Noun + を中心に
shortExplanationEn: Indicates that something is centered around or focused on a main topic or area.
longExplanationEn: 「～を中心に」 is used with nouns to show that an action or situation is mainly or primarily about the specified noun. It can be translated as 'centered on', 'focusing on', or 'with ... as the main focus'.

examples.ja: このイベントは音楽を中心に楽しまれています。
examples.romaji: Kono ibento wa ongaku o chuushin ni tanoshimarete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: This event is mainly enjoyed for its music.

examples.ja: 彼の仕事は東京を中心に行われています。
examples.romaji: Kare no shigoto wa Toukyou o chuushin ni okonawarete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: His work is primarily conducted in and around Tokyo.

examples.ja: 彼女は健康を中心に本を書いています。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa kenkou o chuushin ni hon o kaite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: She’s writing a book focusing on health.

examples.ja: 最近の研究は環境保護を中心に行われている。
examples.romaji: Saikin no kenkyuu wa kankyou hogo o chuushin ni okonawarete iru.
examples.meaningEn: Recent research is being carried out with a primary focus on environmental protection.

title: ～を問わず (〜wo towazu)
formation: Noun + を問わず
shortExplanationEn: Means 'regardless of' or 'irrespective of'.
longExplanationEn: 「～を問わず」 is used to express that something applies or is available without any distinction regarding the stated nouns/conditions. It means 'regardless of...' or 'irrespective of...'.

examples.ja: このイベントは、年齢を問わず、誰でも参加できます。
examples.romaji: Kono ibento wa, nenrei o towazu, dare demo sanka dekimasu.
examples.meaningEn: This event is open to everyone, regardless of age.

examples.ja: このスーパーは、日曜日を問わず、毎日開いています。
examples.romaji: Kono suupaa wa, nichiyoubi o towazu, mainichi aite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: This supermarket is open every day, regardless of Sunday.

examples.ja: 彼女は、暑さ寒さを問わず、毎朝散歩をしています。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa, atsusa samusa o towazu, maiasa sanpo o shite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: She takes a walk every morning, no matter the heat or cold.

examples.ja: 社長は、国籍を問わず、すべての社員を平等に扱っています。
examples.romaji: Shachou wa, kokuseki o towazu, subete no shain o byoudou ni atsukatte imasu.
examples.meaningEn: The company president treats all employees equally, regardless of nationality.

title: ～を込めて (〜wo komete)
formation: Feeling/emotion/intention Noun + を + 込めて + Verb
shortExplanationEn: To express that something is done with a certain feeling, intention, or emotion.
longExplanationEn: The ～を込めて grammar point is used to express that an action is done with a certain feeling, intention or emotion. It can be translated as 'with' or 'filled with' in English. ～を込めて is typically used with feelings or emotions that the speaker wishes to convey through their actions.

examples.ja: 愛を込めて料理を作りました。
examples.romaji: Ai wo komete ryouri wo tsukurimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I cooked the meal with love.

examples.ja: 感謝を込めて先生にお礼の手紙を書きました。
examples.romaji: Kansha wo komete sensei ni orei no tegami wo kakimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I wrote a thank-you letter to my teacher, filled with gratitude.

examples.ja: 彼は全力を込めて最後の瞬間まで戦いました。
examples.romaji: Kare wa zenryoku wo komete saigo no shunkan made tatakaimashita.
examples.meaningEn: He fought until the last moment with all his strength.

examples.ja: 彼女は悲しみを込めて友達へ別れの言葉を伝えました。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa kanashimi wo komete tomodachi e wakare no kotoba wo tsutaemashita.
examples.meaningEn: She conveyed her farewell words to her friend with sadness.

title: ～を通じて (〜wo tsuujite)
formation: Noun + を通じて
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'through', 'throughout', or 'during'.
longExplanationEn: The ～を通じて grammar point is used to express actions or conditions that occur through or throughout a specific period, medium, or method. It can be translated as 'through', 'throughout', or 'during' in English. It is formed by attaching 'を通じて' to a noun that represents time, place, or method.

examples.ja: インターネットを通じて友達と連絡を取ります。
examples.romaji: Intaanetto wo tsuujite tomodachi to renraku wo torimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I keep in touch with my friends through the internet.

examples.ja: 彼は一日を通じて熱心に働いています。
examples.romaji: Kare wa ichinichi wo tsuujite nesshin ni hataraite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: He works diligently throughout the day.

examples.ja: このイベントは週末を通じて開催されます。
examples.romaji: Kono ibento wa shuumatsu wo tsuujite kaisai saremasu.
examples.meaningEn: This event will take place throughout the weekend.

examples.ja: その花は春を通じて咲いています。
examples.romaji: Sono hana wa haru wo tsuujite saiteimasu.
examples.meaningEn: The flowers bloom throughout the spring.

title: ～を頼りに (〜wo tayori ni)
formation: Noun + を頼りに
shortExplanationEn: To rely on or depend on something or someone.
longExplanationEn: The ～を頼りに grammar point is used to express that someone is relying on or depending on something or someone. It can be translated as 'to rely on', 'to depend on', or 'counting on' in English. This phrase is used with nouns (people or objects) that act as a source of support or help.

examples.ja: 彼女は地図を頼りに旅行をしている。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa chizu wo tayori ni ryokou wo shite iru.
examples.meaningEn: She is traveling relying on a map.

examples.ja: 多くの人が彼の力を頼りに生活している。
examples.romaji: Ooku no hito ga kare no chikara wo tayori ni seikatsu shite iru.
examples.meaningEn: Many people are living depending on his strength.

examples.ja: このプロジェクトでは、君のスキルを頼りにしています。
examples.romaji: Kono purojekuto de wa, kimi no sukiru wo tayori ni shiteimasu.
examples.meaningEn: In this project, we are counting on your skills.

examples.ja: 彼は友達の助けを頼りに問題を解決しようとした。
examples.romaji: Kare wa tomodachi no tasuke wo tayori ni mondai wo kaiketsu shiyou to shita.
examples.meaningEn: He tried to solve the problem relying on his friend's help.

title: ～一方 (〜ippou)
formation: Verb-casual + 一方(で), い-Adjective + 一方(で), な-Adjective + な/である一方(で), Noun + である一方(で)
shortExplanationEn: Indicates that while one action or situation is happening, another action or situation is also occurring or is in contrast.
longExplanationEn: The ～一方 grammar point is used to express that while one action or situation is taking place, another is occurring at the same time or in contrast. The clauses connected by 一方 often show simultaneous actions, opposing characteristics, or parallel developments.

examples.ja: 技術が進化する一方、生活が忙しくなっている。
examples.romaji: Gijutsu ga shinka suru ippou, seikatsu ga isogashiku natte iru.
examples.meaningEn: While technology advances, life is getting busier.

examples.ja: 日本語が上達する一方、英語力が下がっている気がする。
examples.romaji: Nihongo ga joutatsu suru ippou, eigo-ryoku ga sagatte iru ki ga suru.
examples.meaningEn: While my Japanese improves, I feel like my English ability is declining.

examples.ja: この料理は美味しい一方、ちょっと高いです。
examples.romaji: Kono ryouri wa oishii ippou, chotto takai desu.
examples.meaningEn: While this dish is delicious, it’s a bit expensive.

examples.ja: 彼は優しい一方、厳しい面もある。
examples.romaji: Kare wa yasashii ippou, kibishii men mo aru.
examples.meaningEn: While he is kind, he also has a strict side.

title: ～一方だ (〜ippou da)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + 一方だ
shortExplanationEn: Expresses that a situation or condition is continuing to increase or decrease in one direction.
longExplanationEn: The ～一方だ grammar point is used when something is trending in a single direction over time, often increasing or worsening. It emphasizes that the tendency keeps going in one direction.

examples.ja: 世界の人口は増える一方だ。
examples.romaji: Sekai no jinkou wa fueru ippou da.
examples.meaningEn: The world's population continues to increase.

examples.ja: この町の治安は悪化する一方だ。
examples.romaji: Kono machi no chian wa akka suru ippou da.
examples.meaningEn: Public safety in this town is getting worse and worse.

examples.ja: 僕の日本語は上達する一方だ。
examples.romaji: Boku no nihongo wa joutatsu suru ippou da.
examples.meaningEn: My Japanese keeps improving.

examples.ja: 夏になると、暑さが厳しくなる一方だ。
examples.romaji: Natsu ni naru to, atsusa ga kibishiku naru ippou da.
examples.meaningEn: As summer arrives, it just keeps getting hotter.

title: ～上で (〜ue de)
formation: Verb-ますstem + 上で, Noun + の上で
shortExplanationEn: Used to express that something is done after or on the basis of something else; 'after', 'upon'.
longExplanationEn: The ～上で grammar point is used to indicate that an action or decision is made after another action or condition, or based on certain information. It can be translated as 'after doing something' or 'on the basis of...'.

examples.ja: 調査を行った上で、結論を出しましょう。
examples.romaji: Chousa wo okonatta ue de, ketsuron wo dashimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Let's draw a conclusion after conducting the investigation.

examples.ja: 就職活動を終えた上で、進学するかどうか決めます。
examples.romaji: Shuushoku katsudou wo oeta ue de, shingaku suru ka dou ka kimemasu.
examples.meaningEn: I will decide whether to continue my studies after finishing my job hunting.

examples.ja: 試着した上で、この服を買うかどうか考えます。
examples.romaji: Shichaku shita ue de, kono fuku wo kau ka dou ka kangaemasu.
examples.meaningEn: I'll think about whether to buy these clothes after trying them on.

examples.ja: 経験者の意見を聞いた上で、プロジェクトの方向性を決定しましょう。
examples.romaji: Keiken sha no iken wo kiita ue de, purojekuto no houkousei wo kettei shimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Let's decide the direction of the project after listening to those with experience.

title: ～上に (〜ue ni)
formation: Verb-plain + 上に, い-Adjective + 上に, な-Adjective + な上に, Noun + の上に
shortExplanationEn: Express 'in addition to', 'besides', 'not only... but also'.
longExplanationEn: The ～上に grammar point is used to indicate that there is an additional piece of information or situation besides the first one. It can be translated as 'in addition to', 'besides', or 'not only... but also'. It can be used with verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives, and nouns.

examples.ja: 彼は頭がいい上に、スポーツも得意です。
examples.romaji: Kare wa atama ga ii ue ni, supo-tsu mo tokui desu.
examples.meaningEn: In addition to being smart, he is also good at sports.

examples.ja: このドレスは美しい上に、値段も安いです。
examples.romaji: Kono doresu wa utsukushii ue ni, nedan mo yasui desu.
examples.meaningEn: This dress is not only beautiful but also affordable.

examples.ja: 彼女は優しい上に、料理も上手です。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa yasashii ue ni, ryouri mo jouzu desu.
examples.meaningEn: Besides being kind, she is also good at cooking.

examples.ja: 彼は社長の上に、有名な作家でもある。
examples.romaji: Kare wa shachou no ue ni, yuumei na sakka demo aru.
examples.meaningEn: Not only is he a company president, but he is also a famous writer.

title: ～上は (～ue wa)
formation: Verb-casual + 上は, い-Adjective + 上は, な-Adjective + な上は, Noun + の上は
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'now that', 'since', or 'once'.
longExplanationEn: The ～上は grammar point is used to express that since a certain condition or situation exists, a certain action or result follows. It can be translated as 'now that', 'since', or 'once' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

examples.ja: 試験に合格した上は、パーティーを開こう。
examples.romaji: Shiken ni goukaku shita ue wa, paatii wo hirakou.
examples.meaningEn: Now that I've passed the exam, let's have a party.

examples.ja: この仕事を引き受けた上は、最後までやり遂げるつもりだ。
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto wo hikiuketa ue wa, saigo made yaritogeru tsumori da.
examples.meaningEn: Now that I have taken on this job, I intend to see it through to the end.

examples.ja: 親切だと言われた上は、手伝ってあげよう。
examples.romaji: Shinsetsu da to iwareta ue wa, tetsudatte ageyou.
examples.meaningEn: Since you said I'm kind, I'll help you out.

examples.ja: 甘いものが好きな上は、このデザートをきっと気に入るでしょう。
examples.romaji: Amai mono ga suki na ue wa, kono dezaato wo kitto ki ni iru deshou.
examples.meaningEn: Now that you like sweets, you'll surely enjoy this dessert.

title: ～以上 (〜ijou)
formation: Verb-casual + 以上, い-Adjective + 以上, な-Adjective + な以上, Noun + の以上
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'since' or 'as long as' in a conditional context.
longExplanationEn: The ～以上 grammar point is used to express a condition or requirement, and it can be translated as 'since', 'as long as', or 'if'. It often emphasizes that if a certain condition is met, a certain action or consequence will follow.

examples.ja: 彼は忙しい以上、手伝いに行っても無駄だろう。
examples.romaji: Kare wa isogashii ijou, tetsudai ni itte mo muda darou.
examples.meaningEn: Since he's busy, it would be pointless to try helping him.

examples.ja: 約束した以上、守るべきだ。
examples.romaji: Yakusoku shita ijou, mamoru beki da.
examples.meaningEn: Since I made a promise, I should keep it.

examples.ja: 締め切りが近い以上、全力で仕事をしなければならない。
examples.romaji: Shimekiri ga chikai ijou, zenryoku de shigoto wo shinakereba naranai.
examples.meaningEn: As long as the deadline is near, I have to work with all my might.

examples.ja: 彼女は大学生である以上、勉強が優先されるべきだ。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa daigakusei de aru ijou, benkyou ga yuusen sareru beki da.
examples.meaningEn: Since she's a college student, studying should be her priority.

title: ～以来 (〜irai)
formation: Verb-past + 以来, い-Adjective + 以来, Noun + 以来
shortExplanationEn: Indicates something happening 'since', 'ever since', or 'after' a particular time.
longExplanationEn: The ～以来 grammar point is used to express a period of time that has continued uninterrupted since a certain point in the past. It can be translated as 'since', 'ever since', or 'after'. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, an adjective, or a noun.

examples.ja: 卒業以来、彼女に会っていない。
examples.romaji: Sotsugyou irai, kanojo ni atte inai.
examples.meaningEn: I haven't seen her since we graduated.

examples.ja: 子供が生まれて以来、忙しくなった。
examples.romaji: Kodomo ga umarete irai, isogashiku natta.
examples.meaningEn: I've been busy ever since my child was born.

examples.ja: 東京に引っ越して以来、友達が増えた。
examples.romaji: Toukyou ni hikkoshite irai, tomodachi ga fueta.
examples.meaningEn: I've made more friends since I moved to Tokyo.

examples.ja: あの映画を見た以来、彼女は怖がりになった。
examples.romaji: Ano eiga wo mita irai, kanojo wa kowagari ni natta.
examples.meaningEn: She's been easily frightened ever since she watched that movie.

title: ～切る (〜kiru)
formation: Verb-stem + 切る
shortExplanationEn: To do something completely or thoroughly.
longExplanationEn: The ～切る grammar point is added to the verb stem (masu-form) to express doing something completely or thoroughly, often implying a strong sense of finishing or using up everything.

examples.ja: 彼は試験勉強をがんばり切るつもりだ。
examples.romaji: Kare wa shiken benkyou o ganbari kiru tsumori da.
examples.meaningEn: He intends to give his absolute all to exam study.

examples.ja: この仕事を終わり切るまで、帰らないでください。
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto o owari kiru made, kaeranai de kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Please don't go home until you've completely finished this job.

examples.ja: 友達と喧嘩して、何も言い切れなかった。
examples.romaji: Tomodachi to kenka shite, nanimo iikirenakatta.
examples.meaningEn: I argued with my friend and couldn't say everything I wanted to say.

examples.ja: 彼らは、そのゲームをやり切ることができました。
examples.romaji: Karera wa, sono geemu o yarikiru koto ga dekimashita.
examples.meaningEn: They were able to complete that game thoroughly.

title: ～反面 (〜hanmen)
formation: Verb-casual + 反面, い-Adjective + 反面, な-Adjective + である反面, Noun + である反面
shortExplanationEn: Express contrast between two aspects; 'on the one hand', 'on the other hand'.
longExplanationEn: The ～反面 grammar point is used to show a contrast between two aspects of something. It can be translated as 'on the one hand' or 'on the other hand'.

examples.ja: この車は速い反面、燃費が悪いです。
examples.romaji: Kono kuruma wa hayai hanmen, nenpi ga warui desu.
examples.meaningEn: On the one hand, this car is fast, but on the other hand, it has poor fuel efficiency.

examples.ja: 彼は頭がいい反面、運動が苦手です。
examples.romaji: Kare wa atama ga ii hanmen, undou ga nigate desu.
examples.meaningEn: On the one hand, he is smart, but on the other hand, he's not good at sports.

examples.ja: 彼女は親切である反面、面倒見が良すぎることがある。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa shinsetsu de aru hanmen, mendoumi ga yoi sugiru koto ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: On the one hand, she is kind, but on the other hand, she can be too helpful.

examples.ja: この仕事は給料が高い反面、ストレスがたくさんあります。
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto wa kyuuryou ga takai hanmen, sutoresu ga takusan arimasu.
examples.meaningEn: On the one hand, this job pays well, but on the other hand, it's quite stressful.

title: ～向け (〜muke)
formation: Noun + 向け
shortExplanationEn: Indicating target or intended audience.
longExplanationEn: The ～向け grammar point is used to indicate the target or intended audience for a product, service, or event. It can be translated as 'for', 'aimed at', or 'intended for'.

examples.ja: この雑誌は若者向けです。
examples.romaji: Kono zasshi wa wakamono-muke desu.
examples.meaningEn: This magazine is aimed at young people.

examples.ja: スーパーで家族向けの食品セットが売っています。
examples.romaji: Suupaa de kazoku-muke no shokuhin setto ga utte imasu.
examples.meaningEn: The supermarket sells food sets intended for families.

examples.ja: このアプリは初心者向けに作られています。
examples.romaji: Kono apuri wa shoshinsha-muke ni tsukurarete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: This application is designed for beginners.

examples.ja: このレストランはビーガン向けのメニューが充実しています。
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa bi-gan-muke no menyu ga juujitsu shite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: This restaurant has an extensive menu aimed at vegans.

title: ～恐れがある (〜osore ga aru)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + 恐れがある
shortExplanationEn: Express a concern or fear that something undesirable might occur.
longExplanationEn: The ～恐れがある grammar point indicates that there is a concern or fear that something undesirable or negative might happen. It is often used for potential problems or risks, and can be translated as 'there is a risk' or 'there is a possibility'.

examples.ja: 地震が来る恐れがあるため、避難所に行ってください。
examples.romaji: Jishin ga kuru osore ga aru tame, hinanjo ni itte kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Please go to the evacuation center because there is a risk of an earthquake.

examples.ja: 彼は事故に遭う恐れがあるので、注意して運転してください。
examples.romaji: Kare wa jiko ni au osore ga aru node, chuui shite unten shite kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: There's a possibility he might have an accident, so please drive carefully.

examples.ja: この薬を飲みすぎると、副作用の恐れがある。
examples.romaji: Kono kusuri wo nomisugiru to, fukusayou no osore ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: If you take too much of this medicine, there is a risk of side effects.

examples.ja: 低い予算でこのプロジェクトを進めると、品質が低くなる恐れがある。
examples.romaji: Hikui yosan de kono purojekuto wo susumeru to, hinshitsu ga hikuku naru osore ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: If we proceed with this project on a low budget, the quality may suffer.

title: ～折には (〜ori ni wa)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + 折には, Verb-た form + 折には, い-Adjective + 折には, な-Adjective + な折には, Noun + の折には
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'when' or 'in case of' specific situations or timings.
longExplanationEn: The ～折には grammar point is used to emphasize a specific moment or situation (often not very frequent). It can be translated as 'when (the occasion arises)' or 'in case of'.

examples.ja: 喉が渇く折には、このペットボトルの水を飲んでください。
examples.romaji: Nodo ga kawaku ori ni wa, kono pettobotoru no mizu o nonde kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: When you get thirsty, please drink water from this bottle.

examples.ja: この地域に来る折には、この神社に必ずお参りしましょう。
examples.romaji: Kono chiiki ni kuru ori ni wa, kono jinja ni kanarazu omairi shimashou.
examples.meaningEn: When you visit this area, make sure to go to this shrine.

examples.ja: 彼が元気じゃない折には、具合が悪いか尋ねてください。
examples.romaji: Kare ga genki ja nai ori ni wa, guai ga warui ka tazunete kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: If he doesn't seem well, please ask if he's feeling sick.

examples.ja: チャンスがある折には、躊躇せずに行動しましょう。
examples.romaji: Chansu ga aru ori ni wa, chuucho sezu ni koudou shimashou.
examples.meaningEn: When you have an opportunity, act without hesitation.

title: ～末 (～sue)
formation: Noun + 末
