shortExplanationEn: Express the outcome after a period of time or series of actions; 'after', 'as a result of'.
longExplanationEn: The ～末 grammar point is used to indicate an outcome or result after a period of time, effort, or a series of actions. It can be translated as 'after', 'as a result of', or 'following'. It is typically used with nouns (e.g., '相談の末', '努力の末').

examples.ja: 長い議論の末、ようやく意見がまとまった。
examples.romaji: Nagai giron no sue, youyaku iken ga matomatta.
examples.meaningEn: After a long discussion, we finally reached a consensus.

examples.ja: 一週間の勉強の末、試験に合格した。
examples.romaji: Isshuukan no benkyou no sue, shiken ni goukaku shita.
examples.meaningEn: After a week of studying, I passed the exam.

examples.ja: 何度も失敗の末、成功を手に入れた。
examples.romaji: Nando mo shippai no sue, seikou wo te ni ireta.
examples.meaningEn: After many failures, I finally achieved success.

examples.ja: 彼との別れの末、新しい人生を始めた。
examples.romaji: Kare to no wakare no sue, atarashii jinsei wo hajimeta.
examples.meaningEn: After breaking up with him, I started a new life.

title: ～次第 (〜shidai)
formation: Verb-ますstem + 次第, Noun + 次第
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'as soon as' (when used with Verb-ますstem) or 'depending on' (when used with a noun).
longExplanationEn: The grammar pattern ～次第 can mean 'as soon as' when used with a verb in its ます-stem, and 'depending on' when used with a noun. For instance, 仕事が終わり次第 (as soon as work finishes) or 天気次第 (depending on the weather).

examples.ja: 仕事が終わり次第、帰ります。
examples.romaji: Shigoto ga owari shidai, kaerimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I will go home as soon as I finish work.

examples.ja: お客様のご意見次第で、サービスを改善します。
examples.romaji: Okyakusama no goiken shidai de, saabisu wo kaizen shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: We will improve our services depending on customer feedback.

examples.ja: 彼の返事次第で、パーティーに行くかどうか決めます。
examples.romaji: Kare no henji shidai de, paatii ni iku ka dou ka kimemasu.
examples.meaningEn: I will decide whether or not to go to the party depending on his answer.

examples.ja: 天気次第で、ピクニックを予定しています。
examples.romaji: Tenki shidai de, pikunikku wo yotei shite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: We are planning a picnic depending on the weather.

title: ～次第で (〜shidai de)
formation: Noun + 次第で
shortExplanationEn: Indicates 'depending on; based on' a certain factor or situation.
longExplanationEn: The ～次第で pattern typically follows a noun to show that something is decided or changes 'depending on' that factor. It can be translated as 'based on...' or 'depending on...'.

examples.ja: 仕事の終わり次第で、家に帰る時間が変わります。
examples.romaji: Shigoto no owari shidai de, ie ni kaeru jikan ga kawarimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Depending on when I finish work, the time I go home may change.

examples.ja: 天気次第で、ピクニックに行く予定です。
examples.romaji: Tenki shidai de, pikunikku ni iku yotei desu.
examples.meaningEn: We plan to go on a picnic depending on the weather.

examples.ja: 彼の気分次第で、パーティーに参加するかどうか決まります。
examples.romaji: Kare no kibun shidai de, paatii ni sanka suru ka dou ka kimarimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether he participates in the party depends on his mood.

examples.ja: 会議の進行次第で、決定が変わるかもしれません。
examples.romaji: Kaigi no shinkou shidai de, kettei ga kawaru kamoshiremasen.
examples.meaningEn: The decision may change depending on how the meeting progresses.

title: ～次第です (〜shidai desu)
formation: Verb-ますstem + 次第です (or Noun + 次第です)
shortExplanationEn: A polite/formal way to say 'it depends on...' or 'as soon as...', often used in concluding statements.
longExplanationEn: 「～次第です」 is frequently used in more formal contexts, either to mean that something depends on certain conditions or to indicate an action will happen as soon as something else is completed. It’s also used as a set phrase to conclude an explanation in writing.

examples.ja: 天気次第ですが、明日はピクニックに行こうと思います。
examples.romaji: Tenki shidai desu ga, ashita wa pikunikku ni ikou to omoimasu.
examples.meaningEn: It depends on the weather, but I’m thinking of going for a picnic tomorrow.

examples.ja: 試験の結果次第ですが、留学したいと考えています。
examples.romaji: Shiken no kekka shidai desu ga, ryuugaku shitai to kangaete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: It depends on my exam results, but I'm considering studying abroad.

examples.ja: お客様のご意見次第で新メニューを追加する次第です。
examples.romaji: Okyakusama no goiken shidai de shin menyuu wo tsuika suru shidai desu.
examples.meaningEn: We intend to add a new menu item depending on our customers’ feedback.

examples.ja: 申請が完了次第ですので、手続きが終わったらパスポートを受け取れます。
examples.romaji: Shinsei ga kanryou shidai desu no de, tetsudzuki ga owattara pasupooto wo uketoremasu.
examples.meaningEn: As soon as the application is completed, you'll be able to receive your passport.

title: ～気味 (〜gimi)
formation: Noun + 気味 (e.g., 風邪気味, 寝不足気味, 緊張気味)
shortExplanationEn: Indicates a slight feeling or tendency toward a particular state.
longExplanationEn: The suffix ～気味 generally attaches to nouns (or nominalized words) to express a slight feeling, tendency, or inclination toward a particular state. It often translates to '-ish' or 'a bit' in English (e.g., 'sleep-deprived-ish', 'nervous-ish').

examples.ja: 最近寝不足気味で、ちょっと疲れています。
examples.romaji: Saikin nebusoku gimi de, chotto tsukarete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: Lately, I'm feeling somewhat sleep-deprived and a bit tired.

examples.ja: この部屋は暗い気味だから、もっと明るい照明をつけよう。
examples.romaji: Kono heya wa kurai gimi dakara, motto akarui shoumei wo tsukeyou.
examples.meaningEn: This room is a bit dark, so let's install brighter lighting.

examples.ja: 彼女は緊張気味で話していました。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa kinchou gimi de hanashite imashita.
examples.meaningEn: She was speaking somewhat nervously.

examples.ja: 彼の声は風邪気味だったね。
examples.romaji: Kare no koe wa kaze gimi datta ne.
examples.meaningEn: His voice sounded like he might have a slight cold.

title: ～限り (〜kagiri)
formation: Verb-casual + 限り, い-Adjective + 限り, な-Adjective + な限り, Noun + の限り
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'as long as', 'while', or 'to the extent that' a certain condition holds true.
longExplanationEn: The phrase ～限り is used to indicate that something continues or is true 'as long as' or 'while' a certain condition is met. It can also mean 'to the extent that'.

examples.ja: 元気な限り、運動し続けます。
examples.romaji: Genki na kagiri, undou shi tsuzukemasu.
examples.meaningEn: As long as I'm healthy, I'll keep exercising.

examples.ja: お金がある限り、旅行を楽しみたい。
examples.romaji: Okane ga aru kagiri, ryokou o tanoshimitai.
examples.meaningEn: As long as I have money, I'd like to travel.

examples.ja: 彼が話し続ける限り、このプロジェクトのメリットが伝わるでしょう。
examples.romaji: Kare ga hanashi tsudzukeru kagiri, kono purojekuto no meritto ga tsutawaru deshou.
examples.meaningEn: As long as he keeps talking about it, the benefits of this project will be conveyed.

examples.ja: 勉強する限り、試験に合格できる可能性が高まります。
examples.romaji: Benkyou suru kagiri, shiken ni goukaku dekiru kanousei ga takamarimasu.
examples.meaningEn: As long as you study, you'll have a higher chance of passing the exam.

title: ～際に (〜sai ni)
formation: Verb-casual + 際に, い-Adjective + 際に, な-Adjective + な際に, Noun + の際に
shortExplanationEn: Means 'when', 'while', or 'on the occasion of' a certain event or situation.
longExplanationEn: ～際に is used to specify a time or occasion when something happens. It’s more formal than a simple 'とき' and often appears in announcements or polite instructions.

examples.ja: 出発の際に、パスポートを忘れないでください。
examples.romaji: Shuppatsu no sai ni, pasupooto wo wasurenai de kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: When departing, please don't forget your passport.

examples.ja: 地震が起こる際に、すぐに避難してください。
examples.romaji: Jishin ga okoru sai ni, sugu ni hinan shite kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: When an earthquake occurs, please evacuate immediately.

examples.ja: 日本へ来る際に、お土産をたくさん買うつもりです。
examples.romaji: Nihon e kuru sai ni, omiyage wo takusan kau tsumori desu.
examples.meaningEn: When I come to Japan, I plan to buy lots of souvenirs.

examples.ja: 面接の際に、スーツを着用してください。
examples.romaji: Mensetsu no sai ni, suutsu wo chakuyou shite kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Please wear a suit when attending the interview.

title: A うが B うが (A uga B uga)
formation: Verb-volitional form + うが + Verb-volitional form + うが, or Adjective (-かろう) + が + Adjective (-かろう) + が
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea of 'no matter how... or', 'even if... or'.
longExplanationEn: The ～うが ～うが grammar pattern is used to express that regardless of the conditions in A and B, the result remains the same. Often you’ll see volitional forms or i-adjective '-かろうが' forms. It can be translated as 'no matter how... or...', 'even if... or...'.

examples.ja: 朝早かろうが夜遅かろうが、いつもバスが遅れる。
examples.romaji: Asa hayakarou ga yoru osokarou ga, itsumo basu ga okureru.
examples.meaningEn: No matter whether it's early morning or late at night, the bus is always late.

examples.ja: 彼に話そうが話すまいが、結果は変わらない。
examples.romaji: Kare ni hanasou ga hanasu maiga, kekka wa kawaranai.
examples.meaningEn: Even if I talk to him or don't, the outcome won't change.

examples.ja: この仕事をしようがしまいが、給料は同じだ。
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto o shiyou ga shimai ga, kyuuryou wa onaji da.
examples.meaningEn: Whether I do this job or not, the salary is the same.

examples.ja: あの人に話そうが話すまいが、理解してくれるとは思えない。
examples.romaji: Ano hito ni hanasou ga hanasu maiga, rikai shite kureru to wa omoenai.
examples.meaningEn: I don't think that person would understand whether I talk to them or not.

title: A うと B うと (A uto B uto)
formation: い-adjective (-かろう) + と, な-adjective / Noun + であろうと
shortExplanationEn: Shows that the speaker is indifferent to both conditions; 'whether A or B'.
longExplanationEn: The うと うと pattern indicates that A and B are both possibilities, yet the speaker's stance or the outcome doesn’t change. It's often translated as 'whether A or B'. For i-adjectives, the '-かろうと' form is used. For nouns or な-adjectives, you’ll see 'Noun/な-adjective + であろうと'.

examples.ja: 雨が降ろうと晴れようと、私は毎日ジョギングをします。
examples.romaji: Ame ga furou to hareyou to, watashi wa mainichi jogingu o shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether it's raining or sunny, I jog every day.

examples.ja: 試験が難しかろうと易しかろうと、一生懸命勉強します。
examples.romaji: Shiken ga muzukashikarou to yasashikarou to, isshoukenmei benkyou shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether the exam is difficult or easy, I will study hard.

examples.ja: 彼が学生であろうと社会人であろうと、私は彼を尊敬しています。
examples.romaji: Kare ga gakusei de arou to shakaijin de arou to, watashi wa kare o sonkei shiteimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether he's a student or a working adult, I respect him.

examples.ja: その映画が新しいであろうと古いであろうと、面白ければ観ます。
examples.romaji: Sono eiga ga atarashii de arou to furui de arou to, omoshirokereba mimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether the movie is new or old, if it's interesting, I'll watch it.

title: A かたわら B (A katawara B)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + かたわら, Noun + のかたわら
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'while also', 'at the same time as'.
longExplanationEn: The ～かたわら grammar point is used when someone is doing B while also doing A. It often implies that both tasks require considerable commitment or skill. It can be translated as 'while also' or 'at the same time as'.

examples.ja: 彼は仕事をするかたわら、大学に通っている。
examples.romaji: Kare wa shigoto o suru katawara, daigaku ni kayotte iru.
examples.meaningEn: While working, he also attends university.

examples.ja: 私は学生のかたわら、レストランでアルバイトをしている。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa gakusei no katawara, resutoran de arubaito o shite iru.
examples.meaningEn: While being a student, I work part-time at a restaurant.

examples.ja: 彼女は医者のかたわら、趣味で絵を描いている。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa isha no katawara, shumi de e o kaite iru.
examples.meaningEn: While being a doctor, she paints as a hobby.

examples.ja: 彼は仕事をするかたわら、ボランティア活動にも積極的に参加している。
examples.romaji: Kare wa shigoto o suru katawara, borantia katsudou ni mo sekkyokuteki ni sanka shite iru.
examples.meaningEn: While doing his job, he also actively participates in volunteer work.

title: A かれ B かれ (A kare B kare)
formation: Commonly i-adjective stems + かれ + i-adjective stems + かれ (e.g. 早かれ遅かれ). Also sometimes Noun + かれ + Noun + かれ in older usage.
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'whether A or B'; both situations are included.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point ～AかれBかれ is a somewhat literary/old-fashioned pattern often seen with i-adjectives (e.g., 早かれ遅かれ). It can mean 'whether A or B'. Here it's shown with nouns or verbs, which is also possible in older or set expressions.

examples.ja: 冬かれ夏かれ、毎日ジョギングする。
examples.romaji: Fuyu kare natsu kare, mainichi jogingu suru.
examples.meaningEn: Whether it's winter or summer, I jog every day.

examples.ja: 彼が来るかれ来ないかれ、パーティーは始める。
examples.romaji: Kare ga kuru kare konai kare, paatii wa hajimeru.
examples.meaningEn: Whether he comes or not, the party will start.

examples.ja: 勝つかれ負けるかれ、試合に出ることが大事だ。
examples.romaji: Katsu kare makeru kare, shiai ni deru koto ga daiji da.
examples.meaningEn: Whether we win or lose, it's important to take part in the match.

examples.ja: 雨かれ雪かれ、犬の散歩は欠かさない。
examples.romaji: Ame kare yuki kare, inu no sanpo wa kakasanai.
examples.meaningEn: Whether it's raining or snowing, I never skip walking the dog.

title: A だの B だの (A dano B dano)
formation: Noun/Verb casual + だの + Noun/Verb casual + だの
shortExplanationEn: Used to list examples (often with a critical or complaining tone); 'things like A, B, etc.'.
longExplanationEn: The ～だの～だの grammar point is used to list examples, typically in a casual or complaining tone. It can be translated as 'things like... and so on'.

examples.ja: 彼はいつも遅刻だの、仕事を忘れるだので困る。
examples.romaji: Kare wa itsumo chikoku dano, shigoto o wasureru dano de komaru.
examples.meaningEn: I'm troubled because he's always late, forgets work, and so on.

examples.ja: 彼女は新しい服だの、化粧品だのによくお金を使います。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa atarashii fuku dano, keshouhin dano ni yoku okane o tsukaimasu.
examples.meaningEn: She often spends money on new clothes, cosmetics, and so on.

examples.ja: 彼が言うには、音楽だの映画だの本だのが好きらしい。
examples.romaji: Kare ga iu ni wa, ongaku dano, eiga dano, hon dano ga suki rashii.
examples.meaningEn: He says he likes music, movies, books, and so on.

examples.ja: 子供にはお菓子だの、おもちゃだの、好きなものを限りなく与えてあげたい。
examples.romaji: Kodomo ni wa okashi dano, omocha dano, suki na mono o kagirinaku ataete agetai.
examples.meaningEn: I want to give children all the things they like, such as sweets and toys, without limit.

title: A であれ B であれ (A deare B deare)
formation: Noun A + であれ + Noun B + であれ
shortExplanationEn: Indicates the same statement applies to both A and B; 'whether A or B'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point A であれ B であれ is used to say a statement applies equally whether it's A or B. Often the nouns/conditions are contrasting pairs, and it's translated as 'whether A or B'.

examples.ja: 雨であれ雪であれ、試合は中止にならない。
examples.romaji: Ame deare yuki deare, shiai wa chuushi ni naranai.
examples.meaningEn: Whether it's rain or snow, the match will not be canceled.

examples.ja: 男であれ女であれ、平等に扱われるべきだ。
examples.romaji: Otoko deare onna deare, byoudou ni atsukawareru beki da.
examples.meaningEn: Whether man or woman, everyone should be treated equally.

examples.ja: 豊かであれ貧乏であれ、真の幸福は金では買えない。
examples.romaji: Yutaka deare binbou deare, shin no shiawase wa kane de wa kaenai.
examples.meaningEn: Whether rich or poor, true happiness can't be bought with money.

examples.ja: 経験者であれ初心者であれ、このゲームは楽しめる。
examples.romaji: Keikensha deare shoshinsha deare, kono geemu wa tanoshimeru.
examples.meaningEn: Whether you're a veteran or a beginner, you can enjoy this game.

title: A というか B というか (A to iu ka B to iu ka)
formation: Phrase A + というか + Phrase B + というか
shortExplanationEn: Means 'rather than say A, it's more like B', or 'whether to call it A or B'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar 'A というか B というか' is used when the speaker isn't sure which expression (A or B) describes the situation better. It can be translated as 'rather than A, it's more B' or 'should I call it A or B?'.

examples.ja: 彼は無礼というか、直接的というか、とにかくはっきりしている。
examples.romaji: Kare wa burei to iu ka, chokusetsuteki to iu ka, tonikaku hakkiri shite iru.
examples.meaningEn: He's rather rude, or maybe just direct; in any case, he is very blunt.

examples.ja: この映画は面白いというか、変というか、とにかく不思議な雰囲気がある。
examples.romaji: Kono eiga wa omoshiroi to iu ka, hen to iu ka, tonikaku fushigi na fun'iki ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: This movie is interesting or maybe strange; anyway, it has a mysterious vibe.

examples.ja: 彼の絵は素晴らしいというか、驚くべきというか、本当に見る価値がある。
examples.romaji: Kare no e wa subarashii to iu ka, odoroku beki to iu ka, hontou ni miru kachi ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: His paintings are wonderful, or perhaps astonishing; they're definitely worth seeing.

examples.ja: 彼女の服はかわいいというか、派手というか、とにかく人目を引く。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no fuku wa kawaii to iu ka, hade to iu ka, tonikaku hitome o hiku.
examples.meaningEn: Her outfit is cute or maybe flashy; in any case, it grabs people's attention.

title: A とも B とも (A tomo B tomo)
formation: Noun + とも + Noun + とも (or sometimes Verb forms for each A, B)
shortExplanationEn: Expresses two possibilities; 'either A or B', 'whether A or B'.
longExplanationEn: The 'A とも B とも' grammar pattern indicates two possible scenarios. It can be translated as 'whether A or B' or 'either A or B'.

examples.ja: 決断とも誤りとも、結果は時間が教えてくれるでしょう。
examples.romaji: Ketsudan tomo ayamari tomo, kekka wa jikan ga oshiete kureru deshou.
examples.meaningEn: Whether it's a wise decision or a mistake, time will show the result.

examples.ja: 彼は父とも兄とも、もう何年も話していない。
examples.romaji: Kare wa chichi tomo ani tomo, mou nannen mo hanashite inai.
examples.meaningEn: He hasn't spoken to either his father or his brother in years.

examples.ja: 彼女とも友達とも映画を見に行くつもりです。
examples.romaji: Kanojo tomo tomodachi tomo eiga o mi ni iku tsumori desu.
examples.meaningEn: I plan to go watch a movie either with my girlfriend or a friend.

examples.ja: このゲームには時間とも努力とも言えないほどの根気が必要です。
examples.romaji: Kono geemu ni wa jikan tomo doryoku tomo ienai hodo no konki ga hitsuyou desu.
examples.meaningEn: This game requires a kind of persistence beyond just time or effort.

title: A にしろ B にしろ (A nishiro B nishiro)
formation: Noun A + にしろ + Noun B + にしろ, or Verb-casual A + にしろ + Verb-casual B + にしろ
shortExplanationEn: Means 'whether it is A or B'; 'regardless of A or B'.
longExplanationEn: A にしろ B にしろ is used to indicate that even though A and B are different possibilities, the statement or conclusion applies regardless. Often translated as 'whether it’s A or B' or 'regardless of A or B'.

examples.ja: 暑いにしろ寒いにしろ、毎日運動するべきです。
examples.romaji: Atsui ni shiro samui ni shiro, mainichi undou suru beki desu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether it's hot or cold, you should exercise every day.

examples.ja: 合格するにしろ不合格になるにしろ、最善を尽くしました。
examples.romaji: Goukaku suru ni shiro fukougaku ni naru ni shiro, saizen o tsukushimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Whether I pass or fail, I did my best.

examples.ja: 忙しいにしろ暇にしろ、時間を大切にすべきです。
examples.romaji: Isogashii ni shiro hima ni shiro, jikan o taisetsu ni subeki desu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether you're busy or free, you should value your time.

examples.ja: 大きいにしろ小さいにしろ、その箱は全部ここに運んでください。
examples.romaji: Ookii ni shiro chiisai ni shiro, sono hako wa zenbu koko ni hakonde kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Whether they're large or small, please move all those boxes here.

title: A にせよ B にせよ (A ni seyo B ni seyo)
formation: Noun + にせよ + Noun + にせよ (also works with verbs in dictionary form + にせよ)
shortExplanationEn: Either A or B; Regardless of whether it's A or B.
longExplanationEn: The 〜にせよ 〜にせよ grammar point is used to express two possibilities A and B, indicating that regardless of whether it is A or B, the statement remains valid. It can be translated as 'whether it's A or B.'

examples.ja: 雨にせよ雪にせよ、試合は中止しない。
examples.romaji: Ame ni seyo yuki ni seyo, shiai wa chuushi shinai.
examples.meaningEn: Whether it's rain or snow, the match won't be cancelled.

examples.ja: 彼にせよ彼女にせよ、誰か一人が行くべきだ。
examples.romaji: Kare ni seyo kanojo ni seyo, dareka hitori ga iku beki da.
examples.meaningEn: Whether it's him or her, someone should go.

examples.ja: 成果にせよ努力にせよ、どちらも重要だ。
examples.romaji: Seika ni seyo doryoku ni seyo, dochira mo juuyou da.
examples.meaningEn: Whether it's results or effort, both are important.

examples.ja: 春にせよ秋にせよ、私は花が好きだ。
examples.romaji: Haru ni seyo aki ni seyo, watashi wa hana ga suki da.
examples.meaningEn: Whether it's spring or fall, I like flowers.

title: A につけ B につけ (A ni tsuke B ni tsuke)
formation: Phrase A (dictionary/adjective form) + につけ、Phrase B (dictionary/adjective form) + につけ
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'whether... or...', 'both... and...', 'in either case'.
longExplanationEn: The ～AにつけBにつけ pattern is used to show that some state or action applies in both situation A and situation B. It often pairs contrasting or related conditions and can be translated as 'whether A or B', 'in either case', etc.

examples.ja: 彼には笑うにつけ泣くにつけ、とても魅力的だ。
examples.romaji: Kare ni wa warau ni tsuke naku ni tsuke, totemo miryokuteki da.
examples.meaningEn: He is very charming whether he’s laughing or crying.

examples.ja: 嬉しいにつけ悲しいにつけ、彼はいつも音楽を聴いている。
examples.romaji: Ureshii ni tsuke kanashii ni tsuke, kare wa itsumo ongaku o kiite iru.
examples.meaningEn: Whether he's happy or sad, he's always listening to music.

examples.ja: 日中につけ夜間につけ、この景色は美しい。
examples.romaji: Nitchuu ni tsuke yakan ni tsuke, kono keishiki wa utsukushii.
examples.meaningEn: This view is beautiful whether it's daytime or nighttime.

examples.ja: 喜びにつけ悲しみにつけ、彼女はいつも私を支えてくれる。
examples.romaji: Yorokobi ni tsuke kanashimi ni tsuke, kanojo wa itsumo watashi o sasaete kureru.
examples.meaningEn: Whether I’m joyful or sorrowful, she always supports me.

title: A のやら B のやら (A no yara B no yara)
formation: Noun1 + のやら + Noun2 + のやら (also works with adjectives and verbs + のやら)
shortExplanationEn: Used when the speaker is unsure or confused between multiple possibilities.
longExplanationEn: The A のやら B のやら grammar expresses the speaker's confusion or inability to decide between possibility A and B. It can be translated roughly as 'whether it's A or B (I don't know).'

examples.ja: 彼が怒っているのやら、悲しいのやら、私には分からない。
examples.romaji: Kare ga okotte iru no yara, kanashii no yara, watashi ni wa wakaranai.
examples.meaningEn: I don't know whether he's angry or sad.

examples.ja: 試験の結果が良いのやら悪いのやら、気になって仕方がない。
examples.romaji: Shiken no kekka ga yoi no yara warui no yara, ki ni natte shikata ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: I can’t help worrying whether my exam results are good or bad.

examples.ja: 彼女が私を好きなのやら嫌いなのやら、わからない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga watashi o suki na no yara kirai na no yara, wakaranai.
examples.meaningEn: I don’t know whether she likes me or hates me.

examples.ja: 彼が本当に行くのやら行かないのやら、はっきりしない。
examples.romaji: Kare ga hontou ni iku no yara ikanai no yara, hakkiri shinai.
examples.meaningEn: It’s unclear whether he will really go or not.

title: Noun1 が Noun1 なら、 Noun2 も Noun2 だ (A ga A nara, B mo B da)
formation: Noun1 + が + (repeat Noun1) + なら、Noun2 + も + (repeat Noun2) + だ
shortExplanationEn: Indicates a parallel or matching situation; 'if Noun1 is (truly) Noun1, then Noun2 is also Noun2'.
longExplanationEn: This structure conveys a comparison: 'If Noun1 is indeed that kind of thing (Noun1), then naturally Noun2 is also that kind of thing (Noun2)'. It’s often used to show parallels or equivalences in a rhetorical way.

examples.ja: 鬼が鬼なら、人間も人間だ。
examples.romaji: Oni ga oni nara, ningen mo ningen da.
examples.meaningEn: If demons are indeed demons, humans are likewise humans.

examples.ja: 彼が社長なら、私も社長だ。
examples.romaji: Kare ga shachou nara, watashi mo shachou da.
examples.meaningEn: If he is the president, then I'm the president too.

examples.ja: 貴様が戦士なら、俺も戦士だ。
examples.romaji: Kisama ga senshi nara, ore mo senshi da.
examples.meaningEn: If you're a warrior, then I'm a warrior as well.

examples.ja: ハムスターがペットなら、猫もペットだ。
examples.romaji: Hamusutā ga petto nara, neko mo petto da.
examples.meaningEn: If a hamster counts as a pet, then a cat is also a pet.

title: Noun1 も Noun1 なら、Noun2 も Noun2 だ (A mo A nara, B mo B da)
formation: Noun1 + も + (repeat Noun1) + なら、Noun2 + も + (repeat Noun2) + だ
shortExplanationEn: Highlights two parallel facts or characteristics; 'if it’s said that ~, then it can also be said that ~'.
longExplanationEn: This grammar point sets two items or facts side by side, emphasizing that both follow a similar logic or share a parallel characteristic. Often it can be rendered as 'If you say Noun1 is Noun1, then Noun2 is also Noun2.'

examples.ja: 君も君なら、私も私だ。
examples.romaji: Kimi mo kimi nara, watashi mo watashi da.
examples.meaningEn: If you are you, then I am also me.

examples.ja: 夏も夏なら、冬も冬だ。
examples.romaji: Natsu mo natsu nara, fuyu mo fuyu da.
examples.meaningEn: If it’s said that summer is summer, then winter is also winter.

examples.ja: 東京も東京なら、大阪も大阪だ。
examples.romaji: Toukyou mo toukyou nara, Oosaka mo Oosaka da.
examples.meaningEn: If Tokyo is Tokyo, then Osaka is also Osaka (they each have their distinctive qualities).

examples.ja: 犬も犬なら、猫も猫だ。
examples.romaji: Inu mo inu nara, neko mo neko da.
examples.meaningEn: If dogs are dogs, then cats are also cats.

title: Noun + あっての + Noun (A atte no B)
formation: Noun1 + あっての + Noun2
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'B cannot exist without A'; 'B only exists because of A'.
longExplanationEn: The 'Noun + あっての + Noun' pattern emphasizes that the second noun (B) exists only thanks to, or on the foundation of, the first noun (A). It's often translated as 'B is nothing without A' or 'B exists only because of A'.

examples.ja: 家族あっての私です。
examples.romaji: Kazoku atte no watashi desu.
examples.meaningEn: I am who I am thanks to my family.

examples.ja: お客様あってのビジネスです。
examples.romaji: Okyakusama atte no bijinesu desu.
examples.meaningEn: A business is nothing without its customers.

examples.ja: 練習あっての成功です。
examples.romaji: Renshuu atte no seikou desu.
examples.meaningEn: Success is nothing without practice.

examples.ja: 健康あっての仕事です。
examples.romaji: Kenkou atte no shigoto desu.
examples.meaningEn: Work is nothing without your health.

title: Noun かたがた (Noun kata gata)
formation: Noun + かたがた
shortExplanationEn: Do one thing while also doing another; 'while', 'and at the same time'.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun かたがた」 is a somewhat formal expression for doing one thing while also doing another. The first action is often the main purpose, and the second is done simultaneously or incidentally.

examples.ja: 東京へ出張のかたがた、友人に会いに行った。
examples.romaji: Toukyou e shucchou no kata gata, yuujin ni ai ni itta.
examples.meaningEn: While on a business trip to Tokyo, I also went to see a friend.

examples.ja: 観光のかたがた、お土産を買いました。
examples.romaji: Kankou no kata gata, omiyage o kaimashita.
examples.meaningEn: While sightseeing, I bought some souvenirs.

examples.ja: 散歩のかたがた、ゴミを拾った。
examples.romaji: Sanpo no kata gata, gomi o hirotta.
examples.meaningEn: While taking a walk, I also picked up trash.

examples.ja: 学習のかたがた、音楽を聞いた。
examples.romaji: Gakushuu no kata gata, ongaku o kiita.
examples.meaningEn: While studying, I also listened to music.

title: Noun がてら (Noun gatera)
formation: Noun + がてら
shortExplanationEn: Means 'while...at the same time', 'on the occasion of', or 'along with'.
longExplanationEn: The suffix ～がてら is used to show doing one activity alongside another, taking advantage of the opportunity. It can often be translated as 'while', 'at the same time', 'along with', or 'on the occasion of'.

examples.ja: 散歩がてらに、近くの公園で花を摘みました。
examples.romaji: Sanpo gatera ni, chikaku no kouen de hana o tsumimashita.
examples.meaningEn: While taking a walk, I picked some flowers in the nearby park.

examples.ja: 買い物がてら、友達に会いに行きました。
examples.romaji: Kaimono gatera, tomodachi ni ai ni ikimashita.
examples.meaningEn: While I was out shopping, I went to see a friend.

examples.ja: 彼女は旅行がてら、新しい言語を勉強しました。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa ryokou gatera, atarashii gengo o benkyou shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: She studied a new language while traveling.

examples.ja: ドライブがてら、新しいアルバムを聞きました。
examples.romaji: Doraibu gatera, atarashii arubamu o kikimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I listened to a new album while going for a drive.

title: Noun からある (〜kara aru)
formation: Number + Counter + からある
shortExplanationEn: Used with large numbers or measurements to emphasize 'at least that many' or 'no less than.'
longExplanationEn: 「Noun + Counter + からある」indicates a large or minimum number, emphasizing that the quantity is at least that high (often more). It conveys the idea that the figure is notably large or impressive.

examples.ja: 彼のコレクションは500個からある。
examples.romaji: Kare no korekushon wa gohyaku ko kara aru.
examples.meaningEn: He has at least 500 items in his collection.

examples.ja: このビルは40階からある。
examples.romaji: Kono biru wa yonjuu kai kara aru.
examples.meaningEn: This building has at least 40 floors.

examples.ja: 彼の財産は10億円からある。
examples.romaji: Kare no zaisan wa juu oku en kara aru.
examples.meaningEn: He has assets worth at least one billion yen.

examples.ja: このマラソンは10kmからある。
examples.romaji: Kono marason wa juu kiro kara aru.
examples.meaningEn: This marathon is at least 10 kilometers long.

title: Noun からする (Noun kara suru)
formation: Noun + からする
shortExplanationEn: Used to indicate that something is judged, estimated, or considered from a certain point or aspect.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun + からする」expresses a judgment or estimation based on a particular perspective or aspect. It translates to 'judging from', 'based on', or 'from the viewpoint of' in English, allowing the speaker to form an opinion or conclusion from that point.

examples.ja: 彼の態度からすると、彼はその計画に反対しているようだ。
examples.romaji: Kare no taido kara suru to, kare wa sono keikaku ni hantai shiteiru you da.
examples.meaningEn: Judging from his attitude, he seems to be against the plan.

examples.ja: このレストランの雰囲気からすると、料理もすばらしいはずだ。
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran no fun'iki kara suru to, ryouri mo subarashii hazu da.
examples.meaningEn: Judging from the atmosphere of this restaurant, the food must be wonderful.

examples.ja: 彼女の成績からすると、彼女は非常に勉強熱心な人でしょう。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no seiseki kara suru to, kanojo wa hijou ni benkyou nesshin na hito deshou.
examples.meaningEn: Judging from her grades, she must be a very diligent student.

examples.ja: その会社の成長率からすると、将来的には大きな利益を得られるでしょう。
examples.romaji: Sono kaisha no seicho-ritsu kara suru to, shouraiteki ni wa ooki na rieki o erareru deshou.
examples.meaningEn: From the company's growth rate, it seems likely to gain substantial profits in the future.

title: Noun からの (~kara no)
formation: Noun + からの
shortExplanationEn: Defines the starting point or the origin; 'from'.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun + からの」indicates the place, source, or origin of something. It simply means 'from (Noun)'.

examples.ja: 日本からの手紙を受け取りました。
examples.romaji: Nihon kara no tegami wo uketorimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I received a letter from Japan.

examples.ja: 彼は東京からの旅行者です。
examples.romaji: Kare wa Tokyo kara no ryokousha desu.
examples.meaningEn: He is a traveler from Tokyo.

examples.ja: 昨日、母からの電話がありました。
examples.romaji: Kinou, haha kara no denwa ga arimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Yesterday, I got a call from my mother.

examples.ja: 彼女は大学からの友人です。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa daigaku kara no yuujin desu.
examples.meaningEn: She is a friend from college.

title: Noun から言わせれば (~kara iwasereba)
formation: Noun + から言わせれば
shortExplanationEn: Express the perspective of the noun; 'from ~'s point of view.'
longExplanationEn: 「Noun + から言わせれば」is used to express an opinion or perspective from that noun's point of view. It can translate as 'from ~'s perspective' or 'if ~ had a say...'.

examples.ja: 先生から言わせれば、毎日勉強するべきだ。
examples.romaji: Sensei kara iwasereba, mainichi benkyou suru beki da.
examples.meaningEn: From the teacher's point of view, you should study every day.

examples.ja: 子供たちから言わせれば、もっと遊ぶ時間がほしい。
examples.romaji: Kodomo-tachi kara iwasereba, motto asobu jikan ga hoshii.
examples.meaningEn: From the children's perspective, they want more time to play.

examples.ja: 僕から言わせれば、そのプランは実行可能だ。
examples.romaji: Boku kara iwasereba, sono puran wa jikkou kanou da.
examples.meaningEn: From my point of view, that plan is feasible.

examples.ja: 犬から言わせれば、もっと散歩したいはずだ。
examples.romaji: Inu kara iwasereba, motto sanpo shitai hazu da.
examples.meaningEn: If the dog could talk, it would probably want more walks.

title: Noun + ぐるみ (〜gurumi)
formation: Noun + ぐるみ
shortExplanationEn: Describes 'entirely', 'all together', or 'as a whole group'.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun + ぐるみ」emphasizes that an action or state involves the entire group or everything related to that noun. It can mean 'altogether', 'as a whole', or 'with everyone/everything included.'

examples.ja: 家族ぐるみで旅行に行きました。
examples.romaji: Kazoku gurumi de ryokou ni ikimashita.
examples.meaningEn: We went on a trip with the whole family.

examples.ja: クラスぐるみで遠足に行きました。
examples.romaji: Kurasu gurumi de ensoku ni ikimashita.
examples.meaningEn: The entire class went on an excursion.

examples.ja: この国は国民ぐるみでその法案を支持しています。
examples.romaji: Kono kuni wa kokumin gurumi de sono houan wo shiji shiteimasu.
examples.meaningEn: The entire population of the country supports that bill.

examples.ja: 彼は友人ぐるみでそのクラブに参加した。
examples.romaji: Kare wa yuujin gurumi de sono kurabu ni sanka shita.
examples.meaningEn: He joined the club along with all of his friends.

title: Noun こそあれ (~koso are)
formation: Noun + こそあれ + (result/continuation)
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'though there is ... (some negative or burdensome aspect), still ...'.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun こそあれ」is a somewhat formal expression meaning 'Though there is X, (still) Y'. It acknowledges one (often negative) aspect while asserting that it does not prevent or change another fact or outcome.

examples.ja: 問題こそあれ、プロジェクトは順調に進んでいます。
examples.romaji: Mondai koso are, purojekuto wa junchou ni susunde imasu.
examples.meaningEn: Though there are problems, the project is progressing smoothly.

examples.ja: 多少のリスクこそあれ、挑戦する価値はある。
examples.romaji: Tashou no risuku koso are, chousen suru kachi wa aru.
examples.meaningEn: Though there is some risk, it's worth taking on the challenge.

examples.ja: 欠点こそあれ、彼の提案は興味深い。
examples.romaji: Ketten koso are, kare no teian wa kyoumibukai.
examples.meaningEn: Though it has flaws, his proposal is intriguing.

examples.ja: 不安こそあれ、今が行動するときだ。
examples.romaji: Fuan koso are, ima ga koudou suru toki da.
examples.meaningEn: Though there is anxiety, now is the time to act.

title: Noun こそ～が (~koso~ga)
formation: Noun + こそ + (statement) + が + (contrasting/additional statement)
shortExplanationEn: Emphasizes that 'it is precisely Noun that ...', then adds a contrasting or additional point.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun こそ～が」highlights the importance or uniqueness of the noun. It often translates as 'Yes, Noun is indeed ... , but ...'. The 'が' introduces a second, sometimes contrasting statement.

examples.ja: お金こそ必要だが、それだけが人生のすべてではない。
examples.romaji: Okane koso hitsuyou da ga, sore dake ga jinsei no subete de wa nai.
examples.meaningEn: Money is indeed necessary, but it isn't everything in life.

examples.ja: 努力こそ大事だが、結果も見逃せない。
examples.romaji: Doryoku koso daiji da ga, kekka mo minogasenai.
examples.meaningEn: Effort is certainly crucial, but results also matter.

examples.ja: 健康こそ宝だが、ときには仕事を優先せざるを得ない。
examples.romaji: Kenkou koso takara da ga, toki ni wa shigoto o yuusen sezaru wo enai.
examples.meaningEn: Health is indeed a treasure, but sometimes you have to prioritize work.

examples.ja: 愛こそ力だが、表現するのは難しい。
examples.romaji: Ai koso chikara da ga, hyougen suru no wa muzukashii.
examples.meaningEn: Love is certainly a powerful force, but it's difficult to express.

title: Noun こそすれ (~koso sure)
formation: Noun + こそすれ + (negative or contrasting statement)
shortExplanationEn: An emphatic pattern meaning 'it may be X, but definitely not Y.' Often pairs with a negative statement.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun/Verb-ます形 + こそすれ」emphasizes that while one aspect (X) might exist or be true, another aspect (Y) absolutely does not. It's frequently used with negative expressions (e.g., 〜ない、〜できない) to strongly deny the second possibility.

examples.ja: 感謝こそすれ、非難などできません。
examples.romaji: Kansha koso sure, hinan nado dekimasen.
examples.meaningEn: I might feel grateful, but I could never criticize.

examples.ja: 忙しさこそすれ、退屈することはありません。
examples.romaji: Isogashisa koso sure, taikutsu suru koto wa arimasen.
examples.meaningEn: I may be busy, but I'm never bored.

examples.ja: 後悔こそすれ、忘れることはできない。
examples.romaji: Koukai koso sure, wasureru koto wa dekinai.
examples.meaningEn: I might regret it, but I can't forget it.

examples.ja: 疑問こそすれ、賛成はできない。
examples.romaji: Gimon koso sure, sansei wa dekinai.
examples.meaningEn: I may have doubts, but I cannot agree.

title: Noun ごとき / Noun ごとく (〜gotoki/〜gotoku)
formation: Noun + ごとき, Noun + ごとく, Verb-casual + ごとく, い-Adjective + ごとく
shortExplanationEn: Means 'like a...', 'as if...', expressing a simile or metaphor.
longExplanationEn: 「〜ごとき／〜ごとく」conveys similarity, as if comparing one thing to another. ごとき often modifies nouns, while ごとく can modify verbs or adjectives as well. It's more formal and literary in tone than simple expressions like みたい or よう.

examples.ja: 彼は天使のごとく純粋だ。
examples.romaji: Kare wa tenshi no gotoku junsui da.
examples.meaningEn: He is as pure as an angel.

examples.ja: 悪魔のごとき行為をするな。
examples.romaji: Akuma no gotoki koui o suru na.
examples.meaningEn: Don't engage in acts like those of a devil.

examples.ja: 彼女は雪のごとく白い肌をしている。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa yuki no gotoku shiroi hada o shiteiru.
examples.meaningEn: She has skin as white as snow.

examples.ja: 彼は風のごとく去った。
examples.romaji: Kare wa kaze no gotoku satta.
examples.meaningEn: He left like the wind.

title: Noun じゃあるまいし (~ja aru mai shi)
formation: Noun or Na-Adjective + じゃあるまいし
shortExplanationEn: Expressing that something 'is not'; 'it's not like...'. Colloquial/masculine form of ではあるまいし.
longExplanationEn: The phrase 「Noun (or Na-Adjective) + じゃあるまいし」 is a somewhat casual, sometimes masculine-sounding expression meaning 'it's not like...', 'it's not as if...', or 'there's no need to...' in certain contexts. It dismisses the idea that the noun or adjective is the reason or cause for something.

examples.ja: 彼は子供じゃあるまいし、その問題をしっかりと解決するべきだ。
examples.romaji: Kare wa kodomo ja aru mai shi, sono mondai o shikkari to kaiketsu suru beki da.
examples.meaningEn: He’s not a child, so he should solve that problem properly.

examples.ja: 急ぐ必要はない、時間じゃあるまいし。
examples.romaji: Isogu hitsuyou wa nai, jikan ja aru mai shi.
examples.meaningEn: We don’t need to hurry; it’s not like we’re short of time.

examples.ja: 監督じゃあるまいし、試合の結果について心配するな。
examples.romaji: Kantoku ja aru mai shi, shiai no kekka ni tsuite shinpai suru na.
examples.meaningEn: You’re not the coach, so don’t worry about the game results.

examples.ja: 飴じゃあるまいし、そのまま飲み込んでも大丈夫だよ。
examples.romaji: Ame ja aru mai shi, sono mama nomikonde mo daijoubu da yo.
examples.meaningEn: It’s not candy, so it’s okay to just swallow it.

title: Noun ずくめ (~zukume)
formation: Noun + ずくめ
shortExplanationEn: Denotes 'entirely' or 'nothing but' the preceding noun.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun + ずくめ」expresses that something consists entirely of that noun or is 'nothing but' that noun. It describes a situation with a predominance of one thing.

examples.ja: 彼は最近、失敗ずくめだ。
examples.romaji: Kare wa saikin, shippai zukume da.
examples.meaningEn: He has had nothing but failures recently.

examples.ja: この冬は雪ずくめで大変だ。
examples.romaji: Kono fuyu wa yuki zukume de taihen da.
examples.meaningEn: This winter has been nothing but snow, which is tough.

examples.ja: 彼の話は嘘ずくめだった。
examples.romaji: Kare no hanashi wa uso zukume datta.
examples.meaningEn: His story was full of nothing but lies.

examples.ja: 今日の仕事は忙しさずくめだった。
examples.romaji: Kyou no shigoto wa isogashisa zukume datta.
examples.meaningEn: Today's work was nothing but constant busyness.

title: Noun だけではすまない (Noun dake dewa sumanai)
formation: Noun + だけではすまない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'it can't be settled with just...', 'cannot be resolved merely by...'.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun + だけではすまない」indicates that simply doing or providing the noun is not sufficient to resolve or finish a matter. Something more is required.

examples.ja: この問題は謝罪だけではすまない。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wa shazai dake dewa sumanai.
examples.meaningEn: This problem cannot be settled with just an apology.

examples.ja: 彼女が怒っているから、お菓子をあげただけではすまないよ。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga okotte iru kara, okashi o ageta dake dewa sumanai yo.
examples.meaningEn: She’s angry, so just giving her sweets won't fix it.

examples.ja: このプロジェクトの成功は努力だけではすまない。
examples.romaji: Kono purojekuto no seikou wa doryoku dake dewa sumanai.
examples.meaningEn: Success on this project cannot be achieved with mere effort.

examples.ja: 資金調達だけではすまない、実際のビジネスプランも必要だ。
examples.romaji: Shikin choutatsu dake dewa sumanai, jissai no bijinesu puran mo hitsuyou da.
examples.meaningEn: We can’t just finish with fundraising; we also need a real business plan.

title: Noun たりとも～ない (~tari tomo ~nai)
formation: Noun + たりとも + Negative Verb
shortExplanationEn: Used to show a strong negative: 'not even ~', 'not a single ~'.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun + たりとも + Negative Verb」expresses a strong negation such as 'not even one', 'not even once', or 'none at all'.

examples.ja: 一分たりとも無駄にするな。
examples.romaji: Ippun tari tomo muda ni suru na.
examples.meaningEn: Don’t waste even a single minute.

examples.ja: 彼は一言たりとも話さない。
examples.romaji: Kare wa hitokoto tari tomo hanasanai.
examples.meaningEn: He doesn't speak even a single word.

examples.ja: 彼女は一度たりともその映画を見たことがない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa ichido tari tomo sono eiga o mita koto ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: She has never seen that movie, not even once.

examples.ja: 私は一枚たりともその写真を持っていない。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa ichimai tari tomo sono shashin o motte inai.
examples.meaningEn: I don't have even a single picture of it.

title: Noun たる Noun (~taru~)
formation: Noun1 + たる + Noun2
shortExplanationEn: Indicates 'being in the position/state of', used for roles or statuses.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun1 たる Noun2」expresses that the second noun is in the 'role/state/condition' of the first. It often has a formal or literary tone, emphasizing that someone/something rightly holds or embodies that status.

examples.ja: リーダーたる者は、常に先を見て行動すべきだ。
examples.romaji: Riidaa taru mono wa, tsune ni saki o mite koudou subeki da.
examples.meaningEn: A person who is a leader should always look ahead and act accordingly.

examples.ja: 教師たる者、学生に模範を示すべきである。
examples.romaji: Kyoushi taru mono, gakusei ni mohan o shimesu beki de aru.
examples.meaningEn: Someone who is a teacher should set a good example for the students.

examples.ja: 母たる者、家族を愛するのは当然だ。
examples.romaji: Haha taru mono, kazoku o aisuru no wa touzen da.
examples.meaningEn: If you are a mother, it’s natural to love your family.

examples.ja: 医者たる者、患者の安全を最優先に考えるべきだ。
examples.romaji: Isha taru mono, kanja no anzen o saiyuusen ni kangaeru beki da.
examples.meaningEn: A person who is a doctor should make patient safety their top priority.

title: Noun ですら (~desura)
formation: Noun + ですら
shortExplanationEn: Means 'even', 'as much as', used for emphasis in formal contexts.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun + ですら」emphasizes 'even' in a formal or literary tone. It can carry nuance similar to さえ or すら but is used more in writing or formal speech.

examples.ja: 明日試験があるのに、彼はテキストですら読まない。
examples.romaji: Ashita shiken ga aru noni, kare wa tekisuto desura yomanai.
examples.meaningEn: He doesn’t even read the textbook, despite having a test tomorrow.

examples.ja: この問題は専門家ですら解けない。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wa senmonka desura tokenai.
examples.meaningEn: Even an expert can’t solve this problem.

examples.ja: 彼女は最後のスピーチですら泣いてしまった。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa saigo no supiichi desura naite shimatta.
examples.meaningEn: She ended up crying, even during the final speech.

examples.ja: 彼は毎日勉強しているが、基本の漢字ですら覚えられない。
examples.romaji: Kare wa mainichi benkyou shite iru ga, kihon no kanji desura oboerarenai.
examples.meaningEn: He studies every day, but can’t even remember basic kanji.

title: Noun でなくてなんだろう (〜de nakute nandarou)
formation: Noun + でなくてなんだろう
shortExplanationEn: A strong rhetorical question: 'If not X, then what is it?'
longExplanationEn: 「Noun + でなくてなんだろう」expresses the speaker’s strong conviction that something must be that noun—no other possibility. Literally, 'If it isn't Noun, then what is it?'

examples.ja: これが真実でなくてなんだろう。
examples.romaji: Kore ga shinjitsu de nakute nandarou.
examples.meaningEn: If this isn’t the truth, then what is?

examples.ja: 彼がリーダーでなくてなんだろう。
examples.romaji: Kare ga riidaa de nakute nandarou.
examples.meaningEn: If he isn’t the leader, then who is?

examples.ja: これが愛でなくてなんだろう。
examples.romaji: Kore ga ai de nakute nandarou.
examples.meaningEn: If this isn’t love, then what is?

examples.ja: これが最高のレストランでなくてなんだろう。
examples.romaji: Kore ga saikou no resutoran de nakute nandarou.
examples.meaningEn: If this isn’t the best restaurant, then what is?

title: Noun ではあるまいし (~dewa aru maishi)
formation: Noun + ではあるまいし
shortExplanationEn: Means 'it’s not as if...', 'it’s not that...', more formal than じゃあるまいし.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun + ではあるまいし」indicates that the noun is not the situation or reason being implied. It dismisses a possibility or justification, e.g. 'It’s not as if X, so don’t worry/do that/etc.' This is the more formal counterpart to じゃあるまいし.

examples.ja: 彼女はプロの歌手ではあるまいし、完璧を求めるのは酷だ。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa puro no kashu de wa aru mai shi, kanpeki o motomeru no wa koku da.
examples.meaningEn: She’s not a professional singer, so it’s rather harsh to demand perfection.

examples.ja: 彼は子供ではあるまいし、そんなにチョコレートばかり食べるのはどうかと思う。
examples.romaji: Kare wa kodomo de wa aru mai shi, sonnani chokoreeto bakari taberu no wa douka to omou.
examples.meaningEn: He’s not a child, so I’m not sure about him eating that much chocolate.

examples.ja: 彼は医者ではあるまいし、あなたの健康状態を詮索する必要はない。
examples.romaji: Kare wa isha de wa aru mai shi, anata no kenkou joutai o sensaku suru hitsuyou wa nai.
examples.meaningEn: He’s not a doctor, so he has no need to pry into your health.

examples.ja: この指輪は高価なものではあるまいし、落としても大したことはない。
examples.romaji: Kono yubiwa wa kouka na mono de wa aru mai shi, otoshite mo taishita koto wa nai.
examples.meaningEn: It’s not an expensive ring, so dropping it isn’t a big deal.

title: Noun と Noun を兼ねて (Noun to Noun o kanete)
formation: Noun + と + Noun + を兼ねて
shortExplanationEn: Used to indicate doing two things at once or fulfilling two roles/purposes.
longExplanationEn: 「Noun と Noun を兼ねて」means that one action or thing serves the purpose of both nouns. It can be translated as 'serving as both… and…', or 'for both… and…'.

examples.ja: お父さんは社長と経理部長を兼ねています。
examples.romaji: Otousan wa shachou to keiri buchou o kanete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: My father serves as both the company president and the head of accounting.

examples.ja: 彼女は音楽教師とピアニストを兼ねています。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa ongaku kyoushi to pianisuto o kanete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: She works as both a music teacher and a pianist.

examples.ja: この部屋は寝室と書斎を兼ねています。
examples.romaji: Kono heya wa shinshitsu to shosai o kanete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: This room serves as both a bedroom and a study.

examples.ja: 彼は父と母を兼ねて子どもの世話をしている。
examples.romaji: Kare wa chichi to haha o kanete kodomo no sewa o shite iru.
examples.meaningEn: He looks after his child, taking on both mother and father roles.

title: Noun といい Noun といい (〜to ii〜to ii)
formation: Noun1 + といい + Noun2 + といい + Comment
shortExplanationEn: Used to express 'both A and B'; 'whether it's A or B'; 'not to mention A and also B'.
longExplanationEn: This grammar structure is used to provide two examples or two situations to demonstrate a certain characteristic or situation. It's a way of mentioning two items of the same theme to generalize or emphasize something applicable to them both. In English, it can be understood as 'both A and B', 'whether it's A or B', or 'not to mention A and also B'.

examples.ja: 日本の食べ物といい観光地といい、すべてが素晴らしいです。
examples.romaji: Nihon no tabemono to ii kankouchi to ii, subete ga subarashii desu.
examples.meaningEn: Both Japanese food and tourist spots are all wonderful.

examples.ja: 彼の優しさといい、体力といい、彼は理想的なパートナーだ。
examples.romaji: Kare no yasashisa to ii, tairyoku to ii, kare wa risouteki na paatonaa da.
examples.meaningEn: Whether it's his kindness or stamina, he is an ideal partner.

examples.ja: この町の映画館といい、公園といい、老若男女が楽しめる。
examples.romaji: Kono machi no eigakan to ii, kouen to ii, rounyakunan'nyo ga tanoshimeru.
examples.meaningEn: Not to mention the movie theaters and parks in this town, they are enjoyable for all ages.

examples.ja: 彼の歌唱力といい、ダンスのスキルといい、完璧だ。
examples.romaji: Kare no kashou ryoku to ii, dansu no sukiru to ii, kanpeki da.
examples.meaningEn: Both his singing ability and his dance skills are perfect.

title: Noun という Noun (~to iu~)
formation: Noun1 という Noun2
shortExplanationEn: Used to specify or define one noun with another; 'the noun called', 'the noun that is', or 'a certain'.
longExplanationEn: The Noun という Noun construction is used to specify or define one noun with another. It usually translates as 'the noun called', 'the noun that is' or simply 'a certain'. It is a very common pattern for clarifying what something is named or known as.

examples.ja: 宮崎駿という人は有名なアニメーション監督です。
examples.romaji: Miyazaki Hayao to iu hito wa yuumei na animation kantoku desu.
examples.meaningEn: The person called Hayao Miyazaki is a famous animation director.

examples.ja: その恋人という女性は彼にとても厳しいです。
examples.romaji: Sono koibito to iu josei wa kare ni totemo kibishii desu.
examples.meaningEn: The woman called his girlfriend is very strict with him.

examples.ja: 東京という都市は夜も賑やかです。
examples.romaji: Tokyo to iu toshi wa yoru mo nigiyaka desu.
examples.meaningEn: The city called Tokyo is bustling even at night.

examples.ja: 私は「ハリーポッター」という本が好きです。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa 'Harry Potter' to iu hon ga suki desu.
examples.meaningEn: I like the book called 'Harry Potter'.

title: Noun というところだ (Noun to iu tokoro da)
formation: Noun + というところだ
shortExplanationEn: Means 'it's approximately...' or 'it's about...'. Used to show approximation or a general assessment.
longExplanationEn: The というところだ grammar point is used to show an approximation or general assessment of something. It is often translated as 'it's approximately...', 'around...', or 'it's about...'.

examples.ja: 今回の試験の合格者は半数というところだろう。
examples.romaji: Konkai no shiken no goukakusha wa hansuu to iu tokoro darou.
examples.meaningEn: The passers in this exam would be approximately half.

examples.ja: 彼の力は80％というところだ。
examples.romaji: Kare no chikara wa hachijuu paasento to iu tokoro da.
examples.meaningEn: His strength is about 80%.

examples.ja: このプランに賛成の人は7割というところだ。
examples.romaji: Kono puran ni sansei no hito wa nanawari to iu tokoro da.
examples.meaningEn: About 70% of people agree with this plan.

examples.ja: 課長の意見には、私としては90％というところでしょう。
examples.romaji: Kachou no iken ni wa, watashi toshite wa kyuujuu paasento to iu tokoro deshou.
examples.meaningEn: As for the section manager's opinion, I'd say I'm about 90% in agreement.

title: Noun + というもの (~ to iu mono)
formation: Noun + というもの
shortExplanationEn: Used to express a generic concept: 'all ~', 'any ~ of that kind'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar pattern 'Noun + というもの' refers to the entirety or general nature of that noun—often implying 'all of this category' or 'this thing called...'. It can also highlight a universal truth about the noun mentioned.

examples.ja: 子供というものは、毎日たくさん遊びます。
examples.romaji: Kodomo to iu mono wa, mainichi takusan asobimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Children, as a group, play a lot every day.

examples.ja: 大学生というものは研究に時間をたくさんかけます。
examples.romaji: Daigakusei to iu mono wa kenkyuu ni jikan o takusan kakemasu.
examples.meaningEn: University students spend a lot of time on research.

examples.ja: 日本文化というものは伝統的なものと新しいものが混ざっています。
examples.romaji: Nihon bunka to iu mono wa dentouteki na mono to atarashii mono ga mazatte imasu.
examples.meaningEn: Japanese culture is a mixture of traditional and new.

examples.ja: 別れというものはいつも悲しいものです。
examples.romaji: Wakare to iu mono wa itsumo kanashii mono desu.
examples.meaningEn: Goodbyes are always sad.

title: Noun といったところだ (Noun to itta tokoro da)
formation: Noun + といったところだ
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'at most', 'as much as', or 'limited to'.
longExplanationEn: Noun といったところだ describes an upper limit or a maximum extent, often translating to 'at most' or 'around...'. It suggests the speaker doesn’t expect anything beyond that limit.

examples.ja: 彼の英語レベルは初級といったところだ。
examples.romaji: Kare no eigo reberu wa shokyuu to itta tokoro da.
examples.meaningEn: His English level is at most beginner level.

examples.ja: 私の日本語の語彙は1000語といったところだ。
examples.romaji: Watashi no nihongo no goi wa sen go to itta tokoro da.
examples.meaningEn: My Japanese vocabulary is limited to about 1000 words.

examples.ja: 彼が毎日ランニングできるのは5キロといったところだ。
examples.romaji: Kare ga mainichi ranningu dekiru no wa go kiro to itta tokoro da.
examples.meaningEn: He can run as much as 5 kilometers a day at most.

examples.ja: その会社の従業員は20人といったところだ。
examples.romaji: Sono kaisha no juugyouin wa nijuu nin to itta tokoro da.
examples.meaningEn: The employees at that company are limited to about 20 people.

title: Noun といわず Noun といわず (A to iwazu B to iwazu)
formation: Noun1 + といわず + Noun2 + といわず + (rest of the sentence)
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'both A and B' or 'be it A or B'; applies to both without distinction.
longExplanationEn: The grammar pattern AといわずBといわず means 'be it A or B' or 'both A and B.' It emphasizes that something applies widely, without distinguishing between A and B.

examples.ja: 冬といわず夏といわず、彼はいつもアイスクリームを食べます。
examples.romaji: Fuyu to iwazu natsu to iwazu, kare wa itsumo aisukuriimu o tabemasu.
examples.meaningEn: Be it winter or summer, he always eats ice cream.

examples.ja: 老いといわず若きといわず、全ての人がそのイベントを楽しめます。
examples.romaji: Oi to iwazu wakaki to iwazu, subete no hito ga sono ibento o tanoshimemasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether old or young, everyone can enjoy that event.

examples.ja: 男といわず女といわず、誰もがこの映画を楽しむでしょう。
examples.romaji: Otoko to iwazu onna to iwazu, daremo ga kono eiga o tanoshimu deshou.
examples.meaningEn: Be it men or women, everyone will enjoy this movie.

examples.ja: 月といわず火といわず、あの店は毎日混んでいます。
examples.romaji: Getsu to iwazu ka to iwazu, ano mise wa mainichi konde imasu.
examples.meaningEn: Be it Monday or Tuesday, that shop is busy every day.

title: Noun + ときたら (〜tokitara)
formation: Noun + ときたら
shortExplanationEn: Expresses strong feelings about a topic; 'speaking of', 'when it comes to'.
longExplanationEn: Noun + ときたら emphasizes strong feelings—often frustration, admiration, or surprise—about that noun. It can translate as 'when it comes to...', 'speaking of...', or 'honestly, about...'.

examples.ja: 寿司ときたら、彼は何も食べられない。
examples.romaji: Sushi tokitara, kare wa nanimo taberarenai.
examples.meaningEn: When it comes to sushi, he can't eat anything.

examples.ja: 数学の問題ときたら、彼女はいつも困っている。
examples.romaji: Suugaku no mondai tokitara, kanojo wa itsumo komatte iru.
examples.meaningEn: When it comes to math problems, she's always struggling.

examples.ja: あの店のハンバーガーときたら、本当に美味しい。
examples.romaji: Ano mise no hanbaagaa tokitara, hontou ni oishii.
examples.meaningEn: Speaking of that shop's hamburgers, they're really delicious.

examples.ja: 彼のいつもの態度ときたら、本当に困ったものだ。
examples.romaji: Kare no itsumo no taido tokitara, hontou ni komatta mono da.
examples.meaningEn: Speaking of his usual attitude, it's truly problematic.

title: Noun とは比べものにならない (~to wa kurabemono ni naranai)
formation: Noun + とは比べものにならない
shortExplanationEn: Indicates something is not comparable; 'beyond comparison'.
longExplanationEn: Use とは比べものにならない to emphasize that two things differ so greatly they cannot be compared—one is vastly superior or different in scale, quality, etc.

examples.ja: 新しい車の速さは古い車とは比べものにならない。
examples.romaji: Atarashii kuruma no hayasa wa furui kuruma to wa kurabemono ni naranai.
examples.meaningEn: The speed of the new car is not comparable to the old one.

examples.ja: 彼の英語のうまさは私とは比べものにならない。
examples.romaji: Kare no eigo no umasa wa watashi to wa kurabemono ni naranai.
examples.meaningEn: His English skills are beyond comparison with mine.

examples.ja: 日本の技術は他の国とは比べものにならない。
examples.romaji: Nihon no gijutsu wa hoka no kuni to wa kurabemono ni naranai.
examples.meaningEn: Japan's technology is not comparable to that of other countries.

examples.ja: 今回の地震の被害は前回とは比べものにならない。
examples.romaji: Konkai no jishin no higai wa zenkai to wa kurabemono ni naranai.
examples.meaningEn: The damage from this earthquake is incomparable to the previous one.

title: Noun + ともあろう + Noun (~tomoarou~)
formation: Noun + ともあろう + Noun
shortExplanationEn: Used to criticize or question unbecoming behavior by someone of a certain status.
longExplanationEn: Noun + ともあろう + Noun is used to express that someone with a certain role or status has done something unbefitting their position. It often carries disapproval or surprise: 'For someone who is supposed to be X, how could they...?'

examples.ja: 先生ともあろう者が、そんな間違いをするなんて。
examples.romaji: Sensei tomoarou mono ga, sonna machigai o suru nante.
examples.meaningEn: For a teacher, it's shocking to make such a mistake.

examples.ja: 大人ともあろう者が子供みたいにふるまってはいけない。
examples.romaji: Otona tomoarou mono ga kodomo mitai ni furumatte wa ikenai.
examples.meaningEn: It's not right for an adult to behave like a child.

examples.ja: 社長ともあろう人がそんな失礼なことを言うなんて。
examples.romaji: Shachou tomoarou hito ga sonna shitsurei na koto o iu nante.
examples.meaningEn: It's shocking that someone who is the company president would say such a rude thing.

examples.ja: プロの選手ともあろう者が、試合に遅刻するなんて。
examples.romaji: Puro no senshu tomoarou mono ga, shiai ni chikoku suru nante.
examples.meaningEn: For a professional athlete, being late to a match is unthinkable.

title: Noun ともなると (〜to mo naru to)
formation: Noun + ともなると
shortExplanationEn: 'When it comes to...', emphasizes a change of situation or status.
longExplanationEn: Noun + ともなると indicates a change of state or circumstances once that noun becomes relevant or once one attains that position. It conveys that 'once it’s X, things change' or 'when it comes to X...'.

examples.ja: クリスマスともなると、街中がきらきらと輝いて見えます。
examples.romaji: Kurisumasu to mo naru to, machijuu ga kirakira to kagayaite miemasu.
examples.meaningEn: When it comes to Christmas time, the whole city sparkles.

examples.ja: 子供が自分の過ちを認めるともなると、話は別ですね。
examples.romaji: Kodomo ga jibun no ayamachi o mitomeru to mo naru to, hanashi wa betsu desu ne.
examples.meaningEn: When a child admits their mistake, that’s a different story.

examples.ja: 夏休みともなると、海辺は観光客で溢れかえります。
examples.romaji: Natsu yasumi to mo naru to, umibe wa kankoukyaku de afurekaerimasu.
examples.meaningEn: When summer vacation comes, the seaside is filled with tourists.

examples.ja: 試験週間ともなると、図書館はいつもより混雑します。
examples.romaji: Shiken shuukan to mo naru to, toshokan wa itsumo yori konzatsu shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: When exam week arrives, the library gets more crowded than usual.

title: Noun ともなれば (〜to mo nareba)
formation: Noun + ともなれば
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'if it comes to', 'when', or 'once'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point ～ともなれば is used to stress the likelihood of a change or consequence that comes about due to the situation expressed before ともなれば. It can be translated as 'when it comes to', 'once', or 'if it comes to' in English. It's usually used with nouns.

examples.ja: 試験ともなれば、大抵の人が緊張するものです。
examples.romaji: Shiken to mo nareba, taitei no hito ga kinchou suru mono desu.
examples.meaningEn: When it comes to exams, most people get nervous.

examples.ja: 彼がリーダーともなれば、チームの性能はさらに向上するでしょう。
examples.romaji: Kare ga riidaa to mo nareba, chiimu no seinou wa sara ni koujou suru deshou.
examples.meaningEn: If he becomes the leader, the team's performance will improve even more.

examples.ja: 夏休みともなれば、観光地はいつも人であふれている。
examples.romaji: Natsuyasumi to mo nareba, kankouchi wa itsumo hito de afurete iru.
examples.meaningEn: When it comes to summer vacation, tourist spots are always packed with people.

examples.ja: 結婚ともなれば、ただ二人が愛し合っているだけではすまされない事情も多い。
examples.romaji: Kekkon to mo nareba, tada futari ga aishiatte iru dake de wa sumasarenai jijou mo oi.
examples.meaningEn: When it comes to marriage, there are often issues that can’t be solved by love alone.

title: Noun と相まって (~ to aimatte)
formation: Noun + と相まって
shortExplanationEn: Used to express 'in combination with', 'together with', 'along with'.
longExplanationEn: The Noun と相まって grammar point is used to indicate that something happens or is the result of two or more factors working together. It can be translated as 'in combination with', 'together with' or 'along with' in English. It is used with nouns.

examples.ja: 天気の良さと相まって、ピクニックは大成功でした。
examples.romaji: Tenki no yosa to aimatte, pikunikku wa daiseikou deshita.
examples.meaningEn: In combination with the good weather, the picnic was a great success.

examples.ja: 努力と運と相まって、彼は試験に合格しました。
examples.romaji: Doryoku to un to aimatte, kare wa shiken ni goukaku shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Thanks to both effort and luck, he passed the exam.

examples.ja: ひまわり油とガーリックと相まって、この料理は美味しいです。
examples.romaji: Himawari abura to gaarikku to aimatte, kono ryouri wa oishii desu.
examples.meaningEn: Combined with sunflower oil and garlic, this dish is delicious.

examples.ja: 新しい技術と経験と相まって、私たちはこの問題を克服できました。
examples.romaji: Atarashii gijutsu to keiken to aimatte, watashitachi wa kono mondai o kokufuku dekimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Together with new technology and experience, we were able to overcome this problem.

title: Noun なくして～はない (Noun nakushite ~ wa nai)
formation: Noun + なくして + Sentence ～はない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'without ~, there can't be ~'; 'can't do ~ without first doing ~'.
longExplanationEn: The なくして grammar point is used to indicate that something is indispensable or necessary for something else to occur. 'Without' the first noun, the second part 'cannot exist/occur'. It demonstrates the principle of causality or necessity.

examples.ja: 努力なくして成功はない。
examples.romaji: Doryoku nakushite seikou wa nai.
examples.meaningEn: There is no success without effort.

examples.ja: 時間なくして完璧な作品は作れない。
examples.romaji: Jikan nakushite kanpeki na sakuhin wa tsukurenai.
examples.meaningEn: You cannot create a perfect work without time.

examples.ja: 経験なくして成長はない。
examples.romaji: Keiken nakushite seichou wa nai.
examples.meaningEn: There is no growth without experience.

examples.ja: リーダーシップなくしてグループの成功はない。
examples.romaji: Riidaashippu nakushite guruupu no seikou wa nai.
examples.meaningEn: Without leadership, there can be no success for the group.

title: Noun なしでは～ない (Noun nashi de wa ~nai)
formation: Noun + なしでは～ない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the necessity of something; 'cannot do without', 'not possible without'.
longExplanationEn: Noun なしでは～ない is used to emphasize that something is indispensable. It can be translated as 'cannot do without' or 'not possible without.'

examples.ja: 水なしでは生きられない。
examples.romaji: Mizu nashi de wa ikirarenai.
examples.meaningEn: We cannot live without water.

examples.ja: 彼の助けなしでは、この問題を解決することはできない。
examples.romaji: Kare no tasuke nashi de wa, kono mondai o kaiketsu suru koto wa dekinai.
examples.meaningEn: Without his help, I cannot solve this problem.

examples.ja: コンピュータなしでは、現代のビジネスは成り立たない。
examples.romaji: Konpyuuta nashi de wa, gendai no bijinesu wa naritatanai.
examples.meaningEn: Modern business cannot function without computers.

examples.ja: 友情なしでは、人生は退屈だろう。
examples.romaji: Yuujou nashi de wa, jinsei wa taikutsu darou.
examples.meaningEn: Life would be boring without friendship.

title: Noun なしには～ない (Noun nashi ni wa ~nai)
formation: Noun + なしには + Verb-negative
shortExplanationEn: Means 'without', 'cannot do without', 'not possible without'.
longExplanationEn: The なしには～ない grammar point is used to express that without the mentioned noun, something is not possible. It can be translated as 'cannot (do) without ~', or 'not possible without ~'.

examples.ja: お金なしには生活ができない。
examples.romaji: Okane nashi ni wa seikatsu ga dekinai.
examples.meaningEn: I cannot get by without money.

examples.ja: 愛情なしには子育てができない。
examples.romaji: Aijou nashi ni wa kosodate ga dekinai.
examples.meaningEn: You cannot raise a child without love.

examples.ja: 彼なしにはこのプロジェクトを進められない。
examples.romaji: Kare nashi ni wa kono purojekuto o susumerarenai.
examples.meaningEn: Without him, I cannot move this project forward.

examples.ja: 水なしには生きていけない。
examples.romaji: Mizu nashi ni wa ikite ikenai.
examples.meaningEn: I cannot live without water.

title: Noun ならいざ知らず (~nara izashirazu)
formation: Noun1 + ならいざ知らず + Noun2 (comment)
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'maybe X is possible, but Y is definitely not'. Often 'let alone...'.
longExplanationEn: 「Xならいざ知らず、Yは…」 emphasizes that while X might be possible or acceptable, Y is out of the question or even more difficult. It can be translated as 'I could understand X, but Y is impossible' or 'Maybe X, but definitely not Y.'

examples.ja: 数学ならいざ知らず、物理なんて全然わからない。
examples.romaji: Suugaku nara iza shirazu, butsuri nante zenzen wakaranai.
examples.meaningEn: Math might be manageable, but as for physics, I'm completely clueless.

examples.ja: 5キロならいざ知らず、10キロなんて走れません。
examples.romaji: Go kiro nara iza shirazu, juu kiro nante hashiremasen.
examples.meaningEn: Maybe I could run 5 kilometers, but 10 kilometers is out of the question.

examples.ja: 日本語ならいざ知らず、中国語なんて話せない。
examples.romaji: Nihongo nara iza shirazu, chuugokugo nante hanasenai.
examples.meaningEn: I might be able to speak Japanese, but Chinese is impossible for me.

examples.ja: 冷たい水ならいざ知らず、熱い水で泳ぐなんて考えたこともない。
examples.romaji: Tsumetai mizu nara iza shirazu, atsui mizu de oyogu nante kangaeta koto mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: Maybe cold water, but swimming in hot water never even crossed my mind.

title: Noun + ならでは (~nara de wa)
formation: Noun + ならでは
shortExplanationEn: Expresses something unique or characteristic only of that noun; 'only found in', 'only possible with'.
longExplanationEn: The pattern Noun + ならでは highlights something that is unique or distinctive to that noun, suggesting that only this place, person, or thing could possess or create it.

examples.ja: これは日本ならではの風景ですね。
examples.romaji: Kore wa Nihon nara dewa no fuukei desu ne.
examples.meaningEn: This is a landscape you can only find in Japan.

examples.ja: 新鮮な魚はこの町ならではの特産品です。
examples.romaji: Shinsen na sakana wa kono machi nara dewa no tokusanhin desu.
examples.meaningEn: Fresh fish is a specialty unique to this town.

examples.ja: 彼の技術は彼ならではのものだ。
examples.romaji: Kare no gijutsu wa kare nara dewa no mono da.
examples.meaningEn: His skills are unique to him.

examples.ja: この手作りの一品は、彼女ならではのものです。
examples.romaji: Kono tezukuri no ippin wa, kanojo nara dewa no mono desu.
examples.meaningEn: This handmade item is something only she could produce.

title: Noun なり Noun なり (A nari B nari)
formation: Noun + なり + Noun + なり
shortExplanationEn: Lists alternatives of the same kind; 'either A or B', 'A or B, for example'.
longExplanationEn: The pattern AなりBなり is used to suggest alternatives or options, usually of the same category. It can be translated as 'whether A or B' or 'A or B, for example'.

examples.ja: 映画なり、音楽なり、何か趣味はありますか。
examples.romaji: Eiga nari, ongaku nari, nanika shumi wa arimasu ka?
examples.meaningEn: Do you have any hobbies, such as movies or music?

examples.ja: 紅茶なり、コーヒーなり、何か飲み物をください。
examples.romaji: Koucha nari, koohii nari, nanika nomimono o kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Please give me something to drink, like tea or coffee.

examples.ja: パンなり、おにぎりなり、何か食べてください。
examples.romaji: Pan nari, onigiri nari, nanika tabete kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Please eat something—bread, onigiri, or whatever you’d like.

examples.ja: タクシーなり、バスなりに乗って来てください。
examples.romaji: Takushii nari, basu nari ni notte kite kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Please come by either taxi or bus.

title: Noun なりとも (~nari tomo)
formation: Noun + なりとも
shortExplanationEn: Indicates even a small amount or degree; 'at least', 'even a little'.
longExplanationEn: Noun + なりとも is used to show that even a small amount or degree is valuable or needed. It translates as 'at least' or 'even a little' in English.

examples.ja: 時間が限られているので、一時間なりとも練習すべきだ。
examples.romaji: Jikan ga kagirarete iru node, ichi-jikan nari tomo renshuu subeki da.
examples.meaningEn: Since time is limited, you should practice for at least an hour.

examples.ja: 彼には一言なりとも謝罪の言葉を聞きたい。
examples.romaji: Kare ni wa hitokoto nari tomo shazai no kotoba o kikitai.
examples.meaningEn: I’d like to hear at least a word of apology from him.

examples.ja: このプロジェクトには、一人なりともメンバーが必要だ。
examples.romaji: Kono purojekuto ni wa, hitori nari tomo membaa ga hitsuyou da.
examples.meaningEn: We need at least one member for this project.

examples.ja: あなたには、一言なりとも感謝の言葉を述べたい。
examples.romaji: Anata ni wa, hitokoto nari tomo kansha no kotoba o nobetai.
examples.meaningEn: I want to offer at least a word of gratitude to you.

title: Noun に Noun を重ねて (A ni B wo kasanete)
formation: Noun に Noun を重ねて
shortExplanationEn: This expression is used to describe repeated actions or states, 'one after another' or 'pile up'.
longExplanationEn: The structure Noun に Noun を重ねて is used to indicate the repetition of actions or states. In English, this could be translated as 'one after another' or 'pile up'. It's typically used to emphasize a great deal of continuation or accumulation of something.

examples.ja: 彼女は失敗に失敗を重ねて、とうとう会社を辞めた。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa shippai ni shippai wo kasanete, toutou kaisha wo yameta.
examples.meaningEn: She piled up failures upon failures and finally quit the company.

examples.ja: 仕事に仕事を重ねて、彼は疲れ果てた。
examples.romaji: Shigoto ni shigoto wo kasanete, kare wa tsukarehateta.
examples.meaningEn: He piled up work upon work and was completely exhausted.

examples.ja: 彼は試験に不合格になり、失望に失望を重ねていた。
examples.romaji: Kare wa shiken ni fugōkaku ni nari, shitsubō ni shitsubō wo kasanete ita.
examples.meaningEn: He failed the exam and kept piling up his disappointments.

examples.ja: 彼は問題に問題を重ねて、とうとう退学になった。
examples.romaji: Kare wa mondai ni mondai wo kasanete, toutou taigaku ni natta.
examples.meaningEn: He kept piling up problems upon problems until he was finally expelled.

title: Noun にあっては (Noun ni atte ha)
formation: Noun + にあっては
shortExplanationEn: Indicates a specific situation or environment in which something happens or is the case.
longExplanationEn: The にあっては grammar point is used after a noun that indicates a specific situation or environment. It expresses the context in which an event takes place, or the circumstances that pertain to the information to be explained or highlighted. It can be translated as 'in', 'at' or 'under' in English.

examples.ja: 戦争時にあっては、すべての人々が困難に直面していました。
examples.romaji: Sensouji ni atte wa, subete no hito ga konnan ni chokumen shiteimashita.
examples.meaningEn: In wartime, all people were facing difficulties.

examples.ja: 経済危機にあっては、多くの企業が倒産の危機に瀕している。
examples.romaji: Keizai kiki ni atte wa, ooku no kigyou ga tousan no kiki ni hinshite iru.
examples.meaningEn: In the economic crisis, many companies are on the verge of bankruptcy.

examples.ja: 不況下にあっては、どの企業も経費削減を迫られている。
examples.romaji: Fukyouka ni atte wa, dono kigyou mo keihi sakugen o semararete iru.
examples.meaningEn: In times of recession, every company is forced to cut costs.

examples.ja: 改革期にあっては、新しいアイデアを大切にする必要がある。
examples.romaji: Kaikaku ki ni atte wa, atarashii aidea o taisetsu ni suru hitsuyou ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: In a period of reform, it's necessary to value new ideas.

title: Noun にあるまじき Noun (Noun ni aru majiki Noun)
formation: Noun1 にあるまじき Noun2
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea of 'unfitting', 'unbecoming' or 'inappropriate'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point Noun にあるまじき Noun is used to express that the noun doesn't fit or is inappropriate in some way for the other noun. It can translate loosely as 'unfitting for', 'unbecoming of' or 'something that must not be for a/ the noun'.

examples.ja: 彼は教師にあるまじき言動をとった。
examples.romaji: Kare wa kyoushi ni aru majiki gendou wo totta.
examples.meaningEn: He took actions that are unbecoming of a teacher.

examples.ja: これは人間にあるまじき行為だ。
examples.romaji: Kore wa ningen ni aru majiki koui da.
examples.meaningEn: This is an act that is unfitting for a human.

examples.ja: 彼女は社長にあるまじき振る舞いをした。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa shachou ni aru majiki furumai wo shita.
examples.meaningEn: She behaved in a way that is unbecoming of a president.

examples.ja: それは社会人にあるまじき行動だ。
examples.romaji: Sore wa shakaijin ni aru majiki koudou da.
examples.meaningEn: That is a behaviour that is not suitable for an adult.

title: Noun にして (Noun ni shite)
formation: Noun + にして
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea 'even for', 'even though', or 'in spite of'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point Noun にして expresses a sense of surprise, disbelief or unexpectedness in response to a situation or circumstance. Generally, it is used when the speaker wants to convey that something is unexpected even for someone (noun) or in their situation.

examples.ja: 彼は医者にしてその基本的な知識がないのが信じられない。
examples.romaji: Kare wa isha ni shite sono kihonteki na chishiki ga nai no ga shinjirarenai.
examples.meaningEn: I can't believe that even as a doctor, he doesn't have that basic knowledge.

examples.ja: 彼は経験豊富な運転手にして、事故を起こした。
examples.romaji: Kare wa keiken houfu na untenshu ni shite, jiko wo okoshita.
examples.meaningEn: Even as an experienced driver, he had an accident.

examples.ja: 彼は成績優秀な生徒にして、その問題が解けなかった。
examples.romaji: Kare wa seiseki yuushuu na seito ni shite, sono mondai ga tokenakatta.
examples.meaningEn: Even as an excellent student, he couldn't solve that problem.

examples.ja: 彼は勉強家にして、その問題を理解できなかった。
examples.romaji: Kare wa benkyouka ni shite, sono mondai o rikai dekinakatta.
examples.meaningEn: Even as a studious person, he could not understand that problem.

title: Noun にして初めて (Noun nishite hajimete)
formation: Noun + にして初めて
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'only when/after... for the first time'.
longExplanationEn: The phrase Noun にして初めて is used to express a sense of realization or experience that only came about or was felt after reaching that particular stage or status. In English, it could be translated as 'only when/after... for the first time'.

examples.ja: 大人にして初めて、親の苦労が分かった。
examples.romaji: Otona ni shite hajimete, oya no kurou ga wakatta.
examples.meaningEn: Only when I became an adult did I understand my parents' struggles for the first time.

examples.ja: 彼は40歳にして初めて、海外旅行を経験した。
examples.romaji: Kare wa yonjuu-sai ni shite hajimete, kaigai ryokou o keiken shita.
examples.meaningEn: Only when he turned 40 did he travel abroad for the first time.

examples.ja: 彼女は親にして初めて、子育ての大変さを理解した。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa oya ni shite hajimete, kosodate no taihensa o rikai shita.
examples.meaningEn: It was only after she became a parent that she realized how challenging raising a child can be.

examples.ja: 社長にして初めて、会社全体の重責を痛感した。
examples.romaji: Shachou ni shite hajimete, kaisha zentai no juuseki o tsuukan shita.
examples.meaningEn: Only when he became president did he feel the full weight of responsibility for the entire company.

title: Noun にすら (〜ni sura)
formation: Noun + にすら
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the concept of 'even'; 'even in', 'even as', 'even to'.
longExplanationEn: The 〜にすら grammar point is used to show surprise that an action or situation applies even to the noun in question. It can be translated into English as 'even in', 'even as', or 'even to'. It's often used with negative or unexpected statements, emphasizing that 'even' in this particular case something can't or doesn't happen.

examples.ja: 彼にすらその問題は解けなかった。
examples.romaji: Kare ni sura sono mondai wa tokenakatta.
examples.meaningEn: Even he could not solve that problem.

examples.ja: 彼女はテストにすら合格しなかった。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa tesuto ni sura goukaku shinakatta.
examples.meaningEn: She did not even pass the test.

examples.ja: この犬は自分の名前にすら反応しない。
examples.romaji: Kono inu wa jibun no namae ni sura hannou shinai.
examples.meaningEn: This dog doesn't even respond to its own name.

examples.ja: 彼にすら話す余裕がなかった。
examples.romaji: Kare ni sura hanasu yoyuu ga nakatta.
examples.meaningEn: I didn't even have the time to talk to him.

title: Noun にとどまらず～も (~ ni todomarazu ~ mo)
formation: Noun + にとどまらず + Noun + も
shortExplanationEn: This expresses the idea of 'not only...but also', 'not stopping at...'.
longExplanationEn: This grammar point is used to express that something is not limited to just one thing (the thing before にとどまらず), but also includes other things (the thing after も). It can be translated as 'not only... but also...' or 'not stopping at...'.

examples.ja: このアプリは、日本語にとどまらず、他の言語もサポートしています。
examples.romaji: Kono apuri wa, nihongo ni todomarazu, hoka no gengo mo sapooto shite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: This app not only supports Japanese, but also other languages.

examples.ja: 彼は、音楽にとどまらず、映画製作にも興味がある。
examples.romaji: Kare wa, ongaku ni todomarazu, eiga seisaku ni mo kyoumi ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: He is not only interested in music, but also in filmmaking.

examples.ja: 彼の影響は、家族にとどまらず、地域全体にも及んでいます。
examples.romaji: Kare no eikyou wa, kazoku ni todomarazu, chiiki zentai ni mo oyonde imasu.
examples.meaningEn: His influence extends not only to his family but also to the entire region.

examples.ja: 彼女の才能は、絵画にとどまらず、文学にも及ぶ。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no sainou wa, kaiga ni todomarazu, bungaku ni mo oyobu.
examples.meaningEn: Her talent extends not only to painting, but also to literature.

title: Noun にひきかえ Noun は (~ni hikikae ~ wa)
formation: Noun 1 + にひきかえ + Noun 2 + は
shortExplanationEn: Represents 'in contrast to', 'as opposed to'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point Noun にひきかえ Noun は is used in Japanese to express contrast or opposition between two nouns. It translates to 'as opposed to' or 'in contrast to' in English. The first noun represents what is being contrasted against and the second noun represents the main subject.

examples.ja: 彼の親切さにひきかえ、彼女は無感情だ。
examples.romaji: Kare no shinsetsusa ni hikikae, kanojo wa mukanjou da.
examples.meaningEn: In contrast to his kindness, she is emotionless.

examples.ja: 夏にひきかえ、冬は雪で寒い。
examples.romaji: Natsu ni hikikae, fuyu wa yuki de samui.
examples.meaningEn: As opposed to summer, winter is cold with snow.

examples.ja: 日本の駅にひきかえ、アメリカの駅は広い。
examples.romaji: Nihon no eki ni hikikae, Amerika no eki wa hiroi.
examples.meaningEn: As opposed to Japanese stations, American stations are wide.

examples.ja: 昔にひきかえ、現代はテクノロジーが進歩した。
examples.romaji: Mukashi ni hikikae, gendai wa tekunorojii ga shinpo shita.
examples.meaningEn: As opposed to the old days, modern times have advanced technology.

title: Noun にもまして (〜ni mo mashite)
formation: Noun + にもまして
shortExplanationEn: This expresses 'Even more than; exceeding'.
longExplanationEn: The Nounにもまして grammar point is used to express that something is even more or exceeds the noun that it’s being compared to. It translates to expressions like 'even more than' or 'exceeding' in English. It is used when making a comparison to emphasize the greater degree of something.

examples.ja: 私は彼にもまして、音楽を愛しています。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa kare ni mo mashite, ongaku o aishiteimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I love music even more than he does.

examples.ja: 彼女は年齢にもまして、経験豊富です。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa nenrei ni mo mashite, keiken houfu desu.
examples.meaningEn: She has even more experience than her age would suggest.

examples.ja: 今日は昨日にもまして寒いです。
examples.romaji: Kyou wa kinou ni mo mashite samui desu.
