examples.meaningEn: It is even colder today than it was yesterday.

examples.ja: このビルは高さにもまして、美しい設計が有名です。
examples.romaji: Kono biru wa takasa ni mo mashite, utsukushii sekkei ga yuumei desu.
examples.meaningEn: This building is famous for its beautiful design even more than its height.

title: Noun によらず (～ni yorazu)
formation: Noun + によらず
shortExplanationEn: Regardless of, irrespective of.
longExplanationEn: The ～によらず grammar point is used to express the meaning 'regardless of' or 'irrespective of' in English. It is used when the outcome does not change regardless of differences in conditions.

examples.ja: 結果は勉強時間によらず変わらないでしょう。
examples.romaji: Kekka wa benkyou jikan ni yorazu kawaranai deshou.
examples.meaningEn: The result will not change regardless of the study time.

examples.ja: 季節によらず、この地域は常に暖かいです。
examples.romaji: Kisetsu ni yorazu, kono chiiki wa tsune ni atatakai desu.
examples.meaningEn: This region is always warm, regardless of the season.

examples.ja: 年齢によらず、皆が楽しめるイベントです。
examples.romaji: Nenrei ni yorazu, mina ga tanoshimeru ibento desu.
examples.meaningEn: It is an event that everyone can enjoy, regardless of age.

examples.ja: 国籍によらず、誰でも参加できます。
examples.romaji: Kokuseki ni yorazu, dare demo sanka dekimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Anyone can participate, regardless of nationality.

title: Noun に先駆けて (〜ni saki gakete)
formation: Noun + に先駆けて
shortExplanationEn: Meaning 'ahead of', 'before', or 'preceding in time'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point 〜に先駆けて indicates something happening ahead or before something else in terms of time. Typically used in written language or formal speech.

examples.ja: この映画は日本に先駆けて、アメリカで公開されました。
examples.romaji: Kono eiga wa Nippon ni sakigakete, Amerika de koukai saremashita.
examples.meaningEn: This film was released in America ahead of Japan.

examples.ja: 彼は周りの人々に先駆けて、その発表を聞いた。
examples.romaji: Kare wa mawari no hitobito ni sakigakete, sono happyou o kiita.
examples.meaningEn: He heard the announcement before the people around him.

examples.ja: ジョンソンさんは他の参加者に先駆けて、会場に到着しました。
examples.romaji: Jonson-san wa hoka no sankasha ni sakigakete, kaijou ni touchaku shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Mr. Johnson arrived at the venue ahead of the other participants.

examples.ja: 彼女は同級生に先駆けて卒業することができました。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa doukyuusei ni sakigakete sotsugyou suru koto ga dekimashita.
examples.meaningEn: She was able to graduate before her classmates.

title: Noun に即した Noun (A ni sokushita B)
formation: Noun1 + に即した + Noun2
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea of 'in accordance with', 'based on'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point 'Noun に即した Noun' is used to express the idea of something being done in accordance with or based on something else. Usually, the first noun is principle, rule, reality, etc. and the second noun could be action, method, plan, etc.

examples.ja: 社会のニーズに即した教育改革が必要だ。
examples.romaji: Shakai no niizu ni sokushita kyouiku kaikaku ga hitsuyou da.
examples.meaningEn: We need an educational reform that is in accordance with the needs of society.

examples.ja: 国際法に即した対応をとるべきだ。
examples.romaji: Kokusaihou ni sokushita taiou wo toru beki da.
examples.meaningEn: We should respond in accordance with international law.

examples.ja: チームのポリシーに即した行動を取りましょう。
examples.romaji: Chiimu no porishii ni sokushita koudou wo torimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Let's take action in accordance with the team's policy.

examples.ja: 現実に即した計画を立てなければならない。
examples.romaji: Genjitsu ni sokushita keikaku wo tatenakereba naranai.
examples.meaningEn: We must make a plan based on reality.

title: Noun に即して Verb (〜ni soku shite ~)
formation: Noun + に即して + Verb
shortExplanationEn: The grammar point Noun に即して Verb is used to mean 'to act in accordance with', 'in line with' or 'based on'.
longExplanationEn: The formation Noun に即して Verb is used when someone is doing something based on or in accordance with some norms, rules, decisions or principles. This structure is often used in business or official contexts.

examples.ja: 会社のポリシーに即して行動しましょう。
examples.romaji: Kaisha no porishii ni soku shite koudou shimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Let's act in accordance with the company's policy.

examples.ja: 法律に即して判断を下すことが重要です。
examples.romaji: Houritsu ni soku shite hantei o kudasu koto ga juuyou desu.
examples.meaningEn: It's important to make judgments in accordance with the law.

examples.ja: 彼の意見に即して、プランを変更しました。
examples.romaji: Kare no iken ni soku shite, puran o henkou shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Based on his opinion, we changed the plan.

examples.ja: ルールに即して行動すれば、問題は起きません。
examples.romaji: Ruuru ni soku shite koudou sureba, mondai wa okimasen.
examples.meaningEn: If we act in accordance with the rules, there will be no problem.

title: Noun に言わせれば (Noun ni iwasereba)
formation: Noun + に言わせれば
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'according to', 'if you ask', 'in the opinion of'.
longExplanationEn: The に言わせれば grammar point is used when we want to express someone's opinion or viewpoint. It can be translated as 'according to', 'if you ask', or 'in the opinion of'. This form is mostly used in formal or written language.

examples.ja: 先生に言わせれば、毎日勉強することが重要だと言う。
examples.romaji: Sensei ni iwasereba, mainichi benkyou suru koto ga juuyou da to iu.
examples.meaningEn: According to the teacher, it's important to study every day.

examples.ja: 彼に言わせれば、早く結婚する方がいいと思っている。
examples.romaji: Kare ni iwasereba, hayaku kekkon suru hou ga ii to omotte iru.
examples.meaningEn: If you ask him, he thinks it's better to get married early.

examples.ja: 評論家に言わせれば、この映画は今年の最高の映画だそうだ。
examples.romaji: Hyouronka ni iwasereba, kono eiga wa kotoshi no saikou no eiga da sou da.
examples.meaningEn: According to the critic, this movie is the best of the year.

examples.ja: 父に言わせれば、海外で働く経験はとても価値がある。
examples.romaji: Chichi ni iwasereba, kaigai de hataraku keiken wa totemo kachi ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: In my father's opinion, the experience of working overseas is very valuable.

title: Noun に限ったことではない (〜ni kagitta koto dewa nai)
formation: Noun + に限ったことではない
shortExplanationEn: Indicates that something is not only limited to; 'not only', 'not just'.
longExplanationEn: This grammar point is used to express that the statement is not only limited to the noun mentioned before it. In English, it can be translated as 'not only', 'not just', or sometimes 'not necessarily'. It underscores that what is being said is also true for other things or situations, not just for the specific noun stated.

examples.ja: この問題は日本に限ったことではない。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wa Nihon ni kagitta koto dewa nai.
examples.meaningEn: This issue is not only limited to Japan.

examples.ja: 英語はアメリカに限ったことではない。
examples.romaji: Eigo wa Amerika ni kagitta koto dewa nai.
examples.meaningEn: English is not just limited to America.

examples.ja: スマホの依存症は若者に限ったことではない。
examples.romaji: Sumaho no izonshou wa wakamono ni kagitta koto dewa nai.
examples.meaningEn: Smartphone addiction is not only limited to young people.

examples.ja: 地球温暖化は我々人間に限ったことではない。
examples.romaji: Chikyuu ondanka wa wareware ningen ni kagitta koto dewa nai.
examples.meaningEn: Global warming is not just limited to us humans.

title: Noun に限ったことでもない (~ni kagitta koto demo nai)
formation: Noun + に限ったことでもない
shortExplanationEn: 'Not limited to'; 'not just', 'not exclusively'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point 'Noun に限ったことでもない' typically expresses that something is not only limited to a specific instance or category. It insinuates that there are also other instances or categories involved. In English, it can be translated as 'not limited to', 'not just', or 'not exclusively'.

examples.ja: この試験が難しいのは、数学に限ったことでもない。
examples.romaji: Kono shiken ga muzukashii no wa, suugaku ni kagitta koto demo nai.
examples.meaningEn: The exam being difficult is not limited to just math.

examples.ja: 彼女が可愛いのは顔だけに限ったことでもない。性格も好きだ。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga kawaii no wa kao dake ni kagitta koto demo nai. Seikaku mo suki da.
examples.meaningEn: Her being cute is not just limited to her face. I also like her personality.

examples.ja: その問題が起こるのは、我々の国に限ったことでもない。
examples.romaji: Sono mondai ga okoru no wa, wareware no kuni ni kagitta koto demo nai.
examples.meaningEn: The occurrence of that problem is not limited to our country.

examples.ja: この作家の本が売れているのは日本に限ったことでもない。
examples.romaji: Kono sakka no hon ga urete iru no wa Nippon ni kagitta koto demo nai.
examples.meaningEn: The selling of this author's books is not limited to just Japan.

title: Noun に限る (~ni kagiru)
formation: Noun + に限る
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea that 'nothing but', 'nothing is better than'.
longExplanationEn: The noun に限る grammar point is used to express the idea that there is nothing better than the noun mentioned in the specific context. It is often used to give personal opinions, advice, or recommendations.

examples.ja: こういう時には家族に話すに限る。
examples.romaji: Kou iu toki ni wa kazoku ni hanasu ni kagiru.
examples.meaningEn: At times like this, there's nothing better than talking to your family.

examples.ja: 寒い冬には温かいお茶に限る。
examples.romaji: Samui fuyu ni wa atatakai ocha ni kagiru.
examples.meaningEn: In cold winter, there's nothing better than warm tea.

examples.ja: 疲れたときは、よい睡眠に限る。
examples.romaji: Tsukareta toki wa, yoi suimin ni kagiru.
examples.meaningEn: When you are tired, nothing is better than a good sleep.

examples.ja: ダイエットするなら、運動に限る。
examples.romaji: Daietto suru nara, undou ni kagiru.
examples.meaningEn: If you want to go on a diet, there is nothing better than exercise.

title: Noun ぬいた Noun (A nuita B)
formation: Noun1 + をぬいた + Noun2
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea of 'taking something out from something else'.
longExplanationEn: This grammar point is used to denote the action of removing or taking out one thing from another. It is usually used with nouns.

examples.ja: このサラダはにんじんをぬいたサラダです。
examples.romaji: Kono sarada wa ninjin o nuita sarada desu.
examples.meaningEn: This is a salad with the carrots removed.

examples.ja: 彼はコーヒーから砂糖をぬいた。
examples.romaji: Kare wa koohii kara satou o nuita.
examples.meaningEn: He removed the sugar from the coffee.

examples.ja: 私は料理から塩をぬいた。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa ryouri kara shio o nuita.
examples.meaningEn: I removed the salt from the cooking.

examples.ja: 彼女は服からシミをぬいた。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa fuku kara shimi o nuita.
examples.meaningEn: She removed the stain from the clothes.

title: Noun + ぬいて（~nuite)
formation: Noun + ぬいて
shortExplanationEn: This is a way to express 'minus' or 'except for' some specific noun.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point Noun + ぬいて is used when you want to exclude a certain noun from the whole or mention something about everything else except for a specific noun. It's usually translated in English as 'minus' or 'except for'.

examples.ja: 私たちは、彼ぬいて全員が集まることができました。
examples.romaji: Watashitachi wa, kare nuite zen'in ga atsumaru koto ga dekimashita.
examples.meaningEn: We were able to gather everyone except for him.

examples.ja: このレストランはお子様メニューをぬいてすべてが美味しい。
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa okosama menyuu o nuite subete ga oishii.
examples.meaningEn: Everything at this restaurant is delicious except for the kids' menu.

examples.ja: 今日の仕事は昼休みをぬいて忙しかった。
examples.romaji: Kyou no shigoto wa hiruyasumi o nuite isogashikatta.
examples.meaningEn: Today's work was busy except for the lunch break.

examples.ja: 休暇の日をぬいて、毎日彼は働きます。
examples.romaji: Kyuuka no hi o nuite, mainichi kare wa hatarakimasu.
examples.meaningEn: He works every day except for the holidays.

title: ～ぬく (~nuku)
formation: Verb-stem + ぬく
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the concept of doing something completely or thoroughly.
longExplanationEn: 'Noun ぬく' is a phrase that conveys the idea of doing an action thoroughly or completely. It demonstrates a determination to carry out a task until the end. While the formation commonly uses verbs, it does convey a meaning similar to the English phrase 'to do completely'.

examples.ja: 彼は一日中本を読みぬく。
examples.romaji: Kare wa ichinichijuu hon o yominuku.
examples.meaningEn: He will read the book all day long.

examples.ja: 私はこのプロジェクトを終わらせぬく。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa kono purojekuto o owarasenuku.
examples.meaningEn: I will finish this project completely.

examples.ja: 彼女は毎日ジムに行き、エクササイズを終わりぬく。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa mainichi jim ni iki, ekusasaizu o owari nuku.
examples.meaningEn: She goes to the gym every day and finishes her exercise completely.

examples.ja: 私たちはこのゲームを解きぬく。
examples.romaji: Watashitachi wa kono geemu o tokinuku.
examples.meaningEn: We will solve this game completely.

title: Noun+のいかんだ (Noun no ikan da)
formation: Noun + のいかんだ/Noun + のいかんでは
shortExplanationEn: Depends on; up to
longExplanationEn: The のいかんだ(Noun no ikan da) grammar point is used to express that something depends on a particular situation or fact. It can be translated as 'Depends on' or 'up to' in English. The construction no ikan is usually used with nouns and naru verbs.

examples.ja: 彼が来るのいかんで決めよう。
examples.romaji: Kare ga kuru no ikan de kimeyou.
examples.meaningEn: Let's decide depending on whether he comes or not.

examples.ja: 試験の結果のいかんで留年が決まります。
examples.romaji: Shiken no kekka no ikan de ryuunen ga kimarimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether you repeat a year or not depends on the test results.

examples.ja: 天気のいかんでは、ピクニックをキャンセルします。
examples.romaji: Tenki no ikan de wa, pikunikku o kyanseru shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I'll cancel the picnic depending on the weather.

examples.ja: 彼の回答のいかんで、我々の次の行動が決まる。
examples.romaji: Kare no kaitou no ikan de, wareware no tsugi no koudou ga kimaru.
examples.meaningEn: Our next action depends on his response.

title: Noun のいかんでは (Noun no ikan de wa)
formation: Noun + のいかんでは
shortExplanationEn: It depends on the Noun
longExplanationEn: The のいかんでは grammar point is used when you want to express that the outcome or decision hinges on the condition described in the noun preceding this phrase. It can be translated as 'depending on ___' or 'it depends on ___' in English.

examples.ja: 試験の結果のいかんでは、大学の入学許可を得られるかどうかが決まります。
examples.romaji: Shiken no kekka no ikan dewa, daigaku no nyuugaku kyoka o erareru ka douka ga kimarimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether you can get permission to enter university depends on the result of the exam.

examples.ja: 天気のいかんでは、ピクニックを延期するかもしれません。
examples.romaji: Tenki no ikan dewa, pikunikku o enki suru kamoshiremasen.
examples.meaningEn: Depending on the weather, we might delay the picnic.

examples.ja: 交通事情のいかんでは、遅れることがあります。
examples.romaji: Koutsuujijou no ikan dewa, okureru koto ga arimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Depending on the traffic conditions, I might be late.

examples.ja: 経済の状況のいかんでは、会社の将来は決まります。
examples.romaji: Keizai no joukyou no ikan dewa, kaisha no shourai wa kimarimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Depending on the state of the economy, the company's future is determined.

title: Noun のいかんにかかわらず (Noun no ikan ni kakawarazu)
formation: Noun + のいかんにかかわらず
shortExplanationEn: Regardless of, irrespective of.
longExplanationEn: The phrase 'Noun のいかんにかかわらず' is used to express that something is the case irrespective of a certain condition. It can be translated as 'regardless of' or 'irrespective of' in English. This phrase is used with nouns.

examples.ja: 結果のいかんにかかわらず、彼は決定を後悔しなかった。
examples.romaji: Kekka no ikan ni kakawarazu, kare wa kettei wo koukai shinakatta.
examples.meaningEn: Regardless of the outcome, he didn't regret his decision.

examples.ja: 年齢のいかんにかかわらず、どんな人でも学ぶことは可能です。
examples.romaji: Nenrei no ikan ni kakawarazu, donna hito demo manabu koto wa kanou desu.
examples.meaningEn: Regardless of age, anyone can learn.

examples.ja: 経験のいかんにかかわらず、始めることが大切です。
examples.romaji: Keiken no ikan ni kakawarazu, hajimeru koto ga taisetsu desu.
examples.meaningEn: Regardless of experience, it's important to start.

examples.ja: 天候のいかんにかかわらず、ハイキングは予定通りに行われます。
examples.romaji: Tenkou no ikan ni kakawarazu, haikingu wa yotei doori ni okonawaremasu.
examples.meaningEn: Regardless of the weather, the hiking will take place as scheduled.

title: Noun のいかんによっては (Noun no ikan ni yotte wa)
formation: Noun + のいかんによっては
shortExplanationEn: Depending on the matter; depending on noun
longExplanationEn: The phrase のいかんによっては (no ikan ni yotte wa) is used to express the concept of 'depending on the matter or situation'. It is used to indicate that the outcome may change based on the condition or quality of the noun (matter) in question.

examples.ja: 天気のいかんによっては、ピクニックを延期しましょう。
examples.romaji: Tenki no ikan ni yotte wa, pikunikku o enki shimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Depending on the weather, we should postpone the picnic.

examples.ja: 試験の結果のいかんによっては、進学できます。
examples.romaji: Shiken no kekka no ikan ni yotte wa, shingaku dekimasu.
examples.meaningEn: You can go on to further education, depending on the result of the exam.

examples.ja: 状況のいかんによっては、早期退職を考えます。
examples.romaji: Joukyou no ikan ni yotte wa, souki taishoku o kangaemasu.
examples.meaningEn: Depending on the situation, I will consider early retirement.

examples.ja: 彼の反応のいかんによっては、私たちの次の行動を決めます。
examples.romaji: Kare no hannou no ikan ni yotte wa, watashitachi no tsugi no koudou o kimemasu.
examples.meaningEn: Depending on his reaction, we will decide our next move.

title: Noun のいかんによらず (~ no ikan ni yorazu)
formation: Noun + のいかんによらず
shortExplanationEn: Means 'regardless of', 'irrespective of', or 'no matter'
longExplanationEn: The Noun のいかんによらず grammar point is used to express that something is happening, will occur, or is true, regardless of a particular situation or condition. It is often translated as 'irrespective of', 'regardless of', or 'no matter' in English.

examples.ja: 試験の結果のいかんによらず、自分を信じ続けてください。
examples.romaji: Shiken no kekka no ikan ni yorazu, jibun o shinji tsudzukete kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Regardless of the test results, keep believing in yourself.

examples.ja: 天気のいかんによらず、毎日、ジョギングをします。
examples.romaji: Tenki no ikan ni yorazu, mainichi, jogingu o shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Regardless of the weather, I jog every day.

examples.ja: 彼の年齢のいかんによらず、彼は非常に経験豊富です。
examples.romaji: Kare no nenrei no ikan ni yorazu, kare wa hijou ni keikenhoufu desu.
examples.meaningEn: Regardless of his age, he is very experienced.

examples.ja: 国籍のいかんによらず、誰でもイベントに参加できます。
examples.romaji: Kokuseki no ikan ni yorazu, daredemo ibento ni sanka dekimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Regardless of nationality, anyone can participate in the event.

title: Noun のごとき Noun (A no gotoki B)
formation: Noun1 + のごとき + Noun2
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'like' or 'as if'; something/someone resembles something else strongly.
longExplanationEn: The のごとき pattern is used to convey the concept of 'just like' or 'as if'. The comparison here is strong, indicating that the thing/person is very similar to or almost the same as the thing/person compared to. It is a bit archaic and thus is more often found in writings than in everyday conversation.

examples.ja: 彼は神のごとき存在だ。
examples.romaji: Kare wa kami no gotoki sonzai da.
examples.meaningEn: He is an existence like a god.

examples.ja: 彼女の美しさは花のごときものだ。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no utsukushisa wa hana no gotoki mono da.
examples.meaningEn: Her beauty is like a flower.

examples.ja: 彼は悪魔のごとき行動をした。
examples.romaji: Kare wa akuma no gotoki koudou o shita.
examples.meaningEn: He acted like a devil.

examples.ja: この地方は春のごとき気候だ。
examples.romaji: Kono chiho wa haru no gotoki kiko da.
examples.meaningEn: This region has a climate like spring.

title: Noun のことだから (〜no koto dakara)
formation: Noun + のことだから
shortExplanationEn: Because it is about ~, considering it's ~
longExplanationEn: The のことだから grammar point is used to express an expectation or decision that is made specifically because of the person or thing mentioned. Usually it implies that the speaker is familiar with the character or typical behavior of the person or thing being talked about.

examples.ja: 彼のことだから、きっと大丈夫だと思う。
examples.romaji: Kare no koto dakara, kitto daijoubu da to omou.
examples.meaningEn: Because it's him, I think it will certainly be fine.

examples.ja: この店のことだから、料理は美味しいはずです。
examples.romaji: Kono mise no koto dakara, ryouri wa oishii hazu desu.
examples.meaningEn: Considering it's this restaurant, the food should be delicious.

examples.ja: 彼女のことだから、もうすぐ到着するでしょう。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no koto dakara, mou sugu touchaku suru deshou.
examples.meaningEn: Since it's her, she will probably arrive soon.

examples.ja: この会社のことだから、もうすぐ新製品が発表されるでしょう。
examples.romaji: Kono kaisha no koto dakara, mou sugu shinseihin ga happyou sareru deshou.
examples.meaningEn: Given this company, a new product will probably be announced soon.

title: Noun の 嫌いがある (Noun no kirai ga aru)
formation: Noun + の嫌いがある
shortExplanationEn: Have a dislike for a particular Noun
longExplanationEn: The grammar point 'Nounの嫌いがある' is used to express a dislike for a particular thing, action or situation, indicated by the noun. This structure is somewhat formal or old-fashioned. In casual speech, people more commonly say 'Noun が嫌い'.

examples.ja: 彼は魚の嫌いがあるので、寿司を食べません。
examples.romaji: Kare wa sakana no kirai ga aru node, sushi o tabemasen.
examples.meaningEn: He has a dislike for fish, so he doesn't eat sushi.

examples.ja: 彼女は高いところの嫌いがあるから、山登りはしない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa takai tokoro no kirai ga aru kara, yamanobori wa shinai.
examples.meaningEn: She has a dislike for high places, so she doesn't go mountain climbing.

examples.ja: 私の父は人ごみの嫌いがあるので、混雑した場所には行かない。
examples.romaji: Watashi no chichi wa hitogomi no kirai ga aru node, konzatsu shita basho ni wa ikanai.
examples.meaningEn: My father has a dislike for crowds, so he doesn't go to crowded places.

examples.ja: 子供の頃、私は野菜の嫌いがあったが、今は好きだ。
examples.romaji: Kodomo no koro, watashi wa yasai no kirai ga atta ga, ima wa suki da.
examples.meaningEn: When I was a child, I had a dislike for vegetables, but now I like them.

title: Noun の手前 (~no temae)
formation: Noun + の手前
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea of 'Before doing something; on the verge of'.
longExplanationEn: This grammar point is used to express the idea of being on the verge of doing something, or just before some major action/event. Nouns that are frequently used with this structure often denote an action or event (試験の手前, 選挙の手前, etc.).

examples.ja: 試験の手前、遊びに行くのはやめた方がいい。
examples.romaji: Shiken no temae, asobi ni iku no wa yameta hou ga ii.
examples.meaningEn: Just before the exam, it's better to stop going out to play.

examples.ja: 彼女と別れる手前、思い直しました。
examples.romaji: Kanojo to wakareru temae, omoinaoshimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Just before breaking up with her, I changed my mind.

examples.ja: 締め切りの手前で、やっとレポートが完成しました。
examples.romaji: Shimekiri no temae de, yatto repooto ga kansei shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I finally finished the report just before the deadline.

examples.ja: 選挙の手前、政治の話が多くなります。
examples.romaji: Senkyo no temae, seiji no hanashi ga ooku narimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Just before the election, there will be a lot of political talk.

title: Noun の極み (〜no kiwami)
formation: Noun + の極み
shortExplanationEn: Used to express the extreme or utmost point of something.
longExplanationEn: The の極み grammar point is used to express the highest level or the extreme of something. It often describes an extreme state or condition. The noun before の極み usually indicates some situation, state, or quality (e.g., 美の極み, 感動の極み).

examples.ja: 彼の料理の極みは、誰もマネできない。
examples.romaji: Kare no ryouri no kiwami wa, daremo mane dekinai.
examples.meaningEn: The pinnacle of his cooking is something nobody can imitate.

examples.ja: 彼女の努力の極みが、この成功を作り出した。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no doryoku no kiwami ga, kono seikou wo tsukuridashita.
examples.meaningEn: The extreme of her effort has created this success.

examples.ja: 彼は音楽の極みを追求し続けている。
examples.romaji: Kare wa ongaku no kiwami o tsuikyuu shitsuzukete iru.
examples.meaningEn: He continues to pursue the pinnacle of music.

examples.ja: この風景は美の極みだ。
examples.romaji: Kono fuukei wa bi no kiwami da.
examples.meaningEn: This scenery is the epitome of beauty.

title: Noun の 至り (~no itari)
formation: Noun + の至り
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'the height of', 'the extreme of' something.
longExplanationEn: The 'Nounの至り' grammar point is used to emphasize an extreme or the highest level of something, often an emotional state or an honor. Common nouns include 光栄, 若気, 感激, 恥, etc.

examples.ja: 彼の冗談には驚きの至りだ。
examples.romaji: Kare no joudan ni wa odoroki no itari da.
examples.meaningEn: His jokes are the height of surprise.

examples.ja: 父がプロジェクトのリーダーになったのは、光栄の至りです。
examples.romaji: Chichi ga purojekuto no riidaa ni natta no wa, kouei no itari desu.
examples.meaningEn: It is the height of honor that my father became the project leader.

examples.ja: 彼女の素晴らしい演技には感動の至りだった。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no subarashii engi ni wa kandou no itari datta.
examples.meaningEn: Her wonderful performance was the height of emotion.

examples.ja: その失敗は恥ずかしさの至りだった。
examples.romaji: Sono shippai wa hazukashisa no itari datta.
examples.meaningEn: That failure was the height of embarrassment.

title: Noun はいざ知らず (~ wa iza shirazu)
formation: Noun + はいざ知らず, Noun + とは/なんて/しかし etc.
shortExplanationEn: ‘I can understand it for ~, but for …? (surprising)’
longExplanationEn: The はいざ知らず grammar point is used when contrasting two scenarios: the first being understandable, and the second being unexpected or surprising by comparison. It often translates as “Well, for A, I can understand, but for B…?!”

examples.ja: 彼が遅れるのはいざ知らず、彼女まで遅刻するとは意外だ。
examples.romaji: Kare ga okureru no wa iza shirazu, kanojo made chikoku suru to wa igai da.
examples.meaningEn: Well, I can understand him being late, but even her...? That's surprising.

examples.ja: 夏はいざ知らず、冬にアイスクリームを食べるのは寒すぎませんか？
examples.romaji: Natsu wa iza shirazu, fuyu ni aisukuriimu o taberu no wa samusugimasen ka?
examples.meaningEn: I can see eating ice cream in summer, but in winter…isn't it too cold?

examples.ja: 子供がテレビゲームに夢中になるのはいざ知らず、大人までそんなに夢中になるとは思わなかった。
examples.romaji: Kodomo ga terebi geemu ni muchuu ni naru no wa iza shirazu, otona made sonnani muchuu ni naru to wa omowanakatta.
examples.meaningEn: I can see why kids get hooked on video games, but I didn't think even adults would get that absorbed.

examples.ja: 苦しい時はいざ知らず、平時でもそんなに努力するなんてすばらしい。
examples.romaji: Kurushii toki wa iza shirazu, heiji demo sonnani doryoku suru nante subarashii.
examples.meaningEn: Well, I can understand working hard in tough times, but to do so even in normal times is impressive.

title: Noun はおろか～すら (Noun wa oroka ～sura)
formation: Noun1 はおろか, Noun2 すら/さえ ～
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'not to mention ~, even ~'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point Noun はおろか～すら is used to emphasize that a particular statement applies not only to the first noun mentioned, but even more so to what comes after すら or さえ. It can be translated as ‘let alone ~, even ~’.

examples.ja: 彼は走るはおろか、速く歩くことすらできない。
examples.romaji: Kare wa hashiru wa oroka, hayaku aruku koto sura dekinai.
examples.meaningEn: He can't even walk fast, let alone run.

examples.ja: 日本語はおろか、英語さえ話しません。
examples.romaji: Nihongo wa oroka, eigo sae hanashimasen.
examples.meaningEn: He doesn't even speak English, let alone Japanese.

examples.ja: 彼は食事はおろか、一日中何も飲まなかった。
examples.romaji: Kare wa shokuji wa oroka, ichinichijuu nani mo nomanakatta.
examples.meaningEn: He didn't even drink anything all day, let alone eat a meal.

examples.ja: このレストランは味はおろか、サービスさえ悪い。
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa aji wa oroka, saabisu sae warui.
examples.meaningEn: Never mind the taste; even the service is bad.

title: Noun はおろか～まで (~wa oroka ~made)
formation: Noun1 + はおろか + Noun2 + まで
shortExplanationEn: Means 'not just A, but even B'; 'let alone A, even B'.
longExplanationEn: The phrase 'Noun はおろか' emphasizes something beyond the obvious. It can be translated as 'not only ..., but even ...'. The '～まで' part extends that idea further to show an unexpected or more advanced case.

examples.ja: 彼は英語はおろか、日本語までペラペラなんです。
examples.romaji: Kare wa eigo wa oroka, nihongo made perapera nan desu.
examples.meaningEn: He is not just fluent in English, but even in Japanese.

examples.ja: 彼女は数学はおろか、物理まで得意なんです。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa suugaku wa oroka, butsuri made tokui nan desu.
examples.meaningEn: She is not just good at math, but even at physics.

examples.ja: この料理は見た目はおろか、味まで最高だ。
examples.romaji: Kono ryouri wa mitame wa oroka, aji made saikou da.
examples.meaningEn: This dish is not only visually appealing, but it even tastes fantastic.

examples.ja: 彼は歌はおろか、ダンスまで上手だ。
examples.romaji: Kare wa uta wa oroka, dansu made umai da.
examples.meaningEn: He is not just good at singing, but even at dancing.

title: Noun はおろか～も (Noun wa oroka ～ mo)
formation: Noun + はおろか + Noun + も
shortExplanationEn: 'Not only...but also', 'not to mention', 'to say nothing of'
longExplanationEn: The 'Noun はおろか～も' grammar point is used to list items with an emphasis on how far the situation extends. It often appears in negative contexts to show that something supposedly more advanced (first part) is out of the question, and even something simpler (second part) is also not possible. It can be translated as 'Needless to say X, (he/she) can't even do Y.'

examples.ja: 絵を描くことはおろか、字さえ書けない。
examples.romaji: E wo kaku koto wa oroka, ji sae kakenai.
examples.meaningEn: Needless to say drawing pictures, he can’t even write letters.

examples.ja: 彼は英語はおろか、日本語もわからない。
examples.romaji: Kare wa eigo wa oroka, nihongo mo wakaranai.
examples.meaningEn: Needless to say English, he doesn’t even understand Japanese.

examples.ja: 彼女は数学はおろか、算数も得意ではない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa suugaku wa oroka, sansuu mo tokui dewa nai.
examples.meaningEn: Needless to say mathematics, she isn’t even good at arithmetic.

examples.ja: 彼は走ることはおろか、歩くことも難しい。
examples.romaji: Kare wa hashiru koto wa oroka, aruku koto mo muzukashii.
examples.meaningEn: Needless to say running, he finds even walking difficult.

title: Noun はさておき (~ wa sateoki)
formation: Noun + はさておき
shortExplanationEn: Means 'putting aside', 'aside from', 'notwithstanding'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point 'Noun はさておき' is used when highlighting something as an exception from an overall generalization, or when putting aside a particular matter before progressing to another point of discussion. The literal translation would be 'putting Noun aside', 'aside from Noun' or 'notwithstanding Noun.'

examples.ja: 試験の結果はさておき、この学期はとても学ぶことが多かった。
examples.romaji: Shiken no kekka wa sateoki, kono gakki wa totemo manabu koto ga ookatta.
examples.meaningEn: Aside from the exam results, I learned a lot this semester.

examples.ja: 天候はさておき、明日のピクニックは楽しみにしています。
examples.romaji: Tenkou wa sateoki, ashita no pikunikku wa tanoshimi ni shiteimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Putting aside the weather, I am looking forward to tomorrow's picnic.

examples.ja: 彼の性格はさておき、彼のスキルは認めざるを得ない。
examples.romaji: Kare no seikaku wa sateoki, kare no sukiru wa mitomezaru wo enai.
examples.meaningEn: Notwithstanding his personality, his skills are undeniable.

examples.ja: 彼があまりにも遅いことはさておき、彼が来ると思われること自体が問題だ。
examples.romaji: Kare ga amari ni mo osoi koto wa sateoki, kare ga kuru to omowareru koto jitai ga mondai da.
examples.meaningEn: Aside from him being late, the issue is that he is expected to come at all.

title: Noun + はどうであれ (~ wa dou de are)
formation: Noun + はどうであれ
shortExplanationEn: This phrase means 'regardless of' or 'no matter how…'.
longExplanationEn: It is used to convey indifference about the state or condition of a certain thing or matter. You can think of it as 'no matter what' or 'regardless of' in English. The phrase shows the speaker’s strong intention to do something or an assertion that doesn’t change regardless of the noun’s state or condition.

examples.ja: 天候はどうであれ、試合は行われます。
examples.romaji: Tenkou wa dou de are, shiai wa okonawaremasu.
examples.meaningEn: No matter what the weather is, the match will be held.

examples.ja: 結果はどうであれ、最善を尽くすだけです。
examples.romaji: Kekka wa dou de are, saizen wo tsukusu dake desu.
examples.meaningEn: No matter what the result is, all we can do is try our best.

examples.ja: あなたの意見はどうであれ、私は彼を信じます。
examples.romaji: Anata no iken wa dou de are, watashi wa kare wo shinjimasu.
examples.meaningEn: No matter what your opinion is, I believe him.

examples.ja: 試験の結果はどうであれ、あなたには何も変わりません。
examples.romaji: Shiken no kekka wa dou de are, anata ni wa nani mo kawarimasen.
examples.meaningEn: No matter what the result of the test is, nothing will change for you.

title: Noun まみれ (~mamire)
formation: Noun + まみれ
shortExplanationEn: Expresses a state in which something is entirely covered or filled with something else.
longExplanationEn: The まみれ grammar point is used to express a state where someone or something is completely covered, smeared, or filled with another thing. The noun before まみれ indicates what the person or thing is covered in. This grammar point often has a negative connotation.

examples.ja: 彼は泥まみれになって帰ってきた。
examples.romaji: Kare wa doro mamire ni natte kaette kita.
examples.meaningEn: He returned home covered in mud.

examples.ja: 彼女は涙まみれになりながら告白した。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa namida mamire ni narinagara kokuhaku shita.
examples.meaningEn: She confessed while crying her eyes out.

examples.ja: その事件後、彼はスキャンダルまみれになった。
examples.romaji: Sono jiken go, kare wa sukandaru mamire ni natta.
examples.meaningEn: After that incident, he was completely embroiled in scandal.

examples.ja: この部屋は埃まみれだ。
examples.romaji: Kono heya wa hokori mamire da.
examples.meaningEn: This room is covered in dust.

title: Noun もさることながら Noun も (A mo saru koto nagara B mo)
formation: Noun1 + もさることながら + Noun2 + も
shortExplanationEn: Means 'not only... but also'; the second noun is especially emphasized.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point 'Noun もさることながら Noun も' is used to express that both Nouns are true, important, or relevant. It can be translated as 'not only... but also' in English. However, among the two nouns, the one coming after もさることながら is more emphasized in comparison to the one before it.

examples.ja: 彼の歌唱力もさることながら、ダンスも素晴らしい。
examples.romaji: Kare no kashou-ryoku mo saru koto nagara, dansu mo subarashii.
examples.meaningEn: Not only his singing ability but also his dancing is wonderful.

examples.ja: このレストラン、味もさることながら、サービスもすばらしい。
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran, aji mo saru koto nagara, saabisu mo subarashii.
examples.meaningEn: At this restaurant, not only the taste but also the service is wonderful.

examples.ja: 彼女の美しさもさることながら、その知性も魅力的だ。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no utsukushisa mo saru koto nagara, sono chisei mo miryoku-teki da.
examples.meaningEn: Not only her beauty but also her intelligence is attractive.

examples.ja: その映画、ストーリーもさることながら、音楽も素晴らしかった。
examples.romaji: Sono eiga, sutoorii mo saru koto nagara, ongaku mo subarashikatta.
examples.meaningEn: In that movie, not only the story but also the music was wonderful.

title: Noun も兼ねて (~mo kanete)
formation: Noun + も兼ねて
shortExplanationEn: Used to express 'as well as', 'also', or 'at the same time'.
longExplanationEn: The も兼ねて grammar point indicates that two actions or purposes are being served simultaneously. The noun before も兼ねて tells what the additional action or purpose is.

examples.ja: 散歩も兼ねて、公園に行きました。
examples.romaji: Sanpo mo kanete, kouen ni ikimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I went to the park, also for a walk.

examples.ja: 今日は日本語の勉強も兼ねて、日本の映画を見ます。
examples.romaji: Kyou wa Nihongo no benkyou mo kanete, Nihon no eiga wo mimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Today, I will watch a Japanese movie, also to study Japanese.

examples.ja: 仕事も兼ねて、海外に行く予定です。
examples.romaji: Shigoto mo kanete, kaigai ni iku yotei desu.
examples.meaningEn: I plan to go abroad, also for work.

examples.ja: 旅行も兼ねて、友人の結婚式に出席します。
examples.romaji: Ryokou mo kanete, yuujin no kekkonshiki ni shusseki shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I will attend a friend's wedding, also as a trip.

title: Noun も相まって (~mo aimatte)
formation: Noun + も相まって
shortExplanationEn: Used to express that combined with something else, it caused a certain outcome.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point Noun も相まって is used to express the meaning that, combined with something else, it caused a certain result. Literally, it translates to 'also contributes to'. It is generally used with nouns and points out that there are multiple contributing factors.

examples.ja: 気候も相まって、この地域は農業に適しています。
examples.romaji: Kikou mo aimatte, kono chiiki wa nougyou ni tekishiteimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Combined with the climate, this area is suitable for agriculture.

examples.ja: 彼の才能も相まって、彼は急速に昇進した。
examples.romaji: Kare no sainou mo aimatte, kare wa kyuusoku ni shoushin shita.
examples.meaningEn: Combined with his talent, he was rapidly promoted.

examples.ja: 技術進歩も相まって、私たちはより効率的に仕事をこなすことができるようになりました。
examples.romaji: Gijutsu shinpo mo aimatte, watashitachi wa yori kouritsu-teki ni shigoto wo konasu koto ga dekiru you ni narimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Combined with technological progress, we have become able to handle our work more efficiently.

examples.ja: 彼の努力も相まって、プロジェクトは成功しました。
examples.romaji: Kare no doryoku mo aimatte, purojekuto wa seikou shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Combined with his efforts, the project was successful.

title: Noun を おいて他に Verb ない (〜wo oite hoka ni〜nai)
formation: Noun + をおいて他に + Verb negative form
shortExplanationEn: Means 'apart from ~ there's nothing else,' implying the Noun is the best or most suitable choice.
longExplanationEn: The structure Nounをおいて他に〜ない is used to express that there is no substitute for the Noun, no other choice, or that the Noun is the best or the only one to choose among various options. This form is often used in praising or making strong positive comments about someone or something.

examples.ja: この仕事を彼に任せる人をおいて他にいない。
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto wo kare ni makaseru hito wo oite hoka ni inai.
examples.meaningEn: Apart from him, there's no one else to entrust this job to.

examples.ja: この問題を解決できるのは彼をおいて他にいない。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wo kaiketsu dekiru no wa kare o oite hoka ni inai.
examples.meaningEn: Apart from him, there's no one else who can solve this problem.

examples.ja: この地域で美味しい麺を作る店をおいて他にない。
examples.romaji: Kono chiiki de oishii men o tsukuru mise o oite hoka ni nai.
examples.meaningEn: There is no other restaurant in this area that makes delicious noodles apart from this one.

examples.ja: 彼女の歌声を超える歌手をおいて他にいない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no utagoe o koeru kashu o oite hoka ni inai.
examples.meaningEn: There is no other singer who could surpass her singing voice.

title: Noun をもって (~wo motte)
formation: Noun + をもって
shortExplanationEn: Used to indicate means, methods, or time; 'with', 'by means of', 'at'.
longExplanationEn: The をもって grammar is used to express a means or method to achieve something, or to indicate a point in time. It can be translated as 'with' or 'by means of' when indicating means, and 'at' when indicating a point in time.

examples.ja: 彼は誠実さをもって人々に信頼されています。
examples.romaji: Kare wa seijitsusa wo motte hitobito ni shinrai sarete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: He is trusted by people with his honesty.

examples.ja: この賞をもって、彼女の努力が認められました。
examples.romaji: Kono shou wo motte, kanojo no doryoku ga mitomeraremashita.
examples.meaningEn: With this award, her efforts were recognized.

examples.ja: 彼は強い決意をもって問題に取り組んだ。
examples.romaji: Kare wa tsuyoi ketsui wo motte mondai ni torikunda.
examples.meaningEn: He tackled the problem with strong determination.

examples.ja: 3月をもって、この店は閉店します。
examples.romaji: Sangatsu wo motte, kono mise wa heiten shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: This store will close at the end of March.

title: Noun をものともせずに (Noun wo mono tomo sezu ni)
formation: Noun + をものともせずに
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea that 'despite something, action was carried out', translating to 'without being fazed by something'.
longExplanationEn: The 'Nounをものともせずに' grammar point indicates that something which could potentially be a hindrance or challenge was not taken into account when carrying out an action. The literal idea is ‘not making something an obstacle’, meaning actions were performed without being fazed or distracted by it.

examples.ja: 彼女は怖さをものともせずに、暗闇の中へ進んで行った。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa kowasa wo mono tomo sezu ni, kurayami no naka e susunde itta.
examples.meaningEn: Despite her fear, she went ahead into the darkness.

examples.ja: 試験のプレッシャーをものともせずに、彼は自信満々に答えた。
examples.romaji: Shiken no puresshaa wo mono tomo sezu ni, kare wa jishin manman ni kotaeta.
examples.meaningEn: Unfazed by the pressure of the exam, he answered with full confidence.

examples.ja: 彼は風雪をものともせずに山を登った。
examples.romaji: Kare wa fuusetsu wo mono tomo sezu ni, yama wo nobotta.
examples.meaningEn: He climbed the mountain unfazed by the wind and snow.

examples.ja: 彼は失敗をものともせずに、前に進み続けた。
examples.romaji: Kare wa shippai wo mono tomo sezu ni, mae ni susumi tsuzuketa.
examples.meaningEn: He continued to move forward, undeterred by failure.

title: Noun をよそに (~wo yoso ni)
formation: Noun + をよそに
shortExplanationEn: Despite; Notwithstanding; Regardless of
longExplanationEn: The Japanese phrase 'Nounをよそに' is used to express a situation where, despite some circumstance (represented by the noun), a certain action takes place. It can convey a feeling of disregard or nonchalance. In English, it can be translated as 'despite', 'notwithstanding', or 'regardless of'.

examples.ja: 経済危機をよそに、彼は高級な車を買った。
examples.romaji: Keizai kiki wo yoso ni, kare wa koukyuu na kuruma wo katta.
examples.meaningEn: Despite the economic crisis, he bought a luxury car.

examples.ja: 試験が迫るをよそに、彼は遊び続けている。
examples.romaji: Shiken ga semaru wo yoso ni, kare wa asobi tsuzukete iru.
examples.meaningEn: Despite the approaching test, he keeps on playing.

examples.ja: 親の反対をよそに、彼女は彼と結婚しました。
examples.romaji: Oya no hantai wo yoso ni, kanojo wa kare to kekkon shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Despite her parents' opposition, she married him.

examples.ja: その国の経済状況をよそに、多くの企業が進出し続けている。
examples.romaji: Sono kuni no keizai joukyou wo yoso ni, ooku no kigyou ga shinshutsu shi tsuzukete iru.
examples.meaningEn: Despite the country's economic situation, many companies continue to expand.

title: Noun を余儀なくされる (Noun wo yogi naku sareru)
formation: Noun + を + 余儀なくされる
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea of being forced into a certain situation, 'to be compelled', 'to be obliged'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar pattern 'Noun を余儀なくされる' is used to describe circumstances where a person or party is compelled or obliged to do something due to necessity or unavoidable circumstances. It translates as 'to be forced to', 'to be compelled to', or 'to have no choice but to'. This form is often used in formal or written Japanese.

examples.ja: 彼は経済的困難により、退学を余儀なくされた。
examples.romaji: Kare wa keizai-teki konnan ni yori, taigaku wo yogi naku saremashita.
examples.meaningEn: Due to financial difficulties, he was forced to drop out of school.

examples.ja: 厳しい天候によって、私たちは遠足を中止する余儀なくされた。
examples.romaji: Kibishi tenkou ni yotte, watashitachi wa ensoku wo chushi suru yogi naku sareta.
examples.meaningEn: We were forced to cancel the excursion due to the severe weather.

examples.ja: 怪我のため、彼は入院を余儀なくされた。
examples.romaji: Kega no tame, kare wa nyuuin wo yogi naku sareta.
examples.meaningEn: Due to his injury, he was forced to be hospitalized.

examples.ja: リストラの恐怖により、多くの従業員が転職を余儀なくされた。
examples.romaji: Risutora no kyoufu ni yori, ooku no juugyouin ga tenshoku wo yogi naku sareta.
examples.meaningEn: Fear of layoffs forced many employees to change jobs.

title: Noun を前提として (Noun wo zentei toshite)
formation: Noun + を前提として
shortExplanationEn: Used to express 'on the assumption of', 'based on'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point 'Noun を前提として' is used to convey that something is done based on or assuming something else. It indicates a preconceived condition or assumption on which the following action or plan relies.

examples.ja: 成功を前提として、ビジネスプランを作成しました。
examples.romaji: Seikou wo zentei toshite, bijinesu puran wo sakusei shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: We made a business plan on the assumption of success.

examples.ja: 彼の勝利を前提として、パーティーの準備を始めました。
examples.romaji: Kare no shouri wo zentei toshite, paatii no junbi wo hajimemashita.
examples.meaningEn: We started preparing for the party assuming his victory.

examples.ja: 両親の理解を前提として、旅行計画を進めます。
examples.romaji: Ryoushin no rikai wo zentei toshite, ryokou keikaku wo susumemasu.
examples.meaningEn: I will proceed with the travel plan based on my parents’ understanding.

examples.ja: このプロジェクトは、資金が揃うことを前提としています。
examples.romaji: Kono purojekuto wa, shikin ga sorou koto wo zentei toshiteimasu.
examples.meaningEn: This project assumes that the funds will be in place.

title: Noun を前提にして (Noun wo zentei ni shite)
formation: Noun + を前提にして
shortExplanationEn: Assuming; presupposing; on the assumption of.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point 'Noun を前提にして' is used to show that something is done assuming or presupposing the noun that comes before it. In English, it can be translated as 'on the assumption of', 'assuming', or 'presupposing'.

examples.ja: 好天を前提にして、明日のピクニックの計画を立てました。
examples.romaji: Kouten wo zentei ni shite, ashita no pikunikku no keikaku wo tatemashita.
examples.meaningEn: We planned tomorrow's picnic assuming fine weather.

examples.ja: 彼女の合格を前提にして、祝いのパーティーの準備を始めました。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no goukaku wo zentei ni shite, iwai no paatii no junbi wo hajimemashita.
examples.meaningEn: We started preparing for the celebration party assuming she would pass the exam.

examples.ja: 経済成長を前提にして、予算案が作られた。
examples.romaji: Keizai seichou wo zentei ni shite, yosan-an ga tsukurareta.
examples.meaningEn: The budget was made on the assumption of economic growth.

examples.ja: 彼の参加を前提にして、会議の日程を決めました。
examples.romaji: Kare no sanka wo zentei ni shite, kaigi no nittei wo kimemashita.
examples.meaningEn: We decided the meeting schedule assuming his participation.

title: Noun を境にして (Noun wo sakai ni shite)
formation: Noun + を境に(して)
shortExplanationEn: Means 'as a turning point', 'since (time)', or 'from (time) on'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point 'Noun を境にして' is used to indicate a significant change or turning point marked by a specific event or time. It means that after this boundary, some situation changes or a new state begins.

examples.ja: 父の死を境にして、彼の性格が一変した。
examples.romaji: Chichi no shi wo sakai ni shite, kare no seikaku ga ippen shita.
examples.meaningEn: His personality changed completely since his father's death.

examples.ja: 試験を境にして、休暇を取る予定です。
examples.romaji: Shiken wo sakai ni shite, kyuka wo toru yotei desu.
examples.meaningEn: I plan to take a vacation after the exam.

examples.ja: 春を境にして、新しい生活を始めるつもりだ。
examples.romaji: Haru wo sakai ni shite, atarashii seikatsu o hajimeru tsumori da.
examples.meaningEn: I intend to start a new life from spring onwards.

examples.ja: 彼女は入学を境にして、ファッションに興味を持ち始めた。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa nyuugaku wo sakai ni shite, fasshon ni kyoumi o mochi hajimeta.
examples.meaningEn: She began to take an interest in fashion from the time she entered school.

title: Noun を機にして (~wo ki ni shite)
formation: Noun + を機にして
shortExplanationEn: To take the opportunity to; taking the occasion to; on the occasion of.
longExplanationEn: The grammar pattern 'Noun を機にして' expresses the idea of using a certain event or situation as an opportunity to do something else. It is often used when some change or new step is taken as a result of a specific event.

examples.ja: 引っ越しを機にして、家具を新しくしました。
examples.romaji: Hikkoshi wo ki ni shite, kagu wo atarashiku shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I took the opportunity of moving to get new furniture.

examples.ja: 卒業を機にして、新しい仕事を探し始めた。
examples.romaji: Sotsugyou wo ki ni shite, atarashii shigoto wo sagashi hajimeta.
examples.meaningEn: I started looking for a new job on the occasion of graduation.

examples.ja: このプロジェクトの失敗を機にして、会社全体の仕事のやり方を見直すことにした。
examples.romaji: Kono purojekuto no shippai wo ki ni shite, kaisha zentai no shigoto no yarikata wo minaosu koto ni shita.
examples.meaningEn: We decided to review the way the entire company works, taking this project's failure as an opportunity.

examples.ja: 結婚を機にして、私たちは新しい町へ引っ越しました。
examples.romaji: Kekkon wo ki ni shite, watashitachi wa atarashii machi e hikkoshimashita.
examples.meaningEn: We moved to a new town on the occasion of our marriage.

title: Noun を皮切りに / を皮切りにして (Noun wo kawakiri ni / wo kawakiri ni shite)
formation: Noun + を皮切りに / を皮切りにして
shortExplanationEn: Used to indicate the starting point of some series of events or actions; 'starting with', 'beginning with'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point 'Noun を皮切りに / を皮切りにして' is used to convey the idea of something beginning or starting with a certain event or action. This pattern is often used when the speaker wants to highlight the starting point of a series or string of events.

examples.ja: 彼はその論文を皮切りにして複数の研究を開始した。
examples.romaji: Kare wa sono ronbun o kawakiri ni shite fukusū no kenkyū o kaishi shita.
examples.meaningEn: Starting with that thesis, he started multiple research projects.

examples.ja: 彼女はこのコンペティションを皮切りにして彼女のダンスキャリアが飛躍的に成長した。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa kono konpetishon o kawakiri ni shite kanojo no dansu kyaria ga hiyakuteki ni seichō shita.
examples.meaningEn: Starting with this competition, her dance career drastically grew.

examples.ja: この映画を皮切りに、彼は人気俳優としての立場を確立した。
examples.romaji: Kono eiga o kawakiri ni, kare wa ninki haiyū to shite no tachiba o kakuritsu shita.
examples.meaningEn: Starting with this movie, he established his position as a popular actor.

examples.ja: このプロジェクトを皮切りにして、会社は新たな方向性を示し始めた。
examples.romaji: Kono purojekuto o kawakiri ni shite, kaisha wa arata na hōkōsei o shimeshi hajimeta.
examples.meaningEn: Starting with this project, the company began to show a new direction.

title: Noun を皮切りにして (Noun wo kawakiri ni shite)
formation: Noun + を皮切りに (して)
shortExplanationEn: Used to express 'Starting with', 'Beginning with'
longExplanationEn: 'Noun を皮切りにして' is a Japanese grammatical form used when one wants to express that something begins with a certain event or action, then continues on in a sequence. It is the same as saying 'starting with...' or 'beginning with...' in English.

examples.ja: 彼はその日を皮切りにして毎日ジムに通い始めました。
examples.romaji: Kare wa sono hi o kawakiri ni shite mainichi jimu ni kayoi hajimemashita.
examples.meaningEn: Starting from that day, he began to go to the gym every day.

examples.ja: 新年会を皮切りにして、今年はたくさんのイベントが開催されます。
examples.romaji: Shinnenkai o kawakiri ni shite, kotoshi wa takusan no ibento ga kaisai saremasu.
examples.meaningEn: Starting with the New Year's party, a lot of events will be held this year.

examples.ja: この映画を皮切りにして彼の人気は急上昇しました。
examples.romaji: Kono eiga o kawakiri ni shite kare no ninki wa kyūjōshō shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Beginning with this movie, his popularity skyrocketed.

examples.ja: 東京を皮切りにして、彼の展示会は全国で開かれる予定です。
examples.romaji: Tōkyō o kawakiri ni shite, kare no tenjikai wa zenkoku de hirakareru yotei desu.
examples.meaningEn: Starting in Tokyo, his exhibitions are set to be held nationwide.

title: Noun を禁じ得ない (〜wo kinjienai)
formation: Noun + を禁じ得ない
shortExplanationEn: 'Cannot help but do', or 'inevitable'
longExplanationEn: The 〜を禁じ得ない (wo kinjienai) grammar point is used to express a situation or emotion that is so strong it can't be helped or is inevitable. It is used with nouns to express an uncontrollable feeling or action.

examples.ja: この結果には驚きを禁じ得ない。
examples.romaji: Kono kekka ni wa odoroki o kinjienai.
examples.meaningEn: I can't help but be surprised at this result.

examples.ja: そのニュースを聞いて、彼の成功を祝福する気持ちを禁じ得ない。
examples.romaji: Sono nyūsu o kiite, kare no seikō o shukufuku suru kimochi o kinjienai.
examples.meaningEn: Hearing that news, I can't help but feel the urge to celebrate his success.

examples.ja: 彼女の美しさには感動を禁じ得ない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no utsukushisa ni wa kandō o kinjienai.
examples.meaningEn: I can't help but be moved by her beauty.

examples.ja: 彼の話を聞いて、笑いを禁じ得ない。
examples.romaji: Kare no hanashi o kiite, warai o kinjienai.
examples.meaningEn: Hearing his story, I can't help but laugh.

title: Noun を経て (〜wo hete)
formation: Noun + を経て
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea of 'through' or 'via'; going through some process or phase.
longExplanationEn: The を経て grammar point is used to express the concept of going through some process, phase, place, or period of time as a means of achieving something or reaching some end point. It can be literally translated as 'through' or 'via' in English, and can be used both literally and metaphorically.

examples.ja: 彼は苦労を経て成功を手に入れた。
examples.romaji: Kare wa kurō o hete seikō o te ni ireta.
examples.meaningEn: He achieved success through hardships.

examples.ja: 彼女はニューヨークを経て日本に帰国した。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa Nyūyōku o hete Nihon ni kikoku shita.
examples.meaningEn: She returned to Japan via New York.

examples.ja: 私達は長い議論を経て決定を下した。
examples.romaji: Watashitachi wa nagai giron o hete kettei o kudashita.
examples.meaningEn: We made a decision through a long discussion.

examples.ja: 多くの修練を経て、彼は達人のレベルになった。
examples.romaji: Ōku no shuren o hete, kare wa tatsujin no reberu ni natta.
examples.meaningEn: Through a lot of training, he reached a master's level.

title: Noun を踏まえて (〜wo fumaete)
formation: Noun + を踏まえて
shortExplanationEn: Based on; taking into account; in light of
longExplanationEn: The verb 踏まえる (fumaeru) means 'to be based on' or 'to take into consideration'. When ～を踏まえて is connected with a noun, it implies that actions or decisions are made based on or taking into account the noun. It also expresses looking at a situation from the standpoint of the noun before proceeding to the following action.

examples.ja: 過去の結果を踏まえて、新たな計画を立てました。
examples.romaji: Kako no kekka o fumaete, aratana keikaku o tatemashita.
examples.meaningEn: Based on previous results, we have made a new plan.

examples.ja: 現地の文化を踏まえて、プレゼンテーションを準備します。
examples.romaji: Genchi no bunka o fumaete, purezentēshon o junbi shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I will prepare a presentation taking into account the local culture.

examples.ja: 教師の意見を踏まえて、規則を変更しました。
examples.romaji: Kyōshi no iken o fumaete, kisoku o henkō shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: We changed the rules in light of the teachers' opinions.

examples.ja: 最新の研究結果を踏まえて、新しい薬が開発されました。
examples.romaji: Saishin no kenkyū kekka o fumaete, atarashii kusuri ga kaihatsu saremashita.
examples.meaningEn: Considering the latest research findings, a new medicine has been developed.

title: Noun を限りに (Noun wo kagiri ni)
formation: Noun + を限りに
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea of 'as of', 'with', or 'from', indicating the end of something or a point of change.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point Noun を限りに is used to indicate the end point of something or a point of change from that time onward. The noun preceding this expression usually refers to a point in time, signifying that something ends or changes starting at that point.

examples.ja: 今年度を限りに、この大学での教授活動を終えるつもりです。
examples.romaji: Konnendo o kagiri ni, kono daigaku de no kyouju katsudou o oeru tsumori desu.
examples.meaningEn: I intend to end my teaching activities at this university as of this academic year.

examples.ja: 今日を限りに、タバコを吸うのをやめます。
examples.romaji: Kyou o kagiri ni, tabako o suu no o yamemasu.
examples.meaningEn: As of today, I will stop smoking.

examples.ja: 3月31日を限りに、会社を退職します。
examples.romaji: 3-gatsu 31-nichi o kagiri ni, kaisha o taishoku shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I will leave the company as of March 31.

examples.ja: 来週を限りに、この番組は終了します。
examples.romaji: Raishuu o kagiri ni, kono bangumi wa shuuryou shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: This program will end as of next week.

title: Noun 並み (~nami)
formation: Noun + 並み
shortExplanationEn: Indicates 'like' or 'as' something or someone; 'equivalent to', 'around'.
longExplanationEn: The Noun 並み grammar point is used to show a comparison, often translated as 'like' or 'equivalent to'. It can also indicate approximate quantity or extent, similar to 'around'.

examples.ja: 彼はプロのボクサー並みの力を持っています。
examples.romaji: Kare wa puro no bokusaa nami no chikara o motte imasu.
examples.meaningEn: He has power equivalent to a professional boxer.

examples.ja: 彼女の料理の腕前はレストラン並みです。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no ryouri no udemae wa resutoran nami desu.
examples.meaningEn: Her cooking skills are like those of a restaurant.

examples.ja: このエリアの夏は砂漠並みの暑さです。
examples.romaji: Kono eria no natsu wa sabaku nami no atsusa desu.
examples.meaningEn: The summer heat in this area is like a desert.

examples.ja: 今日の仕事は社長並みの忙しさだった。
examples.romaji: Kyou no shigoto wa shachou nami no isogashisa datta.
examples.meaningEn: Today's work was as busy as a president's.

title: Noun + 前提で (Noun + zentei de)
formation: Noun + 前提で
shortExplanationEn: Expresses a condition or requirement for something to be established: 'on the assumption that', 'under the condition of'.
longExplanationEn: The Noun + 前提で grammar point is used to define the premise, assumption, or requirement necessary for some event or action. It translates to 'on the assumption that', 'under the condition of', or 'with the understanding that'. The following clause is based on that premise.

examples.ja: 説明できる英語力がある前提で、この仕事を引き受けてください。
examples.romaji: Setsumei dekiru eigo-ryoku ga aru zentei de, kono shigoto o hikiukete kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Please take on this job on the assumption that you have enough English skills to explain.

examples.ja: 彼が一緒に行く前提で、旅行の計画を立てました。
examples.romaji: Kare ga issho ni iku zentei de, ryokou no keikaku o tatemashita.
examples.meaningEn: I planned the trip on the assumption that he would go together.

examples.ja: 成功する前提で、このプロジェクトを進めています。
examples.romaji: Seikou suru zentei de, kono purojekuto o susumete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: We are moving forward with this project on the assumption that we will succeed.

examples.ja: 雨が降らない前提で、ピクニックの準備をしました。
examples.romaji: Ame ga furanai zentei de, pikunikku no junbi o shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I prepared for the picnic under the condition that it wouldn't rain.

title: Verbる / Noun(である) + 限り(は) (kagiri (wa))
formation:
Verb-dictionary form + 限り(は)
Noun(である) + 限り(は)
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'as long as', 'while this condition is true'.
longExplanationEn: This grammar is used to express that something holds true as long as a particular condition remains in effect. When using a noun, it can appear as Noun(である) + 限り(は). With a verb, use the dictionary form + 限り(は).

examples.ja: 私が生きている限りは、あなたを支えます。
examples.romaji: Watashi ga ikite iru kagiri wa, anata o sasaemasu.
examples.meaningEn: As long as I'm alive, I will support you.

examples.ja: 子供が学校に通っている限り、私たちは彼らを守ります。
examples.romaji: Kodomo ga gakkou ni kayotte iru kagiri, watashitachi wa karera o mamorimasu.
examples.meaningEn: As long as the kids are going to school, we will protect them.

examples.ja: 能力がある限り、最善を尽くします。
examples.romaji: Nouryoku ga aru kagiri, saizen o tsukushimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I will do my best as long as I have the ability.

examples.ja: あなたがここにいる限り、私は安心です。
examples.romaji: Anata ga koko ni iru kagiri, watashi wa anshin desu.
examples.meaningEn: As long as you're here, I feel safe.

title: Verb がてら (~ gatera)
formation: Verb (dictionary or -ます stem) + がてら
shortExplanationEn: Expresses doing something while doing another; 'while', 'on the occasion of'.
longExplanationEn: The ～がてら grammar point is used to express doing something else in the middle of or on the occasion of another activity. Often used with verbs of movement or activities that easily combine with a second action.

examples.ja: 散歩がてらに郵便局へ行った。
examples.romaji: Sanpo gatera ni yūbinkyoku e itta.
examples.meaningEn: I went to the post office while taking a walk.

examples.ja: 買い物がてらに友達に会いに行った。
examples.romaji: Kaimono gatera ni tomodachi ni ai ni itta.
examples.meaningEn: I went to see a friend while doing some shopping.

examples.ja: ジョギングがてらに新聞を買いに行った。
examples.romaji: Jogingu gatera ni shinbun o kai ni itta.
examples.meaningEn: I went to buy a newspaper while jogging.

examples.ja: 公園でピクニックがてら読書をした。
examples.romaji: Kōen de pikunikku gatera dokusho o shita.
examples.meaningEn: I read a book while having a picnic in the park.

title: Verb こそすれ (~koso sure)
formation: Verb-ますstem + こそすれ、(絶対に)～ない
shortExplanationEn: Often used in the pattern 'Xこそすれ、Y〜ない', meaning 'One might do X, but definitely not Y'.
longExplanationEn: Verb-ますstem + こそすれ is typically used to emphasize that while someone might certainly do or be X, there's no way they would do or be Y. The sentence that follows is usually a strong negative, denying the possibility of Y.

examples.ja: 彼は友達を助けこそすれ、自分を卑下することはありません。
examples.romaji: Kare wa tomodachi o tasuke koso sure, jibun o higesuru koto wa arimasen.
examples.meaningEn: He might help his friends, but he never belittles himself.

examples.ja: 彼女は料理こそすれ、掃除はほとんどしない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa ryōri koso sure, sōji wa hotondo shinai.
examples.meaningEn: She might cook, but she rarely cleans.

examples.ja: 私の兄は勉強こそすれ、遊ぶ時間がほとんどない。
examples.romaji: Watashi no ani wa benkyō koso sure, asobu jikan ga hotondo nai.
examples.meaningEn: He might study a lot, but he barely has time to have fun.

examples.ja: 母は働きこそすれ、自分のために時間を使うことはほとんどない。
examples.romaji: Haha wa hataraki koso sure, jibun no tame ni jikan o tsukau koto wa hotondo nai.
examples.meaningEn: My mother might work a lot, but she hardly spends any time on herself.

title: Verb させられる (~saserareru)
formation: Verb-casual (non-past) + させられる
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'being forced to do something' or 'being made to do something'.
longExplanationEn: The させられる grammar point is used to express the passive causative form in Japanese. This form is used when you are forced or made to do something by someone or something else. It's a combination of causative and passive form, and it often implies having been made to do something against one's will.

examples.ja: 彼に無理やり飲ませられた。
examples.romaji: Kare ni muriyari nomaserareta.
examples.meaningEn: He forced me to drink against my will.

examples.ja: 私は父に勉強させられました。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa chichi ni benkyō saseraremashita.
examples.meaningEn: My father made me study.

examples.ja: 子供たちは毎日ピアノを弾かせられる。
examples.romaji: Kodomo-tachi wa mainichi piano o hikaserareru.
examples.meaningEn: The kids are made to play the piano every day.

examples.ja: 私たちは強制的に早起きさせられました。
examples.romaji: Watashitachi wa kyōsei-teki ni hayaoki saseraremashita.
examples.meaningEn: We were forcibly made to wake up early.

title: Verb ざるを得ない (~ zaru wo enai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + ざるを得ない (する → せざるを得ない)
shortExplanationEn: 'Cannot but', 'have no choice but to', 'forced to'
longExplanationEn: The Verb ざるを得ない construction is used when the subject is compelled or forced to do something out of necessity, leaving no other choice. It conveys the idea of 'cannot but...' or 'have no choice but...' in English.

examples.ja: この仕事が嫌ならもう辞めざるを得ない。
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto ga iya nara mō yamezaru o enai.
examples.meaningEn: If you hate this job, you have no choice but to quit.

examples.ja: 試験が近いので、夜遅くまで勉強せざるを得ない。
examples.romaji: Shiken ga chikai no de, yoru osoku made benkyō sezaru o enai.
examples.meaningEn: The exam is near, so I have no choice but to study late into the night.

examples.ja: 涙が止まらない。泣かざるを得ない。
examples.romaji: Namida ga tomaranai. Nakazaru o enai.
examples.meaningEn: My tears won't stop. I can't help but cry.

examples.ja: 先生の命令だから、従わざるを得ない。
examples.romaji: Sensei no meirei dakara, shitagawazaru o enai.
examples.meaningEn: It's the teacher's order, so I have no choice but to comply.

title: Verb ずじまい (~zu jimai)
formation: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずじまい
shortExplanationEn: Expressing regret for not having done something; not being able to; missing the chance to do it.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point ～ずじまい (~zu jimai) is used to express regret for not doing something, not being able to do it, or not getting the chance to do it. It highlights an action that ultimately never happened, despite intentions or opportunities.

examples.ja: 日本へ来たのに、一度も富士山を見ずじまいで帰国してしまった。
examples.romaji: Nihon e kita noni, ichidomo Fuji-san o mizu jimai de kikoku shite shimatta.
examples.meaningEn: I came to Japan but ended up going home without ever getting to see Mt. Fuji.

examples.ja: 出張のため、彼女に会わずじまいだった。
examples.romaji: Shucchō no tame, kanojo ni awazu jimai datta.
examples.meaningEn: Because I was on a business trip, I ended up not meeting her.

examples.ja: 昨日は疲れすぎて、宿題をせずじまいだ。
examples.romaji: Kinō wa tsukare-sugite, shukudai o sezu jimai da.
examples.meaningEn: I was too tired yesterday and ended up not doing my homework.

examples.ja: 着物を買ったが、結局、一度も着ずじまいだ。
examples.romaji: Kimono o katta ga, kekkyoku, ichido mo kizu jimai da.
examples.meaningEn: I bought a kimono, but in the end, I never got the chance to wear it.

title: Verb ずとも (〜zu tomo)
formation: Verb-negative stem + ずとも
shortExplanationEn: Means 'Even without doing (X), (Y) can still happen'.
longExplanationEn: The ～ずとも grammar point is used to express that, even if something isn't done, the desired result can still be achieved or the situation remains unaffected. It can be translated as 'even without' or 'even if not'.

examples.ja: 朝ごはんを食べずとも、働くことができます。
examples.romaji: Asagohan o tabezu tomo, hataraku koto ga dekimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Even without eating breakfast, I can work.

examples.ja: 車がなくとも、バスで行くことができます。
examples.romaji: Kuruma ga nakutomo, basu de iku koto ga dekimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Even without a car, you can go by bus.

examples.ja: お金がなくとも、幸せになれる。
examples.romaji: Okane ga nakutomo, shiawase ni nareru.
examples.meaningEn: Even without money, one can be happy.

examples.ja: インターネットがなくとも、情報を得る方法はいくつかあります。
examples.romaji: Intānetto ga nakutomo, jōhō o eru hōhō wa ikutsu ka arimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Even without the internet, there are several ways to obtain information.

title: Verb ずにはおかない (~zuni wa okanai)
formation: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずにはおかない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'cannot help but...', 'will definitely...', or 'must do something'.
longExplanationEn: The ずにはおかない grammar point conveys the sentiment of an inevitable action or a strong emotional compulsion. It can mean 'I can’t let it pass without doing X' or 'I definitely will do X'.

examples.ja: 彼が困っていると聞いたら、助けずにはおかない。
examples.romaji: Kare ga komatte iru to kiitara, tasukezuni wa okanai.
examples.meaningEn: If I hear he is in trouble, I cannot help but assist him.

examples.ja: 食事の準備がこのままだと、手伝わずにはおかないだろう。
examples.romaji: Shokuji no junbi ga kono mama da to, tetsudawazuni wa okanai darō.
examples.meaningEn: If the meal prep stays like this, I won't be able to stop myself from helping.

examples.ja: この問題が解決しないなら、改善策を考えずにはおかない。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai ga kaiketsu shinai nara, kaizen-saku o kangaezuni wa okanai.
examples.meaningEn: If this issue isn't resolved, I can't help but think of a solution.

examples.ja: 彼女が泣いているのを見たら、慰めずにはおかない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga naite iru no o mitara, nagusamezuni wa okanai.
examples.meaningEn: When I see her crying, I cannot help but comfort her.

title: Verb ずにはすまない (Verb zuni wa sumanai)
formation: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずにはすまない
shortExplanationEn: 'Cannot do without...', 'unavoidable', 'have to'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point ずにはすまない is used when something must be done due to social/moral obligation, necessity, or rules. It implies that there is no way around taking that action.

examples.ja: この問題を解決しないと、社会全体が影響を受けるので、解決せずにはすまない。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai o kaiketsu shinai to, shakai zentai ga eikyō o ukeru node, kaiketsu sezu ni wa sumanai.
examples.meaningEn: Since this issue will affect the entire society if not resolved, we cannot do without solving it.

examples.ja: これは子供たちの未来を思うと、教育改革を行わずにはすまないことだ。
examples.romaji: Kore wa kodomo-tachi no mirai o omou to, kyōiku kaikaku o okonawazu ni wa sumanai koto da.
examples.meaningEn: For the sake of the children’s future, we cannot avoid carrying out educational reforms.

examples.ja: この件については、彼に謝らずにはすまない。
examples.romaji: Kono ken ni tsuite wa, kare ni ayamarazu ni wa sumanai.
examples.meaningEn: Regarding this matter, I can’t do without apologizing to him.

examples.ja: 結婚式に出席するなら、プレゼントを渡さずにはすまない。
examples.romaji: Kekkonshiki ni shusseki suru nara, purezento o watasazu ni wa sumanai.
examples.meaningEn: If you attend a wedding, you have to bring a gift.

title: Verb そうにない (Verb sou ni nai)
formation: Verb-ますstem + そうにない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses unlikelihood or that something does not seem likely to happen.
longExplanationEn: The そうにない grammar point is typically formed from the verb’s ます-stem plus そうにない, and is used to express that something seems unlikely to happen or that someone seems unlikely to do something.

examples.ja: 彼はそんなに早く走れそうにない。
examples.romaji: Kare wa sonna ni hayaku hashire sō ni nai.
examples.meaningEn: He doesn't seem likely to run that fast.

examples.ja: この書類を明日までに終わりそうにない。
examples.romaji: Kono shorui o ashita made ni owarisō ni nai.
examples.meaningEn: It doesn’t seem like I’ll be able to finish these documents by tomorrow.

examples.ja: 今日雨が降りそうにない。
examples.romaji: Kyō ame ga furisō ni nai.
examples.meaningEn: It doesn't look like it will rain today.

examples.ja: 彼女は間違えそうにない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa machigae sō ni nai.
examples.meaningEn: She doesn’t seem likely to make a mistake.

title: Verb そうもない (〜sou mo nai)
formation: Verb-ますstem + そうもない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses negative potential; 'it doesn't seem like...', 'it's not likely...'.
longExplanationEn: ～そうもない is used to express a low possibility or unlikelihood of something happening. It is typically attached to the ます-stem of a verb, similar to そうにない but with an even stronger sense of improbability in many contexts.

examples.ja: 今日中に仕事を終えそうもない。
examples.romaji: Kyōjū ni shigoto o oesō mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: It doesn't seem like I'll finish my work today.

examples.ja: 雨が止みそうもない。
examples.romaji: Ame ga yamisō mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: It doesn't seem like the rain will stop.

examples.ja: 彼が約束を守りそうもない。
examples.romaji: Kare ga yakusoku o mamorisō mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: It doesn't seem like he will keep his promise.

examples.ja: この難しい問題は解決しそうもない。
examples.romaji: Kono muzukashii mondai wa kaiketsu shisō mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: It doesn't seem like this difficult problem will be solved.

title: Verb そばから (〜soba kara)
formation: Verb-ますstem + そばから
shortExplanationEn: Expresses that as soon as one action finishes, another begins immediately.
longExplanationEn: The 〜そばから grammar point is used to indicate that right after a certain action is completed, another action takes place—often suggesting a repetitive or frustrating cycle.

examples.ja: 掃除をするそばから子供たちが散らかす。
examples.romaji: Sōji o suru soba kara kodomo-tachi ga chirakasu.
examples.meaningEn: As soon as I clean, the kids mess it up.

examples.ja: 仕事を終えるそばから新たな仕事が舞い込んできた。
examples.romaji: Shigoto o oeru soba kara aratana shigoto ga maikonde kita.
examples.meaningEn: As soon as I finished my work, a new task came in.

examples.ja: それを解決するそばからまた別の問題が出てきた。
examples.romaji: Sore o kaiketsu suru soba kara mata betsu no mondai ga detekita.
examples.meaningEn: As soon as we solve that, another problem arises.

examples.ja: 眠りにつくそばから電話が鳴った。
examples.romaji: Nemuri ni tsuku soba kara denwa ga natta.
examples.meaningEn: The phone rang as soon as I fell asleep.

title: Verb たが最後 (〜ta ga saigo)
formation: Verb-casual, past (た form) + が最後
shortExplanationEn: Emphasizes 'once something happens, there is no going back'; 'once', 'as soon as', etc.
longExplanationEn: The 〜たが最後 grammar point indicates that once an action is taken or event occurs, some (often irreversible) consequence follows. In English, it can be translated as 'once', 'as soon as', or 'the moment that...'.

examples.ja: 彼が一度怒ったが最後、誰も彼を止めることはできません。
examples.romaji: Kare ga ichido okotta ga saigo, dare mo kare o tomeru koto wa dekimasen.
examples.meaningEn: Once he gets angry, no one can stop him.

examples.ja: このゲームを始めたが最後、一晩中やり続けた。
examples.romaji: Kono gēmu o hajimeta ga saigo, hitobanjū yari tsuzuketa.
examples.meaningEn: As soon as I started this game, I played it all night.

examples.ja: 彼女が部屋を出たが最後、二度と戻ってこなかった。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga heya o deta ga saigo, nido to modotte konakatta.
examples.meaningEn: The moment she left the room, she never came back.

examples.ja: 私がその橋を渡ったが最後、それが最後の別れだった。
examples.romaji: Watashi ga sono hashi o watatta ga saigo, sore ga saigo no wakare datta.
examples.meaningEn: Once I crossed that bridge, it was our final goodbye.

title: Verb たことにしてください (~ ta koto ni shite kudasai)
formation: Verb (past, casual) + ことにしてください
shortExplanationEn: Asking someone to pretend or accept that something has already happened or taken a certain state.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point 'Verb たことにしてください' is used to request someone to act as if or pretend that a certain action has already occurred or a certain state exists. It is used in various contexts to express the idea of accepting a hypothetical situation for convenience or to avoid conflicts.

examples.ja: 昨日のことは忘れたことにしてください。
examples.romaji: Kinou no koto wa wasureta koto ni shite kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Please act as if you have forgotten about yesterday.

examples.ja: 私がここに来たことを誰にも言わないで、秘密にしたことにしてください。
examples.romaji: Watashi ga koko ni kita koto o dare ni mo iwanaide, himitsu ni shita koto ni shite kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Please don't tell anyone that I came here; act as if it’s a secret.

examples.ja: 彼が間違えたと思ったことにしてください。
examples.romaji: Kare ga machigaeta to omotta koto ni shite kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Please pretend that you think he made a mistake.

examples.ja: 彼女が帰ったことにしてください。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga kaetta koto ni shite kudasai.
examples.meaningEn: Let’s pretend that she has gone home.

title: Verb たら Verb たで (~ tara ~ tade)
formation: Verb-た form + ら + (same Verb-た form) + で
shortExplanationEn: Conveys that if something happens (or doesn’t happen), there is a particular consequence or situation either way.
longExplanationEn: The pattern VerbたらVerbたで is used for scenarios where doing something creates a certain issue or reaction, and not doing it also creates a different concern. It often shows that no matter which side of the condition you end up on, there’s some related result or trouble.

examples.ja: 行ったら行ったで大変だし、行かなかったら行かなかったで後悔しそうだ。
examples.romaji: Ittara itta de taihen da shi, ikanakattara ikanakatta de kōkai shisō da.
examples.meaningEn: If I go, it’s a hassle; if I don’t go, I’ll regret it.

examples.ja: 買ったら買ったでお金がなくなるし、買わなかったら買わなかったで後で欲しくなる。
examples.romaji: Kattara katta de okane ga naku naru shi, kawanakattara kawanakatta de ato de hoshiku naru.
examples.meaningEn: If I buy it, I’ll run out of money; if I don’t buy it, I’ll want it later.

examples.ja: 作ったら作ったで食べきれないし、作らなかったら作らなかったでお腹が空く。
examples.romaji: Tsukuttara tsukutta de tabekirenai shi, tsukuranakattara tsukuranakatta de onaka ga suku.
examples.meaningEn: If I cook, I can’t finish it all; if I don’t, I’ll be hungry.

examples.ja: 旅行に出かけたら出かけたで疲れるし、家にいたらいたで退屈する。
examples.romaji: Ryokō ni dekaketara dekaketa de tsukareru shi, ie ni itara itada de taikutsu suru.
examples.meaningEn: If I go on a trip, I'll get tired, but if I stay home, I'll be bored.

title: Verb たら きりがない (Verb tara kiri ga nai)
formation: Verb-た form + ら + きりがない
shortExplanationEn: Indicates an endless or unending cycle once an action is started; 'if one starts doing something, it will not stop.'
longExplanationEn: The たらきりがない grammar pattern is used when something negative or undesirable continues indefinitely once it begins. The implication is that you should avoid initiating the action because it’ll just go on and on.

examples.ja: このエンジンを修理したら、またすぐ壊れて、直したらきりがない。
examples.romaji: Kono enjin o shuuri shitara, mata sugu kowarete, naoshitara kiri ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: If I fix this engine, it breaks again, and if I fix it again, it never ends.

examples.ja: 飲み始めたらきりがない。
examples.romaji: Nomihajimetara kiri ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: Once I start drinking, I never stop.

examples.ja: 彼女にお金を貸したら、きりがない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ni okane o kashitara, kiri ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: If I lend her money, there will be no end to it.

examples.ja: 彼の話を聞き始めたらきりがない。
examples.romaji: Kare no hanashi o kikihajimetara kiri ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: Once you start listening to his stories, it never ends.

title: Verb たら最後 (〜tara saigo)
formation: Verb-た form + ら最後
shortExplanationEn: Expresses that once a certain action happens, the situation changes irreversibly—usually in a negative way.
longExplanationEn: The たら最後 grammar point indicates that once an action (in た-form) occurs, there is no going back and an unpleasant or unavoidable state follows. Often translated as 'once...then it's all over' or 'the moment...there’s no turning back.'

examples.ja: このゲームを始めたら最後、時間の経つのを忘れてしまいます。
examples.romaji: Kono gēmu o hajimetara saigo, jikan no tatsu no o wasurete shimaimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Once you start this game, you completely lose track of time.

examples.ja: 彼はビールを一杯飲み始めたら最後、止まらなくなります。
examples.romaji: Kare wa bīru o ippai nomihajimetara saigo, tomaranaku narimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Once he starts drinking a beer, he can't stop.

examples.ja: この本を読み始めたら最後、一気に読破してしまいました。
examples.romaji: Kono hon o yomihajimetara saigo, ikki ni dokuha shite shimaimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Once I started reading this book, I ended up finishing it in one go.

examples.ja: 彼を怒らせたら最後、なかなか許してもらえないよ。
examples.romaji: Kare o okorasetara saigo, nakanaka yurushite moraenai yo.
examples.meaningEn: Once you make him angry, he won't forgive you so easily.

title: Verb つ Verb つ (Verb tsu Verb tsu)
formation: Verb-ますstem + つ + Verb-ますstem + つ
shortExplanationEn: Expresses two alternating actions happening repeatedly.
longExplanationEn: The 〜つ〜つ pattern is used with two verb ます-stems to express two ongoing, repeated or alternating actions. For example, 'push and pull,' 'go and come back,' 'eat and play,' etc. The same subject is typically implied for both verbs.

examples.ja: 朝から晩まで働きつ勉強しつで、疲れてしまった。
examples.romaji: Asa kara ban made hataraki tsu benkyō shi tsu de, tsukarete shimatta.
examples.meaningEn: I’ve been working and studying from morning till night, and now I’m exhausted.

examples.ja: 彼は話しつ笑いつして楽しんでいた。
examples.romaji: Kare wa hanashi tsu warai tsu shite tanoshinde ita.
examples.meaningEn: He was talking and laughing, having a good time.

examples.ja: 子供たちは食べつ遊びつしていた。
examples.romaji: Kodomo-tachi wa tabe tsu asobi tsu shite ita.
examples.meaningEn: The children were eating and playing.

examples.ja: 私は読みつ書きつして過ごした。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa yomi tsu kaki tsu shite sugoshita.
examples.meaningEn: I spent my time reading and writing.

title: Verb てからというもの (Verb te kara to iu mono)
formation: Verb-て form + からというもの
shortExplanationEn: Expresses that ever since a certain event happened, a condition has continued until now.
longExplanationEn: The てからというもの grammar point indicates that from the time a certain action happened, a significant change or condition has persisted continuously. It emphasizes that the new situation contrasts with what came before.

examples.ja: 彼女と別れてからというもの、一人で過ごす時間が増えました。
examples.romaji: Kanojo to wakarete kara to iu mono, hitori de sugosu jikan ga fuemashita.
examples.meaningEn: Ever since I broke up with her, I've spent more time alone.

examples.ja: 子供が生まれてからというもの、自由な時間がなくなった。
examples.romaji: Kodomo ga umarete kara to iu mono, jiyū na jikan ga nakunatta.
examples.meaningEn: Since the baby was born, I have had no free time.

examples.ja: 新しい仕事を始めてからというもの、毎日忙しくなりました。
examples.romaji: Atarashii shigoto o hajimete kara to iu mono, mainichi isogashiku narimashita.
examples.meaningEn: Ever since I started my new job, I've been busy every day.
