examples.ja: 大学に入ってからというもの、スポーツをする時間がなくなった。
examples.romaji: Daigaku ni haitte kara to iu mono, supōtsu o suru jikan ga nakunatta.
examples.meaningEn: I haven’t had time to play sports since I entered university.

title: Verb てこそ (Verb te koso)
formation: Verb-て form + こそ
shortExplanationEn: Emphasizes 'only by doing something, a certain result or realization can occur.'
longExplanationEn: The ～てこそ grammar point is used to highlight that only by performing the verb in て-form can a certain important outcome be achieved or recognized. It often translates as 'only by (doing)' or 'only when (doing).'

examples.ja: 実際に使ってこそ、その価値を理解できる。
examples.romaji: Jissai ni tsukatte koso, sono kachi o rikai dekiru.
examples.meaningEn: You can only understand its value by actually using it.

examples.ja: 自分で経験してこそ、大切なことが学べる。
examples.romaji: Jibun de keiken shite koso, taisetsu na koto ga manaberu.
examples.meaningEn: Only by experiencing it yourself can you learn what’s truly important.

examples.ja: 失敗してこそ、成功の味がわかる。
examples.romaji: Shippai shite koso, seikō no aji ga wakaru.
examples.meaningEn: Only by failing do you really understand the taste of success.

examples.ja: 努力してこそ、何かを成し遂げることができる。
examples.romaji: Doryoku shite koso, nanika o nashitogeru koto ga dekiru.
examples.meaningEn: Only through making an effort can you accomplish anything.

title: Verb ては (～te wa)
formation: Verb-て form + は
shortExplanationEn: Expresses a repeated pattern or cycle, often implying a frustrating outcome; 'whenever X happens, Y happens again.'
longExplanationEn: The grammar ～ては is used to show that whenever one action or situation occurs, another action or situation repeatedly follows. It can also be used in an alternating pattern: 'do this, then that, then this, then that.'

examples.ja: 彼女に会っては、いつも緊張してしまいます。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ni atte wa, itsumo kinchō shite shimaimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whenever I meet her, I get nervous.

examples.ja: この部屋を掃除しては乱雑になり、片づけては散らかる。
examples.romaji: Kono heya o sōji shite wa konzatsu ni nari, katadzukete wa chirakaru.
examples.meaningEn: Whenever I clean this room, it gets messy again, and whenever I tidy it, it gets cluttered again.

examples.ja: 運動しては食べ、食べては運動する生活が続いている。
examples.romaji: Undō shite wa tabe, tabete wa undō suru seikatsu ga tsudzuite iru.
examples.meaningEn: I keep exercising and then eating, and then eating and exercising.

examples.ja: 勉強しては寝て、寝ては勉強する日々です。
examples.romaji: Benkyō shite wa nete, nete wa benkyō suru hibi desu.
examples.meaningEn: My days are spent studying and then sleeping, and then sleeping and studying.

title: Verb ては Verb (~ te wa ~)
formation: Verb-て form + は + Verb
shortExplanationEn: Expresses a repeated pattern of action; translates as 'every time', 'whenever', etc.
longExplanationEn: This grammar point is used when you want to express a repeated or cyclical pattern of actions or events. It can be translated as 'every time', 'whenever', or 'if at all'. The typical formation is a verb in the て-form, followed by は, and then another verb (often in the same tense or a continuing action).

examples.ja: 私が通勤しては、電子書籍を読んでいます。
examples.romaji: Watashi ga tsūkin shite wa, denshi shoseki o yonde imasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whenever I commute, I read e-books.

examples.ja: 友達が部屋に来ては、ゲームをしています。
examples.romaji: Tomodachi ga heya ni kite wa, gēmu o shite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: Every time my friend comes to my room, we play games.

examples.ja: 彼が酒を飲んでは、運転を止めさせています。
examples.romaji: Kare ga sake o nonde wa, unten o tome sasete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whenever he drinks alcohol, I make him stop driving.

examples.ja: 私が新しい曲を作っては、すぐに録音します。
examples.romaji: Watashi ga atarashii kyoku o tsukutte wa, sugu ni rokuon shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Every time I create a new song, I record it immediately.

title: Verb てまでも (~ temademo)
formation: Verb-て form + までも
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'even if it means doing...', 'no matter what it takes'.
longExplanationEn: This grammar point is used when the speaker wants to emphasize the lengths they would go to in order to achieve something. It attaches to the verb's て-form to mean 'even if it means doing X', 'even at the cost of X', or 'no matter what it takes'.

examples.ja: 試験に合格するために、寝る時間を削ってまでも勉強します。
examples.romaji: Shiken ni gōkaku suru tame ni, neru jikan o kezutte mademo benkyō shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I will study to pass the exam, even if it means cutting down on sleep.

examples.ja: 彼女と結婚できるなら、どんな苦労をしてまでもお金を貯めたい。
examples.romaji: Kanojo to kekkon dekiru nara, donna kurō o shite mademo okane o tametai.
examples.meaningEn: If I can marry her, I'll save money no matter what hardships it takes.

examples.ja: この会社で働きたいので、長時間かけてまでも通います。
examples.romaji: Kono kaisha de hatarakitai node, chōjikan kakete mademo kayoimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Because I want to work at this company, I'll commute even if it means spending hours traveling.

examples.ja: 彼の快復のためなら、何をしてまでも支えたい。
examples.romaji: Kare no kaifuku no tame nara, nani o shite mademo sasaetai.
examples.meaningEn: For his recovery, I'll support him no matter what it takes.

title: Verb てみせる (Verb te miseru)
formation: Verb-て form + みせる
shortExplanationEn: 'Show by doing', 'demonstrate', 'prove by action'.
longExplanationEn: The ～てみせる grammar point is used to express the action of demonstrating or proving something by actually doing it. It can be translated as 'show by doing', 'demonstrate', or 'prove by action' in English.

examples.ja: 彼はそのパズルを解いてみせた。
examples.romaji: Kare wa sono pazuru o toite miseta.
examples.meaningEn: He solved the puzzle to show me.

examples.ja: 私はこの仕事ができることをやってみせます。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa kono shigoto ga dekiru koto o yatte misemasu.
examples.meaningEn: I will do this job to prove that I can.

examples.ja: 彼女は試験に合格するために一生懸命勉強してみせました。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa shiken ni gōkaku suru tame ni isshōkenmei benkyō shite misemashita.
examples.meaningEn: She studied hard to prove she could pass the exam.

examples.ja: 彼は自分が何も恐れていないと跳んでみせた。
examples.romaji: Kare wa jibun ga nanimo osorete inai to tonde miseta.
examples.meaningEn: He jumped to show he wasn’t afraid of anything.

title: Verb てやまない (Verb te yamanai)
formation: Verb-て form + やまない (common with certain verbs)
shortExplanationEn: Used with certain emotional/mental verbs to show a strong, unceasing emotion: 'can't stop', 'never cease to'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar ～てやまない is typically used with verbs like 願う, 祈る, 愛する, 尊敬する, 期待する, etc., expressing a deep, continuous emotional state (love, respect, hope, prayer). It emphasizes that the feeling 'never ceases.'

examples.ja: 私は母を思ってやまない。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa haha o omotte yamanai.
examples.meaningEn: I can't stop thinking of my mother.

examples.ja: 彼は彼女を愛してやまない。
examples.romaji: Kare wa kanojo o aishite yamanai.
examples.meaningEn: He loves her beyond measure.

examples.ja: 彼は日本の文化を尊敬してやまない。
examples.romaji: Kare wa Nihon no bunka o sonkei shite yamanai.
examples.meaningEn: He has unceasing respect for Japanese culture.

examples.ja: 彼女の成功を願ってやまない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no seikō o negatte yamanai.
examples.meaningEn: I can’t stop hoping for her success.

title: Verb ないではおかない (~ nai de wa okanai)
formation: Verb-ない form + ではおかない
shortExplanationEn: Means 'cannot help but do', 'will definitely do', or 'is bound to happen'.
longExplanationEn: The pattern Verb ないではおかない indicates that something definitely occurs as a logical or emotional consequence. It can be translated as 'cannot not do', 'can’t help but do', or 'will inevitably do'. The verb is put in the negative form followed by ないではおかない.

examples.ja: 彼女は毎回遅刻をしては、先生に怒られないではおかない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa maikai chikoku o shite wa, sensei ni okorarenaide wa okanai.
examples.meaningEn: Every time she's late, she inevitably gets scolded by the teacher.

examples.ja: 美味しいものがあれば、彼は食べないではおかない。
examples.romaji: Oishii mono ga areba, kare wa tabenai de wa okanai.
examples.meaningEn: If there's delicious food, he can't help but eat it.

examples.ja: この小説は感動的で、読めば涙を流さないではおかない。
examples.romaji: Kono shōsetsu wa kandōteki de, yomeba namida o nagasanai de wa okanai.
examples.meaningEn: This novel is so moving that you can't help but shed tears when you read it.

examples.ja: 毎日練習しているから、試合では勝たないではおかないだろう。
examples.romaji: Mainichi renshū shite iru kara, shiai de wa katanai de wa okanai darō.
examples.meaningEn: He practices every day, so he's bound to win the match.

title: Verb ないではすまない (Verb nai dewa sumanai)
formation: Verb-ない form + ではすまない
shortExplanationEn: 'Cannot end without doing', 'must do'—an unavoidable moral or social necessity.
longExplanationEn: The grammar Verb ないではすまない expresses that something must be done (often for moral, social, or personal reasons) and cannot be left undone. It implies an obligation or inevitability, akin to 'I can't get away without doing it.'

examples.ja: 失敗をしたら、謝らないではすまない。
examples.romaji: Shippai o shitara, ayamaranai de wa sumanai.
examples.meaningEn: If you make a mistake, you have to apologize—there’s no way around it.

examples.ja: この問題は重大なので、報告しないではすまない。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wa jūdai na node, hōkoku shinai de wa sumanai.
examples.meaningEn: This issue is so serious that I must report it.

examples.ja: この映画はとても感動的で、泣かないではすまない。
examples.romaji: Kono eiga wa totemo kandōteki de, nakanai de wa sumanai.
examples.meaningEn: This movie is so moving that you can’t help but cry.

examples.ja: 子供が間違えたら、教えないではすまない。
examples.romaji: Kodomo ga machigaetara, oshienai de wa sumanai.
examples.meaningEn: If a child makes a mistake, you must correct them—there’s no avoiding it.

title: Verb ないまでも (Verb nai made mo)
formation: Verb-ない form + までも
shortExplanationEn: 'Even if not to the extent of...', 'although not completely...'.
longExplanationEn: The pattern ないまでも is used when acknowledging that something doesn’t reach a certain extreme, but at least some part or degree of it applies. Often translated as 'even if not entirely...' or 'though not as far as...'.

examples.ja: 全てを理解しないまでも、基本的なことは分かってほしい。
examples.romaji: Subete o rikai shinai made mo, kihonteki na koto wa wakatte hoshii.
examples.meaningEn: Even if you don't understand everything, I'd like you at least to grasp the basics.

examples.ja: 会えないまでも、電話で声を聞きたい。
examples.romaji: Aenai made mo, denwa de koe o kikitai.
examples.meaningEn: Even if we can’t meet, I still want to hear your voice over the phone.

examples.ja: 毎日は無理にしても、週に一度くらいはジョギングを続けたい。
examples.romaji: Mainichi wa muri ni shite mo, shū ni ichido kurai wa jogingu o tsuzuketai.
examples.meaningEn: Even if I can't do it every day, I want to at least keep jogging once a week.

examples.ja: 全員に気に入られないまでも、誠実に向き合いたい。
examples.romaji: Zen'in ni ki ni irarenai made mo, seijitsu ni mukiai tai.
examples.meaningEn: Even if not everyone will like me, I still want to face them sincerely.

title: Verb ないものだろうか (Verb nai mono darou ka)
formation: Verb-negative (often negative potential) + ものだろうか
shortExplanationEn: Expressing hope or wonder if something can be done: 'Isn't there a way...?', 'Can't we...?'
longExplanationEn: This grammar point is used when the speaker expresses the hope, wish, or wonder if an action could be possible. It creates the nuance of 'Isn't there some way...?' or 'Can't I/you/we...?' in English, often with the negative potential form.

examples.ja: もっと時間があれば、ここに来られないものだろうか。
examples.romaji: Motto jikan ga areba, koko ni korarenai mono darou ka.
examples.meaningEn: If I had more time, wouldn't it be possible to come here?

examples.ja: 彼女にもっとよく説明すれば、理解できないものだろうか。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ni motto yoku setsumei sureba, rikai dekinai mono darou ka.
examples.meaningEn: If I explain it better to her, wouldn't she understand?

examples.ja: この問題を解決する方法はないものだろうか。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai o kaiketsu suru houhou wa nai mono darou ka.
examples.meaningEn: Isn't there a way to solve this problem?

examples.ja: 自分の間違いを認められないものだろうか。
examples.romaji: Jibun no machigai o mitomerarenai mono darou ka.
examples.meaningEn: Can't I admit my own mistakes?

title: Verb ないものでもない (Verb nai mono demo nai)
formation: Verb-negative form (ない form) + ものでもない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea that it's not entirely impossible or non-existent: 'It's not that I don't...', 'It's not that there aren't...'
longExplanationEn: The grammar point Verbないものでもない is used to express that a situation is not completely impossible or nonexistent. Literally, it can be translated as 'it's not that there aren't...' or 'it's not that I never...'. It implies that some possibility or partial truth exists.

examples.ja: 旅行に行きたいと思わないものでもない。
examples.romaji: Ryokou ni ikitai to omowanai mono demo nai.
examples.meaningEn: It's not that I never want to travel.

examples.ja: 彼の言ったこと全部が間違いないものでもない。
examples.romaji: Kare no itta koto zenbu ga machigai nai mono demo nai.
examples.meaningEn: It's not that everything he said is completely free from mistakes.

examples.ja: その問題が解けないものでもない。
examples.romaji: Sono mondai ga tokenai mono demo nai.
examples.meaningEn: It's not that the problem can't be solved.

examples.ja: 彼に会いたくないものでもない。
examples.romaji: Kare ni aitakunai mono demo nai.
examples.meaningEn: It's not that I don't want to see him.

title: Verb の ない Noun (~ no nai ~)
formation: Verb in negative form + の + Noun
shortExplanationEn: Used to modify a noun with a negative verb form; 'a Noun without (Verb)'.
longExplanationEn: VerbのないNoun modifies a noun by describing the absence of the verb's action. It indicates 'a Noun where the action does not happen' or 'a Noun without that action.'

examples.ja: 笑いのない人生は、つまらない。
examples.romaji: Warai no nai jinsei wa, tsumaranai.
examples.meaningEn: A life without laughter is boring.

examples.ja: 水のない場所には、人は住めない。
examples.romaji: Mizu no nai basho ni wa, hito wa sumenai.
examples.meaningEn: People cannot live in a place without water.

examples.ja: 言葉のない世界は、想像するのが難しい。
examples.romaji: Kotoba no nai sekai wa, souzou suru no ga muzukashii.
examples.meaningEn: It's hard to imagine a world without words.

examples.ja: 愛のない結婚は、長続きしない。
examples.romaji: Ai no nai kekkon wa, nagatsudzuki shinai.
examples.meaningEn: A marriage without love will not last.

title: Verb ば きりがない (〜ba kiri ga nai)
formation: Verb-conditional form (ば) + きりがない
shortExplanationEn: Indicates there's no end once you start listing, doing, or saying something: 'If one starts to..., there will be no end.'
longExplanationEn: The pattern ～ばきりがない is used when expressing that if you start an action—like listing examples or complaining—there is no end. It literally means 'if (you) do X, there's no limit/end.'

examples.ja: 悪口を言い始めればきりがない。
examples.romaji: Waruguchi o iihajimereba kiri ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: Once I start speaking ill of someone, there's no end.

examples.ja: 彼の欠点を数えればきりがない。
examples.romaji: Kare no ketten o kazoereba kiri ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: If I start counting his flaws, there will be no end.

examples.ja: この国の美しい場所は、挙げればきりがない。
examples.romaji: Kono kuni no utsukushii basho wa, agereba kiri ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: If I start listing this country's beautiful places, there's no end.

examples.ja: ほかにも例を挙げればきりがない。
examples.romaji: Hoka ni mo rei o agereba kiri ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: If I start giving more examples, there will be no end.

title: Verb もしないで (~ mo shinai de)
formation: Verb (masu stem) + もしないで
shortExplanationEn: Means 'without even doing'.
longExplanationEn: This pattern is used to express that one has not done (or not even tried to do) some action before proceeding to do something else. It emphasizes doing something 'without even' doing the prior action.

examples.ja: 調べもしないで、答えを書きました。
examples.romaji: Shirabe mo shinai de, kotae o kakimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I wrote the answer without even investigating.

examples.ja: 彼は試験を勉強もしないで、テストに合格した。
examples.romaji: Kare wa shiken o benkyou mo shinai de, tesuto ni goukaku shita.
examples.meaningEn: He passed the test without even studying.

examples.ja: 彼女はその本を読みもしないで、レポートを書いた。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa sono hon o yomi mo shinai de, repooto o kaita.
examples.meaningEn: She wrote her report without even reading the book.

examples.ja: 準備もしないで、プレゼンテーションを始めました。
examples.romaji: Junbi mo shinai de, purezenteeshon o hajimemashita.
examples.meaningEn: He started the presentation without even preparing.

title: Verb やしない (~ yashinai)
formation: Verb (masu stem) + やしない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses a strong denial or refusal: 'I would never...', 'No way I would...'.
longExplanationEn: This grammar adds a nuance of strong denial or dismissal of the idea that the speaker (or someone) would do the action. Formed by adding やしない to the verb's stem, it often appears in casual or spoken language.

examples.ja: こんな時に泣きやしない。
examples.romaji: Konna toki ni naki-yashinai.
examples.meaningEn: I would never cry at a time like this.

examples.ja: 私が彼を助けやしない。
examples.romaji: Watashi ga kare o tasuke-yashinai.
examples.meaningEn: There's no way I'd help him.

examples.ja: 彼がそんなことを言いやしない。
examples.romaji: Kare ga sonna koto o ii-yashinai.
examples.meaningEn: He would never say such a thing.

examples.ja: 私がそんな高価なものを買いやしない。
examples.romaji: Watashi ga sonna kouka na mono o kai-yashinai.
examples.meaningEn: There's no way I'd buy something so expensive.

title: Verb よう (~ you / ~ you ni)
formation: Verb (dictionary or negative form) + ように (する / etc.)
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'the way/manner of doing something' or 'in order to / so that / try to'.
longExplanationEn: This usage of よう most commonly appears as 「Verb辞書形 + ように」. It can mean 'in order to do something', 'so that something happens', or 'in the way someone does something'. It is also used in expressions like ～ようにする (to make an effort to) or ～ように言う (to tell someone to do something).

examples.ja: この問題を解くように努力します。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai o toku you ni doryoku shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I will try to solve this problem.

examples.ja: 彼が言ったようにやりましょう。
examples.romaji: Kare ga itta you ni yarimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Let's do it the way he said.

examples.ja: 毎日走るようにしています。
examples.romaji: Mainichi hashiru you ni shiteimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I make it a point to run every day.

examples.ja: 自分の心を大切にするように。
examples.romaji: Jibun no kokoro o taisetsu ni suru you ni.
examples.meaningEn: Try to take care of your own heart.

title: Verb ようか Verbるまいか (Verb you ka Verb ru mai ka)
formation: Verb-volitional form + か + Verb-volitional negative form (まい) + か
shortExplanationEn: Expresses indecision: 'Should I do it or not?'.
longExplanationEn: This grammar shows hesitation about whether to do something or not. The volitional form plus まい (negative volitional) suggests an internal debate about two opposite choices of the *same* verb.

examples.ja: 彼に話そうか話すまいか、本当に迷っている。
examples.romaji: Kare ni hanasou ka hanasu mai ka, hontou ni mayotte iru.
examples.meaningEn: I'm really unsure whether to talk to him or not.

examples.ja: 試験を受けようか受けまいか迷っています。
examples.romaji: Shiken o ukeyou ka ukemai ka mayotte imasu.
examples.meaningEn: I'm wondering whether to take the exam or not.

examples.ja: あの人に謝ろうか謝るまいか考えています。
examples.romaji: Ano hito ni ayamarou ka ayamaru mai ka kangaete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: I'm considering whether to apologize to that person or not.

examples.ja: 進学しようかしまいか悩んでいる。
examples.romaji: Shingaku shiyou ka shimai ka nayande iru.
examples.meaningEn: I'm worried about whether to continue my studies or not.

title: Verb ようが Verb るまいが (Verb you ga Verb ru mai ga)
formation: Verb-volitional form + が + Verb-negative volitional form + が
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'whether or not', 'no matter if something happens or not'.
longExplanationEn: Verbようが Verbるまいが indicates that whatever happens—whether the action is done or not—the outcome remains unchanged or the speaker's stance remains the same.

examples.ja: 彼が来ようが来るまいが、パーティーは始まります。
examples.romaji: Kare ga koyou ga kurumai ga, paatii wa hajimarimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether he comes or not, the party will start.

examples.ja: 勉強しようがしまいが、テストの結果は変わらない。
examples.romaji: Benkyou shiyou ga shimai ga, tesuto no kekka wa kawaranai.
examples.meaningEn: Whether you study or not, the test results won't change.

examples.ja: 雨が降ろうが降るまいが、ピクニックに行きます。
examples.romaji: Ame ga furou ga furumai ga, pikunikku ni ikimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether it rains or not, we're going on a picnic.

examples.ja: 暑かろうが寒かろうが、毎日ジョギングします。
examples.romaji: Atsukarou ga samukarou ga, mainichi jogingu shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether it's hot or cold, I jog every day.

title: Verb ようがない (〜you ga nai)
formation: Verb (masu stem) + ようがない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'no way to do something,' 'impossible to do'.
longExplanationEn: 〜ようがない is used to say that there's no way or method to do something, even if you wanted to.

examples.ja: 電車が混んでいて、動きようがない。
examples.romaji: Densha ga konde ite, ugoki you ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: The train is so crowded that I can't move.

examples.ja: 彼はとても早く走るので、追いつきようがない。
examples.romaji: Kare wa totemo hayaku hashiru no de, oitsuki you ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: He runs so fast, there's no way I can catch up.

examples.ja: 私には理解しようがない。
examples.romaji: Watashi ni wa rikai shiyou ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: There's no way for me to understand it.

examples.ja: どうやっても、これを直しようがない。
examples.romaji: Dou yatte mo, kore o naoshi you ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: No matter what I try, there's no way to fix this.

title: Verb ようと Verbる まいと (Verb you to Verb ru mai to)
formation: Verb-volitional + と + Verb-negative volitional (～まい) + と
shortExplanationEn: This structure indicates that regardless of whether one does (verb) or not, the outcome/attitude remains unchanged.
longExplanationEn: The expression 'Verb-volitional form + と + Verb-negative volitional (～まい) + と' means 'regardless of whether one does X or not, it doesn't change the situation'. It shows the speaker's firm stance or the unchanging nature of some outcome.

examples.ja: あなたが来ようと来るまいと、パーティーは予定通りに始まります。
examples.romaji: Anata ga koyou to kuru mai to, paatii wa yotei doori ni hajimarimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether you come or not, the party will start as scheduled.

examples.ja: 彼があそこに行こうと行くまいと、私たちは止めることができません。
examples.romaji: Kare ga asoko ni ikou to iku mai to, watashitachi wa tomeru koto ga dekimasen.
examples.meaningEn: Whether he tries to go there or not, we can't stop him.

examples.ja: 彼女が謝ろうと謝るまいと、事実は変わらない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga ayamarou to ayamaru mai to, jijitsu wa kawaranai.
examples.meaningEn: Whether she apologizes or not, the fact won't change.

examples.ja: 試験を受けようと受けるまいと、親として私はあなたを支えます。
examples.romaji: Shiken o ukeyou to ukeru mai to, oya to shite watashi wa anata o sasaemasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether you take the exam or not, I will support you as your parent.

title: Verb ようにも (〜you ni mo)
formation: Verb-volitional form + にも + (reason/potential negative, etc.)
shortExplanationEn: Used to express 'even though one wants to do something, one can't for some reason'.
longExplanationEn: The ～ようにも grammar point indicates that a person wants or tries to do something (volitional form), but for some reason cannot. Often, there's a second clause explaining what prevents the action.

examples.ja: もう少し速く走ろうにも、足が痛くて無理だ。
examples.romaji: Mou sukoshi hayaku hashirou ni mo, ashi ga itakute muri da.
examples.meaningEn: I'd like to run a bit faster, but I can’t because my leg hurts.

examples.ja: 彼に話そうにも、彼は私を無視している。
examples.romaji: Kare ni hanasou ni mo, kare wa watashi o mushi shite iru.
examples.meaningEn: I want to talk to him, but he's ignoring me.

examples.ja: もっと勉強しようにも、時間がない。
examples.romaji: Motto benkyou shiyou ni mo, jikan ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: I want to study more, but I have no time.

examples.ja: 彼女に謝ろうにも、彼女はもう怒っている。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ni ayamarou ni mo, kanojo wa mou okotte iru.
examples.meaningEn: I would like to apologize to her, but she's already angry.

title: Verb ようにも Verb れない (〜you ni mo 〜renai)
formation: Verb-volitional form + にも + (potential negative form / reason)
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the inability to do something even if one wants/tries.
longExplanationEn: This grammar shows that you want (or try) to do something (volitional form), but cannot due to some obstacle. Often it appears as 'Verb-volitional + にも + Verb (potential negative)' or simply 'Verb-volitional + にも + (reason you can't)'.

examples.ja: 彼を信じようにも信じられない証拠がある。
examples.romaji: Kare o shinjiyou ni mo shinjirarenai shouko ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: Even if I want to believe him, there's evidence that prevents me from doing so.

examples.ja: 早く出発しようにも車が故障してしまった。
examples.romaji: Hayaku shuppatsu shiyou ni mo kuruma ga koshou shite shimatta.
examples.meaningEn: Even if I want to leave early, my car has broken down.

examples.ja: 彼に電話しようにも電話番号を知らない。
examples.romaji: Kare ni denwa shiyou ni mo denwa bangou o shiranai.
examples.meaningEn: I want to call him, but I don’t know his phone number.

examples.ja: もっと勉強しようにも時間がない。
examples.romaji: Motto benkyou shiyou ni mo jikan ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: I want to study more, but there is no time.

title: Verb ようもない (~you mo nai)
formation: Verb-ます stem + ようもない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'it's impossible to do something'.
longExplanationEn: ～ようもない typically attaches to the verb's ます-stem, adding ようもない to indicate that there is 'no way' or 'no method' to perform the action. It’s the same nuance as 'Verbようがない' in many references.

examples.ja: この問題は解きようもない。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wa toki you mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: It's impossible to solve this problem.

examples.ja: これ以上我慢しようもない。
examples.romaji: Kore ijou gaman shiyou mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: It's impossible to endure any further.

examples.ja: 彼には通じようもない。
examples.romaji: Kare ni wa tsuujiyou mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: There's no way to get through to him.

examples.ja: その薬は飲みようもない。
examples.romaji: Sono kusuri wa nomiyou mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: It's impossible to take (swallow) that medicine.

title: Verbる がままに (〜ga mama ni)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + がままに
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'letting something happen as it is'; 'just as something does'.
longExplanationEn: がままに describes an action happening exactly 'as is,' without intervention or change, often suggesting doing something passively or naturally.

examples.ja: 窓を開けるがままにしておいたら、風が部屋に入ってきました。
examples.romaji: Mado o akeru ga mama ni shite oitara, kaze ga heya ni haitte kimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I left the window open as it was, and the wind came into the room.

examples.ja: 彼女は涙を流すがままに、ただ黙って座っていた。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa namida o nagasu ga mama ni, tada damatte suwatte ita.
examples.meaningEn: She just sat quietly, letting her tears flow.

examples.ja: 子供たちは笑うがままに、公園で走り回っていた。
examples.romaji: Kodomo-tachi wa warau ga mama ni, kouen de hashirimawatte ita.
examples.meaningEn: The children ran around the park, laughing freely as they pleased.

examples.ja: 日が沈むがままに、私たちは海辺で話し続けました。
examples.romaji: Hi ga shizumu ga mama ni, watashitachi wa umibe de hanashi tsudzukemashita.
examples.meaningEn: We kept talking by the sea, letting the sun set as it would.

title: Verbる が早いか (verb-ru ga hayai ka)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + が早いか
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'as soon as...' or 'the moment when...'.
longExplanationEn: Verbる が早いか indicates that something happens immediately after something else. It's similar to 'as soon as...' or 'no sooner than...'.

examples.ja: 部屋に入るが早いか、彼はテレビをつけた。
examples.romaji: Heya ni hairu ga hayai ka, kare wa terebi o tsuketa.
examples.meaningEn: No sooner had he entered the room than he turned on the TV.

examples.ja: 電車に乗るが早いか、私はすぐに眠ってしまった。
examples.romaji: Densha ni noru ga hayai ka, watashi wa sugu ni nemutte shimatta.
examples.meaningEn: As soon as I got on the train, I immediately fell asleep.

examples.ja: 彼女が見るが早いか、すぐに泣き始めた。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga miru ga hayai ka, sugu ni naki hajimeta.
examples.meaningEn: The moment she looked, she began to cry.

examples.ja: 給料をもらうが早いか、全て使い果たしてしまった。
examples.romaji: Kyuuryou o morau ga hayai ka, subete tsukaihatashite shimatta.
examples.meaningEn: No sooner had I received my salary than I spent it all.

title: Verbる くらいなら (〜ru kurai nara)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + くらいなら
shortExplanationEn: Expresses preference to avoid something: 'rather than...', 'if it comes to doing X, I'd rather...'.
longExplanationEn: This grammar is used when the speaker would rather not do something or chooses another option rather than face the stated action. It’s often translated as 'rather than... I'd prefer...' or 'if it comes down to doing X, I'd rather...'.

examples.ja: 一人で行くくらいなら、家にいるほうがましです。
examples.romaji: Hitori de iku kurai nara, ie ni iru hou ga mashi desu.
examples.meaningEn: I'd rather stay at home than go alone.

examples.ja: 負けるくらいなら、試合をしないほうがいい。
examples.romaji: Makeru kurai nara, shiai o shinai hou ga ii.
examples.meaningEn: Rather than lose, it's better not to compete.

examples.ja: 遅刻するくらいなら、一日休んだほうがいい。
examples.romaji: Chikoku suru kurai nara, ichinichi yasunda hou ga ii.
examples.meaningEn: It's better to take a day off than to be late.

examples.ja: 痛い目に遭うくらいなら、そのリスクは避けます。
examples.romaji: Itai me ni au kurai nara, sono risuku wa sakemasu.
examples.meaningEn: I'd rather avoid that risk than suffer the consequences.

title: Verbる こと なし に (Verb-ru koto nashi ni)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + ことなしに
shortExplanationEn: Used to express 'without doing something'.
longExplanationEn: Verbることなしに indicates doing something else 'without' performing the stated action. It can imply skipping or omitting the verb action entirely.

examples.ja: 試験を勉強することなしに、合格しました。
examples.romaji: Shiken o benkyou suru koto nashi ni, goukaku shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I passed the exam without studying.

examples.ja: 試着することなしに、服を買いました。
examples.romaji: Shichaku suru koto nashi ni, fuku o kaimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I bought the clothes without trying them on.

examples.ja: 彼は働くことなしに生活しています。
examples.romaji: Kare wa hataraku koto nashi ni seikatsu shite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: He is living without working.

examples.ja: 手続きをすることなしに、退職しました。
examples.romaji: Tetsuzuki o suru koto nashi ni, taishoku shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I retired without going through any procedures.

title: Verbる ことのないように (Verb-ru koto no nai you ni)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + ことのないように
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the desire or effort to prevent something: 'so as not to...', 'to avoid...'.
longExplanationEn: Verbる ことのないように is used to show that one is trying or intending to prevent some action from happening. It's like 'so that this doesn't happen' or 'in order not to...'.

examples.ja: 車をなくすことのないように、駐車場で必ずロックしましょう。
examples.romaji: Kuruma o nakusu koto no nai you ni, chuushajou de kanarazu rokku shimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Lock your car in the parking lot so you don't lose it.

examples.ja: 風邪を引くことのないように、手をよく洗いましょう。
examples.romaji: Kaze o hiku koto no nai you ni, te o yoku araimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Wash your hands well so you don't catch a cold.

examples.ja: 試験に落ちることのないように、毎日勉強しましょう。
examples.romaji: Shiken ni ochiru koto no nai you ni, mainichi benkyou shimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Study every day so you don't fail the exam.

examples.ja: 間違いをすることのないように、仕事を慎重に行いましょう。
examples.romaji: Machigai o suru koto no nai you ni, shigoto o shinchou ni okonaimashou.
examples.meaningEn: Let's work carefully to avoid making mistakes.

title: Verbる ときりがない (verb-ru to kiri ga nai)
formation: Verb-casual + と + きりがない
shortExplanationEn: Used to indicate that something can go on endlessly or without limits.
longExplanationEn: The ～るときりがない grammar point is used to express that once you start doing the verb, there’s no end or limit. The phrase 'きりがない' means 'no end' or 'endless.'

examples.ja: 彼の悪口を言うときりがない。
examples.romaji: Kare no waruguchi o iu to kiri ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: If we start talking about his faults, there's no end.

examples.ja: その問題を考えるときりがない。
examples.romaji: Sono mondai o kangaeru to kiri ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: If you start thinking about that problem, there's no end.

examples.ja: 彼女の美点を挙げるときりがない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no biten o ageru to kiri ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: If you start listing her virtues, there would be no end.

examples.ja: この世でおいしいものを食べるときりがない。
examples.romaji: Kono yo de oishii mono o taberu to kiri ga nai.
examples.meaningEn: If you start eating all the delicious things in this world, there's no end.

title: Verbる ともなく Verb (Verb-ru tomonaku Verb)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + ともなく + Verb
shortExplanationEn: Used to express 'without a particular intention', 'unconsciously', or 'before one knows it'.
longExplanationEn: The phrase VerbるともなくVerb indicates doing something unintentionally or unconsciously— you just find yourself doing it without a clear aim or intention.

examples.ja: 遠くを見るともなく窓の外を眺めた。
examples.romaji: Tooku o miru tomonaku mado no soto o nagameta.
examples.meaningEn: I found myself gazing out the window, not really looking at anything in particular.

examples.ja: 知るともなく、彼が去ったことを感じた。
examples.romaji: Shiru tomonaku, kare ga satta koto o kanjita.
examples.meaningEn: Without really knowing, I felt that he had left.

examples.ja: 本を読むともなく、ページをめくっていた。
examples.romaji: Hon o yomu tomonaku, peiji o mekutte ita.
examples.meaningEn: Without intending to read, I was just flipping through the pages of the book.

examples.ja: 寝るともなく、ベッドに横になっていた。
examples.romaji: Neru tomonaku, beddo ni yoko ni natte ita.
examples.meaningEn: I was lying in bed without really intending to sleep.

title: Verbる ともなしに Verb (Verb-ru tomonashi ni Verb)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + ともなしに + Verb
shortExplanationEn: Expresses doing something unintentionally or without conscious thought.
longExplanationEn: VerbるともなしにVerb is used to show that you do some action without particularly intending to. Often you ‘end up doing’ something by habit or casually.

examples.ja: 本を読むともなしに眠ってしまった。
examples.romaji: Hon o yomu tomonashi ni nemutte shimatta.
examples.meaningEn: I ended up falling asleep while reading the book without really meaning to.

examples.ja: 軽い気持ちで手紙を書くともなしに、彼に本心を告白した。
examples.romaji: Karui kimochi de tegami o kaku tomonashi ni, kare ni honshin o kokuhaku shita.
examples.meaningEn: While writing a letter casually, I ended up confessing my true feelings to him.

examples.ja: 散歩をするともなしに、新しいカフェを見つけた。
examples.romaji: Sanpo o suru tomonashi ni, atarashii kafe o mitsuketa.
examples.meaningEn: While I was walking with no particular destination, I found a new cafe.

examples.ja: 洋服を整理するともなしに、昔の写真を見つけた。
examples.romaji: Youfuku o seiri suru tomonashi ni, mukashi no shashin o mitsuketa.
examples.meaningEn: As I was absentmindedly organizing clothes, I came across some old photos.

title: Verbる なり (Verb-ru nari)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + なり
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'as soon as' or 'the moment'.
longExplanationEn: Verbるなり indicates that the following action happens immediately after the verb action. It can be translated as 'no sooner than...', 'as soon as...', or 'the moment...'.

examples.ja: 家に帰るなり、すぐに寝た。
examples.romaji: Ie ni kaeru nari, sugu ni neta.
examples.meaningEn: As soon as I got home, I went to sleep.

examples.ja: 彼が駅に着くなり、電話をかけてきた。
examples.romaji: Kare ga eki ni tsuku nari, denwa o kakete kita.
examples.meaningEn: He called me the moment he arrived at the station.

examples.ja: ドアを開けるなり、犬が吠え始めた。
examples.romaji: Doa o akeru nari, inu ga hoe hajimeta.
examples.meaningEn: As soon as I opened the door, the dog started barking.

examples.ja: テストが終わるなり、彼は教室を出て行った。
examples.romaji: Tesuto ga owaru nari, kare wa kyoushitsu o dete itta.
examples.meaningEn: He left the classroom the moment the test ended.

title: Verbる にとどまらず～も (Verb-ru ni todomarazu ~ mo)
formation: Noun / Verb-dictionary form + にとどまらず + も/ほかのこと
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'not only… but also', 'not limited to…'.
longExplanationEn: にとどまらず is used to indicate that something goes beyond just the stated verb or noun. It can be translated as 'not just… but also…' or 'not limited to…'.

examples.ja: 彼は仕事にとどまらず、ボランティア活動もしている。
examples.romaji: Kare wa shigoto ni todomarazu, borantia katsudou mo shite iru.
examples.meaningEn: He does not just work; he also does volunteer activities.

examples.ja: この機能は写真撮影にとどまらず、動画撮影も可能です。
examples.romaji: Kono kinou wa shashin satsuei ni todomarazu, douga satsuei mo kanou desu.
examples.meaningEn: This function is not limited to taking photos; it can also record videos.

examples.ja: このレストランは日本料理にとどまらず、中華料理も提供しています。
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa Nihon ryouri ni todomarazu, Chuuka ryouri mo teikyou shiteimasu.
examples.meaningEn: This restaurant not only serves Japanese food, but also Chinese cuisine.

examples.ja: その企業は製造業にとどまらず、流通業務も手がけている。
examples.romaji: Sono kigyou wa seizougyou ni todomarazu, ryuutsuugyou mo tegakete iru.
examples.meaningEn: That company’s operations go beyond manufacturing; they also handle distribution.

title: Verbる にはあたらない (Verb-ru ni wa ataranai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + にはあたらない
shortExplanationEn: Used to say 'there is no need to...', 'it’s not necessary to...'.
longExplanationEn: Verbる にはあたらない expresses that something is not worth the reaction or there is no need to do the verb. It's somewhat formal and often used in writing.

examples.ja: パニックに陥るにはあたらない。
examples.romaji: Panikku ni ochiiru ni wa ataranai.
examples.meaningEn: There’s no need to panic.

examples.ja: 彼に謝るにはあたらない。
examples.romaji: Kare ni ayamaru ni wa ataranai.
examples.meaningEn: There’s no need to apologize to him.

examples.ja: そのようなことを心配するにはあたらない。
examples.romaji: Sono you na koto o shinpai suru ni wa ataranai.
examples.meaningEn: It’s not worth worrying about such things.

examples.ja: それについて深く考えるにはあたらない。
examples.romaji: Sore ni tsuite fukaku kangaeru ni wa ataranai.
examples.meaningEn: There’s no need to think too deeply about it.

title: Verbる にも (Verb-ru ni mo)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + にも + (reason it can't be done)
shortExplanationEn: Indicates that doing the verb is impossible or difficult, even if one wants to.
longExplanationEn: Verbる にも is often used with an additional clause explaining why the action can’t be done. It can be translated as 'even if (I) want to do X, I can’t (because...)'.

examples.ja: 明日雨が降ったとしても、傘を持つにも荷物が多すぎる。
examples.romaji: Ashita ame ga futta to shite mo, kasa o motsu ni mo nimotsu ga oosugiru.
examples.meaningEn: Even if it rains tomorrow, I have too much luggage to bring an umbrella.

examples.ja: 試験に落ちたが、もう一度受けるにもお金が足りない。
examples.romaji: Shiken ni ochita ga, mou ichido ukeru ni mo okane ga tarinai.
examples.meaningEn: I failed the exam, but I don’t have the money to take it again even if I wanted to.

examples.ja: 彼に会いたいと思っても、彼に会うにも難しい状況だ。
examples.romaji: Kare ni aitai to omotte mo, kare ni au ni mo muzukashii joukyou da.
examples.meaningEn: Even if I want to see him, it's a difficult situation to meet him.

examples.ja: この空港から彼女の家に行くにもタクシーが必要だ。
examples.romaji: Kono kuukou kara kanojo no ie ni iku ni mo takushii ga hitsuyou da.
examples.meaningEn: I'd need a taxi to get from this airport to her house, even if I wanted to go.

title: Verbる にも Verb れない (Verb-ru ni mo Verb-re nai)
formation: Verb-volitional + にも + Verb-potential negative
shortExplanationEn: Expresses: 'Even if I try/want to do something, I cannot.'
longExplanationEn: This pattern emphasizes that a person wants or attempts to do something (often in volitional form), but is incapable of doing so. It is often used with potential verbs in negative form: 'Even if I try to ~, I can't ~.'

examples.ja: 彼を理解しようにも理解できない。
examples.romaji: Kare o rikai shiyou ni mo rikai dekinai.
examples.meaningEn: Even if I try to understand him, I can't.

examples.ja: 走ろうにも走れない。
examples.romaji: Hashirou ni mo hashirenai.
examples.meaningEn: Even if I want to run, I can't.

examples.ja: 泣こうにも泣けない。
examples.romaji: Nakou ni mo nakenai.
examples.meaningEn: Even if I want to cry, I can't.

examples.ja: 忘れようにも忘れられない。
examples.romaji: Wasureyou ni mo wasurerarenai.
examples.meaningEn: Even if I try to forget, I just can’t.

title: Verbる べからざる Noun (Verb-ru bekara zaru Noun)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + べからざる + Noun
shortExplanationEn: Used to express what should not be done; 'must not', 'should not'.
longExplanationEn: The Japanese grammar point Verbる べからざる Noun is used to indicate something that should not be done, or is not allowed to be done. It translates to 'must not' or 'should not' in English. It is often used in formal written Japanese or in formal speeches. It is constructed by using the verb in its dictionary form followed by 'べからざる' and a noun, usually related to behavior or events.

examples.ja: 人を裏切るべからざる行為だ。
examples.romaji: Hito o uragiru bekara zaru koui da.
examples.meaningEn: Betraying others is an act that must not be done.

examples.ja: 子供を傷つけるべからざる行動だ。
examples.romaji: Kodomo o kizutsukeru bekara zaru koudou da.
examples.meaningEn: Hurting a child is a behavior that must not happen.

examples.ja: 公に嘘をつくべからざる行動だ。
examples.romaji: Ooyake ni uso o tsuku bekara zaru koudou da.
examples.meaningEn: Lying publicly is an act that should not be done.

examples.ja: 他人の財産を侵すべからざる行為だ。
examples.romaji: Tanin no zaisan o okasu bekara zaru koui da.
examples.meaningEn: Infringing on someone else’s property is an act that must not be performed.

title: Verbる べからず (〜ru bekara zu)
formation: Verb-ru + べからず
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea of 'must not do' or 'should not do'.
longExplanationEn: The ～るべからず grammar point is used to express a strong prohibition or firmly established rule. It can be translated as 'must not do' or 'should not do'. It is common in formal or instructive settings (e.g., signs, notices). The formation is straightforward with verbs in dictionary form followed by べからず.

examples.ja: 嘘をつくべからず。
examples.romaji: Uso o tsuku bekara zu.
examples.meaningEn: One must not tell lies.

examples.ja: 彼らの秘密を漏らすべからず。
examples.romaji: Karera no himitsu o morasu bekara zu.
examples.meaningEn: You must not leak their secrets.

examples.ja: ルールを破るべからず。
examples.romaji: Ruuru o yaburu bekara zu.
examples.meaningEn: You must not break the rules.

examples.ja: 勉強を怠るべからず。
examples.romaji: Benkyou o okotaru bekara zu.
examples.meaningEn: One must not neglect their studies.

title: Verbる べく (Verb-ru beku)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + べく
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the meaning of 'in order to', 'for the purpose of'.
longExplanationEn: The 'Verbる べく' grammar point is used to indicate a strong intention or purpose of the speaker. It can be translated as 'in order to' or 'for the purpose of' in English. It is more common in written language or formal contexts.

examples.ja: 成功するべく、日々努力しています。
examples.romaji: Seikou suru beku, hibi doryoku shite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: In order to succeed, I am working hard every day.

examples.ja: 健康を維持するべく、毎日運動をしています。
examples.romaji: Kenkou o iji suru beku, mainichi undou o shite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: In order to maintain my health, I exercise every day.

examples.ja: 彼は学位を得るべく、毎日勉強しています。
examples.romaji: Kare wa gakui o eru beku, mainichi benkyou shite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: He studies every day in order to get a degree.

examples.ja: 早く目的地に到着するべく、タクシーを呼びました。
examples.romaji: Hayaku mokutekichi ni touchaku suru beku, takushii o yobimashita.
examples.meaningEn: I called a taxi in order to arrive at the destination quickly.

title: Verbる べくもない (Verb-ru beku mo nai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + べくもない
shortExplanationEn: Means 'it's impossible to...' or 'there's no way to...'.
longExplanationEn: This grammar point is used when you want to express that something is impossible or there is no way to do something. The verb before 'べくもない' should be in its dictionary form.

examples.ja: その問題はとても難しく、解くべくもない。
examples.romaji: Sono mondai wa totemo muzukashiku, toku beku mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: That problem is so difficult that it's impossible to solve.

examples.ja: 彼女はあまりに速く走って、追いつくべくもない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa amari ni hayaku hashitte, oitsuku beku mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: She runs so fast that there's no way to catch up.

examples.ja: 試験の筆記用具を忘れた。書くべくもない。
examples.romaji: Shiken no hikki yougu o wasureta. Kaku beku mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: I forgot my writing utensils for the test. It's impossible to write.

examples.ja: 彼はとても高く跳ぶ。追い越すべくもない。
examples.romaji: Kare wa totemo takaku tobu. Oikosu beku mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: He jumps so high that there's no way to surpass him.

title: Verbる までもない (〜ru made mo nai)
formation: Verb-る + までもない
shortExplanationEn: It's not necessary to the extent of; no need to.
longExplanationEn: The 〜るまでもない grammar point is used to express that something is not necessary to the extent of doing that action. It can be translated as 'no need to' in English.

examples.ja: そんなに心配するまでもない。
examples.romaji: Sonna ni shinpai suru made mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: There's no need to worry that much.

examples.ja: 電話するまでもないことだ。
examples.romaji: Denwa suru made mo nai koto da.
examples.meaningEn: It's not something you need to call about.

examples.ja: 小さな失敗であきらめるまでもない。
examples.romaji: Chiisana shippai de akirameru made mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: You don't need to give up over a small mistake.

examples.ja: この問題は、会議で話し合うまでもない。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wa, kaigi de hanashiau made mo nai.
examples.meaningEn: This problem doesn't need to be discussed at a meeting.

title: Verbる ものとする (〜ru mono to suru)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + ものとする
shortExplanationEn: Assuming or deciding something as...
longExplanationEn: The ～るものとする grammar point is often used in formal or legal contexts to express a decision or assumption. It can be translated as 'it is decided/assumed that...' or 'shall be deemed that...'.

examples.ja: ここでの話し合いは秘密とするものとします。
examples.romaji: Koko de no hanashiai wa himitsu to suru mono to shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: The discussions here are to be kept confidential.

examples.ja: この契約は明日から有効とするものとする。
examples.romaji: Kono keiyaku wa ashita kara yuukou to suru mono to suru.
examples.meaningEn: This contract shall become effective from tomorrow.

examples.ja: 彼をチームのリーダーとするものとします。
examples.romaji: Kare o chiimu no riidaa to suru mono to shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: We will designate him as the team leader.

examples.ja: この件については終了とするものとする。
examples.romaji: Kono ken ni tsuite wa shuuryou to suru mono to suru.
examples.meaningEn: We will consider this matter closed.

title: Verbる や否や (Verb-ru ya ina ya)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + や否や
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea of 'as soon as', 'the moment...'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point Verbる や否や is used to express something happening immediately after another action or event. It can be translated as 'as soon as...' or 'the moment...'. The verb must be in dictionary form.

examples.ja: 彼女が家に帰るや否や、すぐにテレビをつけました。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga ie ni kaeru ya ina ya, sugu ni terebi o tsukemashita.
examples.meaningEn: As soon as she returned home, she turned on the TV.

examples.ja: 私が郵便箱を開けるや否や、手紙が落ちました。
examples.romaji: Watashi ga yuubinbako o akeru ya ina ya, tegami ga ochimashita.
examples.meaningEn: The moment I opened the mailbox, the letters fell out.

examples.ja: 子供たちは学校が終わるや否や、公園に走りました。
examples.romaji: Kodomo-tachi wa gakkou ga owaru ya ina ya, kouen ni hashirimashita.
examples.meaningEn: As soon as school was over, the children ran to the park.

examples.ja: 父が新聞を読むや否や、何かに震えていました。
examples.romaji: Chichi ga shinbun o yomu ya ina ya, nanika ni furuete imashita.
examples.meaningEn: The moment my father read the newspaper, he started trembling about something.

title: Verbる 始末だ (〜ru shimatsu da)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + 始末だ
shortExplanationEn: Indicates a (often unfortunate) result or conclusion of an action.
longExplanationEn: The ～る始末だ grammar point expresses the outcome or conclusion of an action, typically a negative or unfortunate result. It can be translated as 'end up...', 'the result is...', or 'the conclusion is...'.

examples.ja: 忘れてしまって、パスポートを家に置いてくる始末だった。
examples.romaji: Wasurete shimatte, pasupooto o ie ni oite-kuru shimatsu datta.
examples.meaningEn: I ended up leaving my passport at home because I forgot.

examples.ja: 毎晩遅くまで働いて、倒れる始末だ。
examples.romaji: Maiban osoku made hataraite, taoreru shimatsu da.
examples.meaningEn: I work until late every night and end up collapsing.

examples.ja: 勉強しなかったので、試験に落ちる始末だ。
examples.romaji: Benkyou shinakatta node, shiken ni ochiru shimatsu da.
examples.meaningEn: I didn't study, so I ended up failing the exam.

examples.ja: 電車が遅れたため、会議に遅れる始末だ。
examples.romaji: Densha ga okureta tame, kaigi ni okureru shimatsu da.
examples.meaningEn: Because the train was delayed, I ended up being late for the meeting.

title: Verbる 嫌いがある (～ru kirai ga aru)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + 嫌いがある
shortExplanationEn: Tendency to do a negative action; have a bad habit of ~.
longExplanationEn: The ～る嫌いがある grammar point is used to express a negative tendency or habit. It often carries a critical or disapproving tone and points out someone's habitual action.

examples.ja: 彼女は遅刻する嫌いがある。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa chikoku suru kirai ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: She has a tendency to be late.

examples.ja: 私の父はお酒を飲み過ぎる嫌いがある。
examples.romaji: Watashi no chichi wa osake o nomisugiru kirai ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: My father has a habit of drinking too much alcohol.

examples.ja: 彼は物をすぐ忘れる嫌いがある。
examples.romaji: Kare wa mono o sugu wasureru kirai ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: He tends to forget things quickly.

examples.ja: 彼女は人の話を途中で切る嫌いがある。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa hito no hanashi o tochuu de kiru kirai ga aru.
examples.meaningEn: She has a habit of interrupting people while they are speaking.

title: いつまで～のやら (itsumade ~ no yara)
formation: いつまで + Verb (dictionary form) + のやら
shortExplanationEn: Expresses uncertainty regarding duration; 'don't know when... will end'.
longExplanationEn: The いつまで～のやら grammar point is used to express uncertainty about when something will end or finish. It is often used in situations where the speaker is unsure about when a particular state of affairs or event will conclude.

examples.ja: この試験勉強はいつまで続くのやら。
examples.romaji: Kono shiken benkyou wa itsumade tsuzuku no yara.
examples.meaningEn: I don't know when this exam study will end.

examples.ja: 彼の話がいつ終わるのやら、わからない。
examples.romaji: Kare no hanashi ga itsu owaru no yara, wakaranai.
examples.meaningEn: I don't know when his talk will finish.

examples.ja: この雨がいつまで降り続くのやら。
examples.romaji: Kono ame ga itsumade furitsuzuku no yara.
examples.meaningEn: I don't know when this rain will stop.

examples.ja: コロナのパンデミックがいつまで続くのやら。
examples.romaji: Korona no pandemikku ga itsumade tsuzuku no yara.
examples.meaningEn: I don't know when this coronavirus pandemic will end.

title: ～が Verb られる (〜ga Verb rareru)
formation:
❶ (Group 1) Verb:  書く → 書ける / 飲む → 飲める, etc.
❷ (Group 2) Verb-ます stem + られる (食べる→食べられる、見る→見られる)
shortExplanationEn: Expresses potential or ability to do something; 'can', 'able to'.
longExplanationEn: The ～が Verb られる grammar pattern generally refers to the potential form of Japanese verbs (i.e., 'can do something'). However, the exact formation depends on the verb group. For most Group 1 (五段) verbs, potential form changes the final -u to -eru (e.g., 書く→書ける). For Group 2 (一段) verbs, you often see ～られる (e.g., 食べる→食べられる). In English, it translates as 'can' or 'able to'.

examples.ja: 彼がこの問題を解けると思いますか？
examples.romaji: Kare ga kono mondai o tokeru to omoimasu ka?
examples.meaningEn: Do you think he can solve this problem?

examples.ja: 私がその大きな箱を運べるか？
examples.romaji: Watashi ga sono ookina hako o hakoberu ka?
examples.meaningEn: Can I carry that big box?

examples.ja: 彼が早く走れると思います。
examples.romaji: Kare ga hayaku hashireru to omoimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I think he can run fast.

examples.ja: 彼女は日本語が話せる。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa nihongo ga hanaseru.
examples.meaningEn: She can speak Japanese.

title: ～かと思いきや (〜ka to omoikiya)
formation: Sentence + かと思いきや (Verb plain form, i-/na-Adjective, or Noun + だ + かと思いきや)
shortExplanationEn: Expressing unexpected outcomes; 'just when I thought...', 'but...'.
longExplanationEn: The ～かと思いきや grammar point is used when the outcome turns out different from what was initially assumed or expected. It can be translated as 'just when I thought...' or simply 'but (actually)...'. It often highlights that the speaker’s assumption was proven wrong by subsequent events.

examples.ja: 明日は晴れるかと思いきや、まさかの雨が降った。
examples.romaji: Ashita wa hareru ka to omoikiya, masaka no ame ga futta.
examples.meaningEn: I thought it would be sunny tomorrow, but unexpectedly it rained.

examples.ja: 彼が試験に落ちたかと思いきや、合格していた。
examples.romaji: Kare ga shiken ni ochita ka to omoikiya, goukaku shite ita.
examples.meaningEn: I thought he had failed the test, but he actually passed.

examples.ja: このケーキは美味しそうだったかと思いきや、全然甘くなかった。
examples.romaji: Kono keeki wa oishisou datta ka to omoikiya, zenzen amakunakatta.
examples.meaningEn: I thought this cake would be delicious, but it wasn’t sweet at all.

examples.ja: 列車が遅れるかと思いきや、時間通りに到着した。
examples.romaji: Ressha ga okureru ka to omoikiya, jikan doori ni touchaku shita.
examples.meaningEn: I thought the train would be late, but it arrived on time.

title: ～がゆえに (～ga yue ni)
formation: Noun / Verb / Adjective + がゆえに
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the reason or cause; 'because', 'due to', 'as a result of'.
longExplanationEn: The ～がゆえに grammar point is used to express a reason or cause, often in more formal writing or speech. It roughly corresponds to 'because (of)', 'due to', or 'as a result of' in English.

examples.ja: 愛情がゆえに、彼は彼女を許した。
examples.romaji: Aijou ga yue ni, kare wa kanojo o yurushita.
examples.meaningEn: Because of love, he forgave her.

examples.ja: 突然の雨がゆえに、試合は延期された。
examples.romaji: Totsuzen no ame ga yue ni, shiai wa enki sareta.
examples.meaningEn: Due to the sudden rain, the match was postponed.

examples.ja: 彼の努力がゆえに、彼は成功を収めた。
examples.romaji: Kare no doryoku ga yue ni, kare wa seikou o osameta.
examples.meaningEn: Because of his efforts, he achieved success.

examples.ja: 彼の死がゆえに、彼の家族は深い悲しみに包まれた。
examples.romaji: Kare no shi ga yue ni, kare no kazoku wa fukai kanashimi ni tsutsumareta.
examples.meaningEn: Due to his death, his family was enveloped in deep sorrow.

title: ～がゆえの Noun (〜ga yue no Noun)
formation: Noun / Verb-dictionary form + がゆえの + Noun
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the reason or cause for something; 'because of', 'due to'.
longExplanationEn: The ～がゆえの Noun pattern is used to describe a noun that exists or occurs due to some cause. It is often used in more literary or formal contexts.

examples.ja: 愛がゆえの悲しみ。
examples.romaji: Ai ga yue no kanashimi.
examples.meaningEn: Sadness due to love.

examples.ja: 健康がゆえの幸せ。
examples.romaji: Kenkou ga yue no shiawase.
examples.meaningEn: Happiness due to good health.

examples.ja: 悪行がゆえの罰。
examples.romaji: Akugyou ga yue no batsu.
examples.meaningEn: Punishment due to wrongdoing.

examples.ja: 努力がゆえの成功。
examples.romaji: Doryoku ga yue no seikou.
examples.meaningEn: Success due to effort.

title: ～から Noun に 至る まで (〜kara 〜ni itaru made)
formation: Noun (start) + から + Noun (end) + に至るまで
shortExplanationEn: Expressing range from one thing to another; 'from...to', 'ranging from...to'.
longExplanationEn: The ～から〜に至るまで grammar point emphasizes a range or scope extending from one extreme to another. It can be translated as 'from...to...' or 'ranging from...to...' in English. It can apply to people, places, times, objects, and so on.

examples.ja: このバンドの曲は、青年から老人に至るまで、人々に愛されています。
examples.romaji: Kono bando no kyoku wa, seinen kara roujin ni itaru made, hitobito ni aisarete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: The songs of this band are loved by people ranging from the young to the elderly.

examples.ja: 小さい物件から大きな建物に至るまで、全てのプロパティの管理を我々が行います。
examples.romaji: Chiisai bukken kara ooki na tatemono ni itaru made, subete no property no kanri o wareware ga okonaimasu.
examples.meaningEn: We handle all property management, ranging from small units to large buildings.

examples.ja: 朝のニュース放送から夜のドラマに至るまで、テレビは毎日見ています。
examples.romaji: Asa no nyuusu housou kara yoru no dorama ni itaru made, terebi wa mainichi mite imasu.
examples.meaningEn: I watch TV every day, from the morning news broadcasts to the night dramas.

examples.ja: この地域は、春から冬に至るまで旅行者で賑わいます。
examples.romaji: Kono chiiki wa, haru kara fuyu ni itaru made ryokousha de nigiwaimasu.
examples.meaningEn: This area is bustling with tourists from spring to winter.

title: ～ごとく (〜gotoku)
formation: Verb-て + ごとく / Noun + の + ごとく
shortExplanationEn: To seem like; as if; as though.
longExplanationEn: The ～ごとく grammar point is used to express similarity or resemblance. It is quite formal/literary, meaning 'as if,' 'as though,' or 'like.' It can be used with verbs and nouns.

examples.ja: 彼は鳥のごとく飛んでいるように見えた。
examples.romaji: Kare wa tori no gotoku tonde iru you ni mieta.
examples.meaningEn: He looked as though he was flying like a bird.

examples.ja: 彼は死んだごとく眠っていた。
examples.romaji: Kare wa shinda gotoku nemutte ita.
examples.meaningEn: He was sleeping as though he were dead.

examples.ja: 彼女はアイドルのごとく崇められている。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa aidoru no gotoku agamerarete iru.
examples.meaningEn: She is revered as if she were an idol.

examples.ja: 彼は風のごとく走った。
examples.romaji: Kare wa kaze no gotoku hashitta.
examples.meaningEn: He ran like the wind.

title: ～こととて (〜koto tote)
formation: Verb (plain form) + こととて / Noun + の + こととて / (な-Adjective + な) + こととて
shortExplanationEn: Used to express the reason or premise for a decision or judgment; 'since', 'because'.
longExplanationEn: The ～こととて grammar point expresses a reason or premise in a slightly formal or literary way. It can often be interpreted as 'since' or 'because'.

examples.ja: 彼は裁判に勝つために全資産を使うこととて、弁護士に頼んだ。
examples.romaji: Kare wa saiban ni katsu tame ni zenshisan o tsukau koto tote, bengoshi ni tanonda.
examples.meaningEn: Since he's using all his assets to win the trial, he hired a lawyer.

examples.ja: 明日は大切な試験があることとて、今夜は早く寝ます。
examples.romaji: Ashita wa taisetsu na shiken ga aru koto tote, konya wa hayaku nemasu.
examples.meaningEn: Since I have an important exam tomorrow, I’m going to bed early tonight.

examples.ja: このプロジェクトの成功は会社の未来に影響することとて、全力を尽くします。
examples.romaji: Kono purojekuto no seikou wa kaisha no mirai ni eikyou suru koto tote, zenryoku o tsukushimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Because the success of this project will affect the company's future, I will do my best.

examples.ja: 彼が有名人であることとて、プライバシーが保護されるべきだ。
examples.romaji: Kare ga yuumeijin de aru koto tote, puraibashii ga hogo sareru beki da.
examples.meaningEn: Since he is a celebrity, his privacy should be protected.

title: ～ずにすんだ (〜zuni sunda)
formation: Verb-ず form + に + すんだ
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the idea of 'was able to do without'; 'managed to avoid'.
longExplanationEn: The ～ずにすんだ grammar point indicates that one was able to avoid doing something or managed to get by without doing it. It translates as 'managed to avoid ~' or 'got by without ~.'

examples.ja: 彼は事故を起こさずにすんだ。
examples.romaji: Kare wa jiko o okosazu ni sunda.
examples.meaningEn: He managed to avoid having an accident.

examples.ja: 私は手術せずにすんだ。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa shujutsu sezu ni sunda.
examples.meaningEn: I was able to do without surgery.

examples.ja: 雨が降らずにすんだ。
examples.romaji: Ame ga furazu ni sunda.
examples.meaningEn: We managed to avoid the rain.

examples.ja: 罰金を払わずにすんだ。
examples.romaji: Bakkin o harawazu ni sunda.
examples.meaningEn: I managed to avoid paying a fine.

title: ～だろうとなかろうと (〜darou to nakarou to)
formation: Noun / i-Adjective / na-Adjective / Verb + だろうとなかろうと
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'regardless of whether it is or isn't'.
longExplanationEn: The ～だろうとなかろうと grammar point is used to express the idea of 'regardless of whether it is or isn't.' It translates roughly as 'whether it is ~ or not' in English. This construction emphasizes that the speaker’s stance or action does not change, regardless of the two possible conditions.

examples.ja: 試験が難しいだろうとなかろうと、全力で頑張ります。
examples.romaji: Shiken ga muzukashii darou to nakarou to, zenryoku de ganbarimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Regardless of whether the exam is difficult or not, I will do my best.

examples.ja: 彼が来るだろうとなかろうと、私たちは始めます。
examples.romaji: Kare ga kuru darou to nakarou to, watashitachi wa hajimemasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether he comes or not, we will start.

examples.ja: この仕事が面白いだろうとなかろうと、私は続けます。
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto ga omoshiroi darou to nakarou to, watashi wa tsuzukemasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether this job is interesting or not, I will continue.

examples.ja: 彼が賛成するだろうとなかろうと、私たちは進行します。
examples.romaji: Kare ga sansei suru darou to nakarou to, watashitachi wa shinkou shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Whether he agrees or not, we will proceed.

title: ～つもりだ (〜tsumori da)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + つもりだ
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the speaker's intention or plan.
longExplanationEn: The ～つもりだ grammar point is used to express the speaker's intention or plan to do something. It translates as 'intend to,' 'plan to,' or 'think that (I will).' It's often used with the verb in its dictionary form.

examples.ja: 明日は早く起きるつもりです。
examples.romaji: Ashita wa hayaku okiru tsumori desu.
examples.meaningEn: I plan to wake up early tomorrow.

examples.ja: 私は日本語を勉強するつもりです。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa nihongo o benkyou suru tsumori desu.
examples.meaningEn: I intend to study Japanese.

examples.ja: 彼は留学するつもりだと言った。
examples.romaji: Kare wa ryuugaku suru tsumori da to itta.
examples.meaningEn: He said he plans to study abroad.

examples.ja: 私は来週末に家族と旅行するつもりだ。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa raishuumatsu ni kazoku to ryokou suru tsumori da.
examples.meaningEn: I plan to travel with my family next weekend.

title: ～つもりで (〜tsumori de)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + つもりで
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the speaker's intention or plan; 'with the intention of', 'planning to'.
longExplanationEn: The ～つもりで grammar point is used to express the speaker's intention or mindset when doing something. It can often be translated as 'with the intention of' or 'with the mindset that.'

examples.ja: 私は日本に住むつもりで日本語を勉強しています。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa Nihon ni sumu tsumori de Nihongo o benkyou shiteimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I am studying Japanese with the intention of living in Japan.

examples.ja: 彼は全てを失うつもりでギャンブルをしました。
examples.romaji: Kare wa subete o ushinau tsumori de gyanburu o shimashita.
examples.meaningEn: He gambled with the intention of risking everything.

examples.ja: 明日のテストに合格するつもりで一生懸命勉強します。
examples.romaji: Ashita no tesuto ni goukaku suru tsumori de isshoukenmei benkyou shimasu.
examples.meaningEn: I will study hard with the intention of passing tomorrow’s test.

examples.ja: 彼は早く起きるつもりで早く寝ました。
examples.romaji: Kare wa hayaku okiru tsumori de hayaku nemashita.
examples.meaningEn: He went to bed early with the intention of waking up early.

title: ～ではすまない (〜dewa sumanai)
formation: Verb-dictionary form + ではすまない / い-Adjective + ではすまない / な-Adjective + ではすまない / Noun + ではすまない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses that just doing something is not enough or will not suffice.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point ～ではすまない is used when the speaker strongly feels that an action or situation alone is not sufficient or acceptable, implying that more serious measures are needed or that the consequences are more severe.

examples.ja: ただ謝るではすまない。何か行動を取らなければならない。
examples.romaji: Tada ayamaru dewa sumanai. Nanika koudou o toranakereba naranai.
examples.meaningEn: Just apologizing will not suffice. Some action needs to be taken.

examples.ja: この問題は議論だけではすまない。具体的な計画が必要だ。
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wa giron dake dewa sumanai. Gutaiteki na keikaku ga hitsuyou da.
examples.meaningEn: This problem won't be solved with just discussions. A concrete plan is needed.

examples.ja: 彼の失敗は注意だけではすまない。彼はその責任を取るべきだ。
examples.romaji: Kare no shippai wa chuui dake dewa sumanai. Kare wa sono sekinin o toru beki da.
examples.meaningEn: His failure won't be resolved with just a warning. He should take responsibility.

examples.ja: この失敗は金だけではすまない。信頼を取り戻すため、真心で謝罪するべきだ。
examples.romaji: Kono shippai wa kane dake dewa sumanai. Shinrai o torimodosu tame, magokoro de shazai suru beki da.
examples.meaningEn: This mistake cannot be settled with money alone. To regain trust, an earnest apology is needed.

title: ～とあって (〜to atte)
formation: Noun + とあって / (Sometimes Verb + とあって, depending on context)
shortExplanationEn: Expresses a special situation or event due to certain circumstances.
longExplanationEn: The ～とあって grammar point is used to indicate that, due to a special or noteworthy circumstance, a particular situation has occurred. It often translates as 'because (it is)...' or 'given that...' in English, emphasizing the uniqueness of the cause.

examples.ja: 春休みとあって、公園は子供たちでいっぱいだ。
examples.romaji: Haruyasumi to atte, kouen wa kodomo-tachi de ippai da.
examples.meaningEn: Because it's spring break, the park is full of children.

examples.ja: 試験前とあって、図書館は学生で混んでいる。
examples.romaji: Shiken mae to atte, toshokan wa gakusei de konde iru.
examples.meaningEn: With exams coming up, the library is crowded with students.

examples.ja: 開店日とあって、その店にはたくさんの人が並んでいた。
examples.romaji: Kaitenbi to atte, sono mise ni wa takusan no hito ga narande ita.
examples.meaningEn: Because it was the store's opening day, a lot of people lined up.

examples.ja: 彼の誕生日とあって、友人たちはサプライズパーティーを計画した。
examples.romaji: Kare no tanjoubi to atte, yuujin-tachi wa sapuraizu paatii o keikaku shita.
examples.meaningEn: Because it was his birthday, his friends planned a surprise party.

title: ～とあれば (〜to areba)
formation: Verb-casual + とあれば / い-Adjective + とあれば / な-Adjective + だとあれば / Noun + だとあれば
shortExplanationEn: Expresses a condition; 'if', 'when', 'since'.
longExplanationEn: The ～とあれば grammar point is used to express a condition or premise in which a certain outcome or action naturally follows. It can be translated as 'if,' 'when,' or 'since,' depending on context. It emphasizes that under this specific condition, a certain response is expected.

examples.ja: 無料とあれば、誰でも利用したいと思います。
examples.romaji: Muryou to areba, dare demo riyou shitai to omoimasu.
examples.meaningEn: If it's free, anyone would want to use it.

examples.ja: この曲が流れるとあれば、彼は必ず踊り始めます。
examples.romaji: Kono kyoku ga nagareru to areba, kare wa kanarazu odorihajimemasu.
examples.meaningEn: If this song starts playing, he will definitely begin to dance.

examples.ja: 明日が休日だとあれば、今夜は遅くまで起きていられます。
examples.romaji: Ashita ga kyuujitsu da to areba, konya wa osoku made okite iraremasu.
examples.meaningEn: If tomorrow is a holiday, I can stay up late tonight.

examples.ja: 彼が参加するとあれば、このイベントは成功でしょう。
examples.romaji: Kare ga sanka suru to areba, kono ibento wa seikou deshou.
examples.meaningEn: If he participates, this event will surely be successful.

title: ～といえども (〜to iedomo)
formation: Noun + といえども / Verb-plain form + といえども / Adjective + といえども
shortExplanationEn: Even if it is said that; Even though; Although.
longExplanationEn: The ～といえども grammar point is used to show a contrast or contradiction. It is similar to 'even though' or 'although' in English. It can be used with nouns, verbs, adjectives, etc., to emphasize that the reality is different from the assumption or label.

examples.ja: 彼が天才といえども、ミスをすることはあります。
examples.romaji: Kare ga tensai to iedomo, misu o suru koto wa arimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Even though he’s a genius, he still makes mistakes.

examples.ja: このパソコンが新しいといえども、問題があります。
examples.romaji: Kono pasokon ga atarashii to iedomo, mondai ga arimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Even though this computer is new, it has problems.

examples.ja: 春といえども、まだ寒い日があります。
examples.romaji: Haru to iedomo, mada samui hi ga arimasu.
examples.meaningEn: Even though it's spring, there are still cold days.

examples.ja: 彼女が一番好きな人といえども、嫌なことは嫌です。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga ichiban suki na hito to iedomo, iya na koto wa iya desu.
examples.meaningEn: Even though she’s my favorite person, unpleasant things are still unpleasant.

title: ～といったらありはしない (〜to ittara ari wa shinai)
formation: Noun + といったらありはしない / な-Adjective + といったらありはしない
shortExplanationEn: Express extreme exclamation; 'extremely', 'unbelievable', 'unsurpassed'.
longExplanationEn: The ～といったらありはしない grammar point is used to express an extreme degree of something, often surprise or admiration. It can be loosely translated as 'unbelievably...' or 'incredibly...'.

examples.ja: 彼の英語の上手さといったらありはしない。
examples.romaji: Kare no eigo no jouzusa to ittara ari wa shinai.
examples.meaningEn: His English is unbelievably good.

examples.ja: このスイーツの美味しさといったらありはしない。
examples.romaji: Kono suiitsu no oishisa to ittara ari wa shinai.
examples.meaningEn: These sweets are unbelievably delicious.

examples.ja: その子の頭の良さといったらありはしない。
examples.romaji: Sono ko no atama no yosa to ittara ari wa shinai.
examples.meaningEn: That kid's intelligence is unsurpassed.

examples.ja: 彼女の美しさといったらありはしない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no utsukushisa to ittara ari wa shinai.
examples.meaningEn: Her beauty is beyond belief.

title: ～といったらありゃしない (〜to ittara arya shinai)
formation: Verb-casual + といったらありゃしない / い-Adjective + といったらありゃしない / な-Adjective + だ + といったらありゃしない / Noun + だ + といったらありゃしない
shortExplanationEn: Emphasizes that something is to the extreme; 'you have no idea how...', 'it's incredibly...'.
longExplanationEn: The ～といったらありゃしない grammar point is an idiomatic expression used to emphasize that something is extremely or incredibly a certain way—often beyond words. It can be loosely rendered as 'you wouldn't believe how...' or 'it's indescribably...'.

examples.ja: その店のラーメンは美味しいといったらありゃしない。
examples.romaji: Sono mise no ramen wa oishii to ittara arya shinai.
examples.meaningEn: You have no idea how delicious the ramen at that shop is.

examples.ja: 彼女の料理の腕前は上手だといったらありゃしない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no ryouri no udemae wa jouzu da to ittara arya shinai.
examples.meaningEn: You have no idea how skilled she is at cooking.

examples.ja: この冬の寒さは厳しいといったらありゃしない。
examples.romaji: Kono fuyu no samusa wa kibishii to ittara arya shinai.
examples.meaningEn: You have no idea how harsh this winter's cold is.

examples.ja: その小説は面白いといったらありゃしない。
examples.romaji: Sono shousetsu wa omoshiroi to ittara arya shinai.
examples.meaningEn: You have no idea how interesting that novel is.

title: ～といったらない (〜to ittara nai)
formation: い-Adjective + といったらない / な-Adjective + だといったらない / Noun + だといったらない
shortExplanationEn: Expresses the extreme of something; 'nothing can be more ... than this'.
longExplanationEn: The ～といったらない grammar point is used to emphasize the high degree or intensity of an action or state. It can be translated as “nothing can be more ... than this” or “it’s extremely...”. It conveys that something is so intense or extreme that it cannot be adequately described.

examples.ja: 今日は寒いといったらない。
examples.romaji: Kyou wa samui to ittara nai.
examples.meaningEn: It’s extremely cold today.

examples.ja: 彼女の料理の美味しさといったらない。
examples.romaji: Kanojo no ryouri no oishisa to ittara nai.
examples.meaningEn: Nothing can be more delicious than her cooking.

examples.ja: この子の頭の良さだといったらない。
examples.romaji: Kono ko no atama no yosa da to ittara nai.
examples.meaningEn: This child is unbelievably smart.

examples.ja: 彼の怠けっぷりだといったらない。
examples.romaji: Kare no namakeppuri da to ittara nai.
examples.meaningEn: Nobody could be lazier than him.

title: ～ときている (〜to kite iru)
formation: Verb-casual / い-Adjective / な-Adjective（＋だ） / Noun（＋だ） + ときている
shortExplanationEn: Highlights a situation or condition that is serious/extreme, often with a negative nuance.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point ～ときている is used to emphasize that some circumstance is particularly notable—often negatively. It implies, “Given that things are so ~, it’s no wonder that...” or “Because the situation is (too) ~, such-and-such follows.” It is relatively informal and more common in spoken language.

examples.ja: 仕事が忙しいときているので、なかなか休みが取れません。
examples.romaji: Shigoto ga isogashii to kite iru no de, nakanaka yasumi ga toremasen.
examples.meaningEn: Because work is so busy, I just can’t take any time off.

examples.ja: 経済が悪いときているだけに、会社が大量解雇を発表した。
examples.romaji: Keizai ga warui to kite iru dake ni, kaisha ga tairyou kaiko o happyou shita.
examples.meaningEn: Since the economy is in such bad shape, the company announced mass layoffs.

examples.ja: 旅行先で荷物を盗まれたときているから、旅行を楽しむどころではない。
examples.romaji: Ryokousaki de nimotsu o nusumareta to kite iru kara, ryokou o tanoshimu dokoro de wa nai.
examples.meaningEn: Given that my luggage was stolen while traveling, there’s no way I can enjoy the trip.

examples.ja: 試験が近いときているうえに、課題も山ほどあって大変だ。
examples.romaji: Shiken ga chikai to kite iru ue ni, kadai mo yamahodo atte taihen da.
examples.meaningEn: Not only are exams coming up, but I also have a mountain of assignments, so it’s really tough.

title: ～ところを (〜tokoro wo)
formation: Verb-ている + ところを / Noun + の + ところを / い-Adjective + ところを / な-Adjective + な + ところを
shortExplanationEn: Indicates a contrasting or inconvenient timing; 'just when...', 'even though...'.
longExplanationEn: The grammar point ～ところを is often used when something happens (or one acts) at a certain moment/condition that makes the timing inconvenient or contrary to what is expected. It can imply “in spite of the fact that...” or “just when...” depending on context.

examples.ja: ちょうど映画を見ているところを、停電で真っ暗になった。
examples.romaji: Choudo eiga o mite iru tokoro o, teiden de makkura ni natta.
examples.meaningEn: Just when I was in the middle of watching a movie, the power went out and everything went dark.

examples.ja: 美味しく食べているところを、彼が全部横取りしてしまった。
examples.romaji: Oishiku tabete iru tokoro o, kare ga zenbu yokodori shite shimatta.
examples.meaningEn: Right when I was enjoying my meal, he took it all away.

examples.ja: 飛行機が離陸しようとしているところを、急にエンジンが止まった。
examples.romaji: Hikouki ga ririku shiyou to shite iru tokoro o, kyuu ni enjin ga tomatta.
examples.meaningEn: Just as the plane was about to take off, the engine suddenly stopped.

examples.ja: 彼女が怒っているところを、私は冗談を言ってしまった。
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga okotte iru tokoro o, watashi wa joudan o itte shimatta.
examples.meaningEn: Even though she was angry, I went ahead and made a joke.

title: ～とされる (〜to sareru)
formation: Verb-casual + とされる / い-Adjective + とされる / な-Adjective + だとされる / Noun + だとされる
shortExplanationEn: Indicates a general belief or widely accepted view; 'is considered', 'is seen as', 'is said to be'.
longExplanationEn: The ～とされる grammar point expresses what is generally believed, accepted, or reputed. It can be translated as 'is considered to be,' 'is seen as,' or 'is said to be.' It’s often used in formal or written contexts. The structure differs slightly depending on whether it’s used with a noun, adjective, or verb.

examples.ja: サッカーは世界で最も人気のあるスポーツとされています。
examples.romaji: Sakkaa wa sekai de mottomo ninki no aru supootsu to sarete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: Soccer is considered the most popular sport in the world.

examples.ja: 彼は最も有能な科学者とされる。
examples.romaji: Kare wa mottomo yuunou na kagakusha to sareru.
examples.meaningEn: He is considered the most talented scientist.

examples.ja: この建物は地元のランドマークだとされています。
examples.romaji: Kono tatemono wa jimoto no randomaaku da to sarete imasu.
examples.meaningEn: This building is said to be a local landmark.

examples.ja: そのお寺は古代の美術品とされている。
examples.romaji: Sono otera wa kodai no bijutsuhin to sarete iru.
examples.meaningEn: That temple is considered an ancient work of art.

title: ～としたところで (〜to shita tokoro de)
formation: Verb-casual + としたところで / い-Adjective + としたところで / な-Adjective + だとしたところで / Noun + だとしたところで
shortExplanationEn: Means 'even if' or 'even assuming'; emphasizes that the outcome won’t change.
longExplanationEn: The ～としたところで grammar point means 'even if' or 'even assuming that ~.' It highlights that even if the stated condition were met, the result would not significantly change. Often used to convey a sense of futility or skepticism in hypothetical statements.

examples.ja: 彼が来るとしたところで、何も変わらないだろう。
examples.romaji: Kare ga kuru to shita tokoro de, nani mo kawaranai darou.
examples.meaningEn: Even if he comes, nothing will probably change.

examples.ja: 美味しいお菓子を作るとしたところで、それが売れる保証はない。
examples.romaji: Oishii okashi o tsukuru to shita tokoro de, sore ga ureru hoshou wa nai.
examples.meaningEn: Even if we make delicious sweets, there's no guarantee they’ll sell.

examples.ja: 電話をかけるとしたところで、彼女が出るとは限らない。
examples.romaji: Denwa o kakeru to shita tokoro de, kanojo ga deru to wa kagiranai.
examples.meaningEn: Even if I call her, she might not pick up.

examples.ja: 最高の材料を使うとしたところで、美味しくなるとは限らない。
examples.romaji: Saikou no zairyou o tsukau to shita tokoro de, oishiku naru to wa kagiranai.
examples.meaningEn: Even if you use the best ingredients, it might not turn out delicious.

title: ～とすると (〜to suru to)
formation: Verb-casual + とすると / い-Adjective + とすると / な-Adjective + だとすると / Noun + だとすると
shortExplanationEn: Expresses 'if', 'when', or 'assuming that' in a speculative sense.
longExplanationEn: The ～とすると grammar point is used for hypothetical or speculative situations, often in the sense of 'if we assume...', 'when it comes to...', or 'given that...' . It sets up a condition and then states the expected or possible outcome.

examples.ja: 彼が来るとすると、もうすぐ到着するはずです。
examples.romaji: Kare ga kuru to suru to, mou sugu touchaku suru hazu desu.
examples.meaningEn: If he’s coming, he should arrive soon.

examples.ja: この仕事を終えるとすると、最低でも3日はかかるでしょう。
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto o oeru to suru to, saitei demo mikka wa kakaru deshou.
examples.meaningEn: Assuming we finish this work, it will take at least three days.

examples.ja: 戦争が起きるとすると、多くの人々が犠牲になるでしょう。
examples.romaji: Sensou ga okiru to suru to, ooku no hitobito ga gisei ni naru deshou.
examples.meaningEn: If a war were to break out, many people would become victims.

examples.ja: 彼が経営者だとすると、多くの責任を負うことになるでしょう。
examples.romaji: Kare ga keieisha da to suru to, ooku no sekinin o ou koto ni naru deshou.
examples.meaningEn: If he’s the manager, he will bear a lot of responsibility.

title: ～とすれば (～to sureba)
formation: Verb-casual + とすれば / い-Adjective + とすれば / な-Adjective + だとすれば / Noun + だとすれば
shortExplanationEn: Used to express 'if it were the case that...', 'if we assume that...'.
longExplanationEn: The ～とすれば grammar point poses a hypothetical condition and then draws a conclusion or inference. It translates as 'if we suppose/assume that...' or 'if it is the case that...'.

examples.ja: 彼が嘘つきだとすれば、その話は信じられない。
examples.romaji: Kare ga usotsuki da to sureba, sono hanashi wa shinjirarenai.
examples.meaningEn: If he’s a liar, then his story can’t be believed.

examples.ja: あなたが一緒に行くとすれば、私も行きます。
examples.romaji: Anata ga issho ni iku to sureba, watashi mo ikimasu.
examples.meaningEn: If you are going, I'll go too.

examples.ja: 彼が浮気しているとすれば、どうすればいい？
examples.romaji: Kare ga uwaki shite iru to sureba, dou sureba ii?
examples.meaningEn: If he’s cheating, what should I do?

examples.ja: その薬が効かないとすれば、他の方法を試すべきだ。
examples.romaji: Sono kusuri ga kikanai to sureba, hoka no houhou o tamesu beki da.
examples.meaningEn: If that medicine doesn’t work, we should try something else.

title: ～となったら (〜to nattara)
formation: Verb-casual + となったら / Noun + となったら
shortExplanationEn: Used to express 'if it comes to...', 'when it becomes the case that...'.
longExplanationEn: The ～となったら grammar point is used to describe a situation where, if matters progress to that stage, a certain action or outcome will follow. It’s often translated as 'when it comes to...', 'if it comes to...'.

examples.ja: 旅行に行くとなったら、荷造りをしなければいけません。
examples.romaji: Ryokou ni iku to nattara, nizukuri o shinakereba ikemasen.
examples.meaningEn: If it comes to going on a trip, we have to pack.
